meanings.definitionCn: 取消， 废除； 删去， 删除； 抵消

etymology.story: late 14c., "cross out with lines," from Anglo-French canceler, from Latin cancellare "to make resemble a lattice," which in Late Latin took on a sense "cross out something written" by marking it with crossed lines, from cancelli, plural of cancellus "lattice, grating," diminutive of cancer "crossed bars, lattice," a variant of carcer "prison" (see incarceration).

early 15c., from Old French recliner "rest, lay; bend, lean over" (13c.) and directly from Latin reclinare "to bend back, to lean back; cause to lean," from re- "back, against" (see re-) + clinare "to bend," from PIE *klei-n-, suffixed form of *klei "to lean" (see lean (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 爆炸的，爆发的；使人冲动的

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 爆发/鼓掌 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1660s, "tending to explode," from Latin explos-, past participle stem of explodere "drive out, reject" (see explosion) + -ive.
mid-15c., a back formation from preparation and in part from Middle French preparer (14c.), from Latin praeparare "make ready beforehand" (see preparation).

meanings.definitionCn: 加入十字军， 投身正义运动

etymology.story: early 14c., "to trip up, overthrow, defeat, dispossess," from Old French suplanter, sosplanter "to trip up, overthrow, drive out, usurp," or directly from Latin supplantare "trip up, overthrow," from sub "under" (see sub-) + planta "sole of the foot" (see plant (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: rise high and roll forcefully + on

meanings.definitionCn: 突然的， 意外的； 唐突的， 鲁莽的

etymology.story: 1580s, from Latin abruptus "broken off, precipitous, disconnected," past participle of abrumpere "break off," from ab- "off" (see ab-) + rumpere "break" (see rupture (n.)).

"lame," in Old English lemphalt "limping," from Proto-Germanic *haltaz (source also of Old Saxon, Old Frisian halt, Old Norse haltr, Old High German halz, Gothic halts "lame"), from PIE *keld-, from root *kel- "to strike, cut," with derivatives meaning "something broken or cut off" (source also of Russian koldyka "lame," Greek kolobos "broken, curtailed").

meanings.definitionCn: 事先影响某人； 易受感染

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + 不/分离 + 放置

etymology.story: 1640s, "to put into a certain frame of mind," perhaps a back-formation from predisposition.

meanings.definitionCn: 自发的， 自然产生的； 自然的， 无雕饰的

etymology.story: 1650s, "occurring without external stimulus," from Late Latin spontaneus "willing, of one's free will," from Latin (sua) sponte "of one's own accord, willingly;" of uncertain origin.

1620s, from Latin convectionem (nominative convectio) "the act of carrying," noun of action from past participle stem of convehere "to carry together," from com- "together" (see com-) + vehere "to carry" (see vehicle).

meanings.definitionCn: 拆除； 破坏； 驳倒， 推翻

etymology.story: 1560s, from Middle French demoliss-, present participle stem of démolir "to destroy, tear down" (late 14c.), from Latin demoliri "tear down," from de- "down" (see de-) + moliri "build, construct," from moles (genitive molis) "massive structure" (see mole (n.3)).

meanings.definitionCn: 忧郁的， 令人沮丧的； 昏暗的， 阴暗的， 阴沉的

etymology.story: 1580s, probably from gloom (n.) even though that word is not attested as early as this one.

meanings.definitionCn: 指责， 斥责， 批评

etymology.story: mid-14c., reprochen "to rebuke, reproach," from  Anglo-French repruchier, Old French reprochier "upbraid, blame, accuse, speak ill of," from reproche (see reproach (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 关税， 进口税； 海关
授权，批准；许可证；委任状，执行令
保证， 担保； 使正当

etymology.story: late 13c., "to keep safe from danger," from Old North French warantir "safeguard, protect; guarantee, pledge" (Old French garantir), from warant (see warrant (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 缘故， 理由； 好处
隐蔽； 隐瞒； 覆盖

etymology.story: early 14c., concelen, from Old French conceler "to hide, conceal, dissimulate," from Latin concelare "to hide," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + celare "to hide," from PIE root *kel- (2) "to cover, conceal" (see cell).

meanings.definitionCn: 固体的；实心的；结实的，稳固的；可靠的；连续的

etymology.story: "a tale, story," 1560s, from Middle French narrative and from narrative (adj.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 同时发生； 一致， 相符； 重合

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 落入/巧合

etymology.story: 1705, "be identical in substance or nature," but from 1640s as a verb in English in Latin form, "occupy the same space, agree in position," from Medieval Latin coincidere (used in astrology), literally "to fall upon together," from Latin com- "together" (see co-) + incidere "to fall upon" (in- "upon" + cadere "to fall;" see case (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 圆形的， 环形的； 循环的； 绕行的

morphology.mnemonic: ring环，圆 + 与…有关的/像…的

meanings.definitionCn: 需要； 要求， 命令

etymology.story: late 14c., "to ask a question, inquire," from Old French requerre "seek, procure; beg, ask, petition; demand," from Vulgar Latin *requaerere, from Latin requirere "seek to know, ask," from re-, here perhaps meaning "repeatedly" (see re-), + quaerere "ask, seek" (see query (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 工作 + 使…/具有…的 + 有…倾向的

meanings.definitionCn: 柳， 柳树； 柳木制品

etymology.story: Old English welig "willow," from Proto-Germanic *wel- (source also of Old Saxon wilgia, Middle Dutch wilghe, Dutch wilg), probably from PIE *wel- (3) "to turn, roll," with derivatives referring to curved, enclosing objects, cognate with Greek helix (see volvox).

meanings.definitionCn: 肉体的， 身体的； 物质的； 实体的

morphology.mnemonic: body体（corpor也作corp） + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., with adjectival suffix -al (1) + Latin corporeus "of the nature of a body," from corpus "body" (living or dead), from PIE *kwrpes, from root *kwrep- "body, form, appearance," probably from a verbal root meaning "to appear" (source also of Sanskrit krp- "form, body," Avestan kerefsh "form, body," Old English hrif "belly," Old High German href "womb, belly, abdomen")..

meanings.definitionCn: 急躁的， 易怒的； 易受刺激的； 过敏的

etymology.story: 1660s, "susceptible to mental irritation," from French irritable and directly from Latin irritabilis "easily excited," from irritare "excite, provoke" (see irritate).

meanings.definitionCn: 三部分的； 三方的； 三倍的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French triple or directly from Latin triplus (see triple (adj.)).
late 14c., from Late Latin legibilis "that can be read, written plainly," from Latin legere "to read" (see lecture (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 易接近的； 可得到的； 可进入的

etymology.story: "discrimination against people based on age," coined 1969 by U.S.

Old English clif "rock, promontory, steep slope," from Proto-Germanic *kliban (source also of Old Saxon clif, Old Norse klif, Middle Dutch klippe, Dutch klip, Old High German klep, German Klippe "cliff, promontory, steep rock").

meanings.definitionCn: 滚动，转动；卷，绕；摇摆，摇晃
卷； 卷形物； 名单， 花名册

etymology.story: early 13c., "rolled-up piece of parchment or paper" (especially one inscribed with an official record), from Old French rolle "document, parchment scroll, decree" (12c.), from Medieval Latin rotulus "a roll of paper" (source also of Spanish rollo, Italian rullo), from Latin rotula "small wheel," diminutive of rota "wheel" (see rotary).

meanings.definitionCn: 显著的， 值得注意的； 非凡的

etymology.story: 1590s, from un- (1) "not" + past participle of parallel (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 蒸发； 消失， 不复存在

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + vapor + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: early 15c., "draw off (humors or spirits) as vapor," from Late Latin evaporatum, past participle of evaporare "disperse in vapor" (see evaporation).

meanings.definitionCn: 亮度， 发光度； 光明

morphology.mnemonic: 光 + 具有…性质的 + ity

etymology.story: 1630s, "quality of being luminous," from French luminosité or else a native formation from luminous + -ity.

meanings.definitionCn: 授权，委任；委员会；佣金，回扣

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 发送 + 状态/行为/过程

meanings.definitionCn: 政策， 方针； 原则； 保险单

etymology.story: also résumé, 1804, "a summary," from French résumé, noun use of past participle of Middle French resumer "to sum up," from Latin resumere (see resume (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 挑衅的， 煽动性的； 惹人讨厌的； 挑逗的

morphology.mnemonic: 测试/证明 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "eliciting," from Middle French provocatif (15c.) and directly from Late Latin provocativus "calling forth," from provocat-, past participle stem of Latin provocare (see provoke).

meanings.definitionCn: 作讲话； 写姓名地址
盘旋；盘旋上升；急剧增长
螺旋形； 螺旋式的上升
艺术的； 富有艺术性的， 精美的； 有艺术天赋的

morphology.mnemonic: 技艺/技巧 + …主义者/…专家 + ic

etymology.story: 1753, from French artistique, from artiste (see artist).

meanings.definitionCn: 职业； 使命感； 圣召

etymology.story: early 15c., "spiritual calling," from Old French vocacion "call, consecration; calling, profession" (13c.) or directly from Latin vocationem (nominative vocatio), literally "a calling, a being called" from vocatus "called," past participle of vocare "to call" (see voice (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 使泄气， 使灰心； 阻止， 劝阻

etymology.story: mid-15c., discoragen, from Middle French descourager, from Old French descoragier, from des- "away" (see dis-) + corage (see courage).
late 14c., from sun (n.) + set (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 身材， 身高； 地位， 声望， 名望

etymology.story: early 14c., "natural height of a body, height," from Old French stature, estature "build, structure," from Latin statura "height, size of body, size, growth," from PIE *ste-tu-, from root *stā- "to stand," with derivatives meaning "place or thing that is standing" (see stet).

meanings.definitionCn: 顽强的， 坚持不懈的； 顽固的， 固执的

etymology.story: "having the qualities of a dog" (mostly in a negative sense), c.

1712, "form, shape" (earlier plasm), from Late Latin plasma, from Greek plasma "something molded or created," hence "image, figure; counterfeit, forgery; formed style, affectation," from plassein "to mold," originally "to spread thin," from PIE *plath-yein, from root *pele- (2) "flat, to spread" (see plane (n.1)).

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + orbit + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: mid-15c., a legal term, "deviating from rule or principle, eccentric;" from Late Latin exorbitantem (nominative exorbitans), present participle of exorbitare "deviate, go out of the track," from ex- "out of" (see ex-) + orbita "wheel track" (see orb).

meanings.definitionCn: 外科手术； 外科学； 手术室， 诊疗室
暴力的； 感情强烈的， 激情的； 强烈的， 激烈的

morphology.mnemonic: 暴力/违反 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French violent or directly from Latin violentus, related to violare (see violation).

meanings.definitionCn: 难达到的， 不可及的； 不能得到的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French inaccessible (14c.), from Late Latin inaccessibilis "unapproachable," from in- "not" (see in- (1)) + accessibilis "approachable" (see accessible).

meanings.definitionCn: 解决；决定，决心；分解；显现

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 太阳/独自

meanings.definitionCn: 设计舞蹈动作， 编舞

etymology.story: 1943, American English, back-formation from choreography, or else from French choréographier (1827).

meanings.definitionCn: 使面临， 使遭遇； 对抗

etymology.story: 1560s, "to stand in front of," from Middle French confronter (15c.), from Medieval Latin confrontare "assign limits, adjoin," from Latin com- "together" (see com-) + frontem (nominative frons) "forehead" (see front (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 部署， 调度； 利用

etymology.story: 1786 as a military word, from French déployer "unroll, unfold," from Old French desploiier "unfold," from Latin displicare "unfold, scatter," from dis- (see dis-) + plicare "to fold" see ply (v.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 繁殖， 生育； 复制； 再现

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 向前/支持 + 引导

etymology.story: 1610s, "to produce again," from re- "again" + produce (v.), probably on model of French reproduire (16c.).

meanings.definitionCn: 梗概， 提纲； 骨架， 骨骼； 框架

etymology.story: 1570s, from Modern Latin sceleton "bones, bony framework of the body," from Greek skeleton soma "dried-up body, mummy, skeleton," from neuter of skeletos "dried-up" (also, as a noun, "dried body, mummy"), from skellein "dry up, make dry, parch," from PIE root *skele- "to parch, wither" (see sclero-).

meanings.definitionCn: 聚集在一起， 挤作一团； 蜷缩， 缩成一团， 蜷缩

etymology.story: 1570s, "to heap or crowd together," probably from Low German hudern "to cover, to shelter" (of hens on chicks or nurses with children), from Middle Low German huden "to cover up," which is probably a frequentative form from Proto-Germanic *hud-, from PIE *keudh-, extended form of the root *(s)keu- "to cover, conceal" (see hide (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 句法的； 按照句法的

etymology.story: 1771, from Modern Latin syntacticus, from Greek syntaktikos "a joining together, a joining in order," from syntassein "put in order" (see syntax)..

meanings.definitionCn: 提议；出价；意图；录取通知书

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French ofre "act of offering; offer, proposition" (12c.), verbal noun from offrir (see offer (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 交点； 十字路口， 道路交叉口

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + cut切割 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1550s, "act or fact of crossing," from Middle French intersection (14c.) and directly from Latin intersectionem (nominative intersectio) "a cutting asunder, intersection," noun of action from past participle stem of intersecare "intersect, cut asunder," from inter- "between" (see inter-) + secare "to cut" (see section (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 奖学金； 学问， 学识

morphology.mnemonic: school + 与…有关的/像…的 + 身份/状态/技能

etymology.story: 1530s, "status of a scholar," from scholar + -ship.

meanings.definitionCn: 使生效， 执行， 实施
分散， 散开； 疏散； 散布

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin dispersus, past participle of dispergere "to scatter," from dis- "apart, in every direction" (see dis-) + spargere "to scatter" (see sparse).

meanings.definitionCn: 古怪的， 反复无常的； 不规则的； 不稳定的

etymology.story: late 14c., "wandering, moving," from Old French erratique "wandering, vagrant" (13c.) and directly from Latin erraticus "wandering, straying, roving," from erratum "an error, mistake, fault," past participle of errare "to wander; to err" (see err).

"burn to ashes" (transitive), 1550s, from Medieval Latin incineratus, past participle of incinerare "reduce to ashes," from in- "into" (see in- (2)) + a verb from Latin cinis (genitive cineris) "ashes," from PIE root *keni- "dust, ashes" (source also of Greek konis "dust").

early 14c., "illusory appearance," from Old French fantaisie, phantasie "vision, imagination" (14c.), from Latin phantasia, from Greek phantasia "power of imagination; appearance, image, perception," from phantazesthai "picture to oneself," from phantos "visible," from phainesthai "appear," in late Greek "to imagine, have visions," related to phaos, phos "light," phainein "to show, to bring to light" (see phantasm).

meanings.definitionCn: 毛状的； 毛细血管的

etymology.story: 1650s, "of or pertaining to the hair," from Latin capillaris "of hair," from capillus "hair" (of the head); perhaps related to caput "head" (but de Vaan finds this "difficult on the formal side" and "far from compelling, since capillus is a diminutive, and would mean 'little head', which hardly amounts to 'hair'").

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + 形状/形态

etymology.story: 1610s, "gnaw or eat away" (transitive), a back-formation from erosion, or else from French éroder, from Latin erodere "to gnaw away, consume," from assimilated form of ex- "away" (see ex-) + rodere "gnaw" (see rodent).

meanings.definitionCn: 提供消息的， 给予知识的； 见闻广博的

etymology.story: 1650s, "instructive, didactic," from Medieval Latin informativus, from Latin informatus, past participle of informare "to train, instruct, educate" (see inform).

meanings.definitionCn: 使感到灰心， 挫败； 阻止

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin frustratus, past participle of frustrari "to deceive, disappoint, make vain," from frustra (adv.) "in vain, in error," related to fraus "injury, harm" (see fraud).

meanings.definitionCn: 稀疏的， 稀少的； 贫乏的

etymology.story: 1727, from Latin sparsus "scattered," past participle of spargere "to scatter, spread," from PIE root *(s)preg- (2) "to jerk, scatter" (source also of Sanskrit parjanya- "rain, rain god," Avestan fra-sparega "branch, twig," literally "that which is jerked off a tree," Old Norse freknur "freckles," Swedish dialectal sprygg "brisk, active," Lithuanian sprogti "shoot, bud," Old Irish arg "a drop").

meanings.definitionCn: 漂亮的； 令人愉快的； 华丽的， 灿烂的
务实的， 注重实效的， 实用的

etymology.story: 1610s, "meddlesome, impertinently busy," short for earlier pragmatical, or else from Middle French pragmatique (15c.), from Latin pragmaticus "skilled in business or law," from Greek pragmatikos "fit for business, active, business-like; systematic," from pragma (genitive pragmatos) "a deed, act; that which has been done; a thing, matter, affair," especially an important one; also a euphemism for something bad or disgraceful; in plural, "circumstances, affairs" (public or private), often in a bad sense, "trouble," literally "a thing done," from stem of prassein/prattein "to do, act, perform" (see practical).

meanings.definitionCn: 腐蚀， 侵蚀； 削弱， 减少

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + 咬，啮 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1540s, from Middle French erosion (16c.), from Latin erosionem (nominative erosio) "a gnawing away," noun of action from past participle stem of erodere "gnaw away" (see erosion).
"inflammable colorless and odorless gas, marsh gas," 1867, coined from chemical suffix -ane + syllable abstracted from methyl..

1650s, "of or relating to building or construction," from Late Latin tectonicus, from Greek tektonikos "pertaining to building," from tekton (genitive tektonos) "builder, carpenter, woodworker; master in any art (sculpture, metal-work, writing)," from PIE root *teks- "to make" (see texture (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 迷信的， 受迷信思想支配的

etymology.story: late 14c., "involving faith in supernatural powers or magic; characteristic of pagan religion or false religion," from Anglo-French supersticius, Old French supersticios, or directly from Latin superstitiosus "prophetic; full of dread of the supernatural," from superstitio "prophecy, soothsaying, excessive fear of the gods" (see superstition)..

meanings.definitionCn: 上升， 升高； 攀登

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin ascendere "to climb up, mount, ascend," figuratively "to rise, reach," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + scandere "to climb" (see scan (v.)).
1660s, from French élasticité, or else from elastic + -ity..

meanings.definitionCn: 积极主动的； 先发制人的

etymology.story: also pro-active, of persons or policies, as an opposition to reactive, 1921, from pro- + active.

meanings.definitionCn: 原始的；土著的，土生土长的

morphology.mnemonic: 远离/脱离 + 起源/开始 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1660s, "first, earliest," especially in reference to inhabitants of lands colonized by Europeans, from aborigines (see aborigine) + -al (1); specific Australian sense is from 1820.

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 分配/免除 + able

etymology.story: 1530s, "not subject to dispensation," from Medieval Latin *indispensabilis, from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + dispensabilis, from Latin dispensare "disburse, administer, distribute (by weight)" (see dispense).

meanings.definitionCn: 透镜； 镜头； 镜片

etymology.story: 1690s, "glass to regulate light rays," from Latin lens (genitive lentis) "a lentil," on analogy of the double-convex shape.

meanings.definitionCn: 表达， 体现； 含有

etymology.story: 1540s, in reference to a soul or spirit invested with a physical form; from 1660s of principles, ideas, etc.; from em- (1) "in" + body (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 可证实的， 可核实的

etymology.story: 1590s, from verify + -able.

meanings.definitionCn: 可理解的， 能懂的， 能充分理解的

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 抓住/理解 + able

etymology.story: 1520s, "able to be contained," from Latin comprehensibilis, from comprehensus, past participle of comphrehendere (see comprehend).

meanings.definitionCn: 发动的； 导致运动的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French motif "moving" or directly from Medieval Latin motivus "moving, impelling," from past participle stem of movere "to move" (see move (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 椭圆的； 隐晦的； 省略的， 简略的

etymology.story: 1650s, "elliptic in shape;" see elliptic + -al (1).

1620s, "set free from control," from Latin emancipatus, past participle of emancipare "put (a son) out of paternal authority, declare (someone) free, give up one's authority over," in Roman law, the freeing of a son or wife from the legal authority (patria potestas) of the pater familias, to make his or her own way in the world; from assimilated form of ex- "out, away" (see ex-) + mancipare "deliver, transfer or sell," from mancipum "ownership," from manus "hand" (see manual (adj.)) + capere "take" (see capable).

meanings.definitionCn: 爱情的； 浪漫的， 富有情调的； 多情的； 不切实际的， 幻想的； [R-]浪漫主义的

etymology.story: 1863, "to stimulate toward action," from motive + -ate (2); perhaps modeled on French motiver or German motivieren.

meanings.definitionCn: 破坏； 宠坏， 溺爱； 变质

etymology.story: late 14c., of animals, "to graze;" early 15c., of humans, "to lead to pasture, to feed by putting in a pasture," from Old French pasturer (12c., Modern French pâturer, from pasture (see pasture (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 驾驶， 领航； 试行， 试用； 使通过， 引导
有助于治疗的，有疗效的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French curatif (15c.) "curative, healing," from Latin curat-, past participle stem of curare "to cure" (see cure (v.)).
early 15c., from Middle French cartilage (16c.) and directly from Latin cartilaginem (nominative cartilago) "cartilage, gristle," possibly related to Latin crates "wickerwork.".

meanings.definitionCn: 伪造的； 不诚实的； 讽刺的， 挖苦的， 含沙射影的

etymology.story: 1859, thieves' slang, "counterfeit, sham, bad, spurious," of unknown origin.
1640s, "involving many parts," from French multiple (14c.), from Late Latin multiplus "manifold," from Latin multi- "many, much" (see multi-) + -plus "-fold" (see -plus).

meanings.definitionCn: 暴露， 显露； 揭露， 揭穿； 使接触， 使面临

etymology.story: also exposé, "display of discreditable information," 1803, initially as a French word; noun use of past participle of French exposer "lay open" (see expose (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 磁铁， 磁体； 有吸引力的人
多才多艺的； 多用途的

root.meaning: to rest as at a center

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + to rest as at a center + 使…/具有…的

meanings.definitionCn: 对， 对抗； 与…相对， 与…相比

etymology.story: mid-15c., in legal case names, denoting action of one party against another, from Latin versus "turned toward or against," from past participle of vertere (frequentative versare) "to turn, turn back, be turned, convert, transform, translate, be changed," from PIE *wert- "to turn, wind," from root *wer- (3) "to turn, bend" (source also of Old English -weard "toward," originally "turned toward," weorthan "to befall," wyrd "fate, destiny," literally "what befalls one;" Sanskrit vartate "turns round, rolls;" Avestan varet- "to turn;" Old Church Slavonic vrŭteti "to turn, roll," Russian vreteno "spindle, distaff;" Lithuanian ver čiu "to turn;" Greek rhatane "stirrer, ladle;" German werden, Old English weorðan "to become" (for sense, compare turn into); Welsh gwerthyd "spindle, distaff;" Old Irish frith "against")..
1690s, from obsolete penman "copyist, clerk, scrivener" + -ship..

meanings.definitionCn: 苔原， 冻原， 冻土地带

etymology.story: an Arctic steppe, 1841, from Russian tundra, from Lappish (Finno-Ugric) tundar, said to mean "elevated wasteland, high-topped hill," or "a marshy plain.".

meanings.definitionCn: 爆炸； 爆发； 激增

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 爆发/鼓掌 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1620s, "action of driving out with violence and noise," from French explosion, from Latin explosionem (nominative explosio) "a driving off by clapping," noun of action from past participle stem of explodere "drive out by clapping" (see explode for origin and sense evolution).
1610s, from Medieval Latin populatus, past participle of populare "inhabit, to people," from Latin populus "inhabitants, people, nation" (see people (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 承认； 准许…进入， 接纳

etymology.story: late 14c., "let in," from Latin admittere "to allow to enter, let in, let come, give access," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + mittere "let go, send" (see mission).

meanings.definitionCn: 翻译； 译文， 译本

etymology.story: mid-14c., "removal of a saint's body or relics to a new place," also "rendering of a text from one language to another," from Old French translacion "translation" of text, also of the bones of a saint, etc.
1837, from French personnel (a contrastive term to matériel), noun use of personnel (adj.) "personal," from Old French personel (see personal)..

meanings.definitionCn: 重点文章；溅上的液体；斑点
喷泉； 丰富来源， 源泉

etymology.story: early 15c., "spring of water that collects in a pool," from Old French fontaine "natural spring" (12c.), from Medieval Latin fontana "fountain, a spring" (source of Spanish and Italian fontana), from post-classical noun use of fem.

1530s, from Middle French porcelaine and directly from Italian porcellana "porcelain" (13c.), literally "cowrie shell," the chinaware so called from resemblance of its lustrous transparency to the shiny surface of the shells.

meanings.definitionCn: 兼职的； 部分时间的

etymology.story: also parttime, 1891, from part (n.) + time (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 寒冷的； 冷淡的， 不友好的

morphology.mnemonic: hang out + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1560s, "causing a sensation of cold," from chill (n.) + -y (2).

meanings.definitionCn: [昆] 鞭毛；鞭子；鞭状匍匐枝

etymology.story: "long, lash-like appendage," 1837, from Latin flagellum "whip, scourge," also figurative, diminutive of flagrum "a whip," from PIE root *bhlag- "to strike" (source also of Latin flagitium "shameful act, passionate deed, disgraceful thing," flagitare "to demand importunately;" Old Norse blakra "to flutter with the wings," blekkja "to impose upon;" Lithuanian blaškau "to and fro")..

meanings.definitionCn: 含义； 暗示， 暗指； 卷入， 牵连

morphology.mnemonic: in + 折叠/交织 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "action of entangling," from Latin implicationem (nominative implicatio) "an interweaving, an entanglement," noun of state from past participle stem of implicare "involve, entangle; embrace; connect closely, associate," from assimilated form of in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + plicare "to fold" (see ply (v.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 凶猛的， 残忍的； 狂热的， 强烈的； 激烈的， 猛烈的

etymology.story: mid-13c., "proud, noble, bold, haughty," from Old French fers, fiers, nominative form of fer, fier "strong, overwhelming, violent, fierce, wild; proud, mighty, great, impressive" (Modern French fier "proud, haughty"), from Latin ferus "wild, untamed, uncultivated; waste, desert;" figuratively "wild, uncultivated, savage, cruel," from PIE root *ghwer- "wild; wild animal" (source also of Greek ther, Old Church Slavonic zveri, Lithuanian zveris "wild beast").

meanings.definitionCn: 预约， 预订； 保留意见， 疑惑； 居留地

root.meaning: self-imposed restraint on freedom of words or actions; habit of keeping back the feelings

morphology.mnemonic: self-imposed restraint on freedom of words or actions; habit of keeping back the feelings + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "act of reserving," from Old French reservation (14c.) and directly from Late Latin reservationem (nominative reservatio), noun of action from past participle stem of Latin reservare (see reserve (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 妨碍， 阻碍， 阻止

morphology.mnemonic: 心 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., "of the heart," from Middle French cordial, from Medieval Latin cordialis "of or for the heart," from Latin cor (genitive cordis) "heart," from PIE root *kerd- (1) "heart" (see heart (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 有气味的， 难闻的； 香的， 芬芳的

etymology.story: "fragrant," early 15c., from Medieval Latin odorosus, from Latin odorus "having a smell," from odor (see odor)..

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 工作 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 15c., from Middle French coopération, or directly from Late Latin cooperationem (nominative cooperatio) "a working together," noun of action from past participle stem of cooperari "to work together," from com- "with" (see com-) + operari "to work" (see operation)..
late 14c., "something formed in the mind, thought, notion," from conceiven (see conceive) based on analogy of deceit and receipt.

meanings.definitionCn: 调整， 调节； 调制

etymology.story: 1610s, in music, back-formation from modulation, or else from Latin modulatus, past participle of modulari.
1670s, past participle adjective; see mottle (v.)..

morphology.mnemonic: 形态 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1824 in biology (from German Morphologie, 1817); 1869 in philology; from morpho- + -logy.

meanings.definitionCn: 远离的， 遥远的； 疏远的， 冷淡的； 久远的； 不同的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French distant (14c.), from Latin distantem (nominative distans), present participle of distare "to stand apart, be remote" (see distance (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 共振， 共鸣； 回声， 反响

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 声音 + ance

etymology.story: mid-15c., in acoustics, "prolongation of sound by reverberation;" 1660s, "act of resonating;" from Middle French resonance (15c.), from Latin resonantia "echo," from resonare "to sound again" (see resound).

meanings.definitionCn: 建议， 提议； 提名， 推荐； 打算， 计划； 求婚

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French proposer "propose, advance, suggest" (12c.), from pro- "forth" (see pro-) + poser "put, place" (see pose (v.1)).
"instrument for measuring the motions of an earthquake," 1858, from seismo- + -graph.

meanings.definitionCn: 改革， 改造， 革新； 改正， 改过自新

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 形状/形态

meanings.definitionCn: 有角的， 尖角的； 消瘦的， 瘦骨嶙峋的

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: 1590s, from Latin angularis "having corners or angles," from angulus (see angle (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 讲坛， 讲台； 指挥台

etymology.story: 1743, "raised platform around an ancient arena," also "projecting base of a pedestal," from Latin podium "raised platform," from Greek podion "foot of a vase," diminutive of pous (genitive podos) "foot," from PIE root *ped- (1) "a foot" (see foot (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 二进制的； 二元的； 由两部分组成的

etymology.story: "dual," mid-15c., from Late Latin binarius "consisting of two," from bini "twofold, two apiece, two-by-two" (used especially of matched things), from bis "double" (see bis-).

meanings.definitionCn: 继而发生， 接着发生
疯狂的， 不理智的； 狂热的， 热衷的； 精神失常的

etymology.story: 1570s, "diseased, sickly," from craze + -y (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 用显微镜可见的； 极小的

etymology.story: 1732, "like a microscope;" see microscope + -ic.
1590s (agent noun homager is from c.

1929, coined in English by Alexander Fleming (1881-1955), who first recognized its antibiotic properties, from Modern Latin Penicillium notatum (1867), the name of the mould from which it was first obtained, from Latin penicillus "paintbrush" (see pencil (n.)), in reference to the shape of the mould cells..

meanings.definitionCn: 憔悴； 难过， 悲伤

etymology.story: Old English pinian "to torture, torment, afflict, cause to suffer," from *pine "pain, torture, punishment," possibly ultimately from Latin poena "punishment, penalty," from Greek poine (see penal).

meanings.definitionCn: 启发， 教化， 启迪； [the E-]启蒙运动

morphology.mnemonic: 使成为/放入 + something used for igniting + 使变成/变得 + 结果/过程/状态

etymology.story: 1660s, "action of enlightening," from enlighten + -ment.

meanings.definitionCn: 反话， 讽刺， 嘲弄； 具有讽刺意味的事

morphology.mnemonic: shackle with irons + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: "of or resembling iron," late 14c., from iron (n.) + -y (2)..

meanings.definitionCn: 强制的，法定的，义务的； 受托人； 代理人

morphology.mnemonic: 命令/委托 + 使…/具有…的 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1570s, "of the nature of a mandate," from Late Latin mandatorius "pertaining to a mandator," from Latin mandatus, past participle of mandare (see mandate (n.)).

mid-15c., catyrpel, probably altered (by association with Middle English piller "plunderer;" see pillage) from Old North French caterpilose "caterpillar" (Old French chatepelose), literally "shaggy cat" (probably in reference to the "wooly-bear" variety), from Late Latin catta pilosa, from catta "cat" (see cat (n.)) + pilosus "hairy, shaggy, covered with hair," from pilus "hair" (see pile (n.3)).
1969, from hyper- "over, above" + text (n.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 基础， 根基； 基岩

etymology.story: also bed-rock, 1850, from bed (n.) + rock (n.).

"a number of symptoms occurring together," 1540s, from medical Latin, from Greek syndrome "concurrence of symptoms, concourse of people," from syndromos "place where several roads meet," literally "a running together," from syn- "with" (see syn-) + dromos "a running, course" (see dromedary).
"cheap paper from pulp, used to print newspapers," 1909, from news (n.) + print..

"pull with a rope," Old English togian "to drag, pull," from Proto-Germanic *tugojanan (source also of Old English teon "to draw," Old Frisian togia "to pull about," Old Norse toga, Old High German zogon, German ziehen "to draw, pull, drag"), from PIE root *deuk- "to pull, draw" (source also of Latin ducere "to lead;" see duke (n.)).
1560s, "relating to music," from Latin harmonicus, from Greek harmonikos "harmonic, musical, skilled in music," from harmonia (see harmony).

meanings.definitionCn: 拖， 拉， 拖运； 一次获得的数量； 旅行的距离
精力充沛的， 积极的

etymology.story: 1650s, "powerful in operation," from Greek energetikos "active," from energein "to work, be in action, act upon" (see energy).

meanings.definitionCn: 第一的， 首要的； 最著名的

etymology.story: 1711 in the political sense, a shortening of premier minister (1680s); see premier (adj.).

meanings.definitionCn: 过敏的， 变态反应的； 对…讨厌的

etymology.story: 1911, from allergy + -ic; perhaps modeled on French allergique (1906).
1886, agent noun from oceanography..

meanings.definitionCn: 秘闻； 轶事， 趣闻

etymology.story: 1670s, "secret or private stories," from French anecdote (17c.) or directly from Greek anekdota "things unpublished," neuter plural of anekdotos, from an- "not" (see an-) + ekdotos "published," from ek- "out" + didonai "to give" (see date (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 爱发号施令的， 爱指手画脚的

etymology.story: 1560s, "zealous, eager to serve," from Latin officiosus "full of courtesy, dutiful, obliging," from officium "duty, service" (see office).

meanings.definitionCn: 渗入， 渗透； 悄悄进入， 潜入

etymology.story: 1758, of fluids, from in- (2) "in" + filtrate (v.).
early 14c., "a Franciscan," from Latin Fratres Minores "lesser brethren," name chosen by St.

meanings.definitionCn: 不友好的， 敌意的； 敌方的

etymology.story: late 15c., from Middle French hostile "of or belonging to an enemy" (15c.) or directly from Latin hostilis "of an enemy, belonging to or characteristic of the enemy; inimical," from hostis "enemy" (see guest (n.)).

late 14c., "pertaining to heaven," from Old French celestial "celestial, heavenly, sky-blue," from Latin caelestis "heavenly, pertaining to the sky," from caelum "heaven, sky; abode of the gods; climate," which is of uncertain origin; perhaps from PIE *kaid-slo-, perhaps from a root also found in Germanic and Baltic meaning "bright, clear" (compare Lithuanian skaidrus "shining, clear;" Old English hador, German heiter "clear, shining, cloudless," Old Norse heið "clear sky").

meanings.definitionCn: 伤痕， 擦痕， 青肿

morphology.mnemonic: 相同/识别 + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1610s as a term in logic; general sense of "being the same or very similar" is from 1630s, from Medieval Latin identicus "the same," from Late Latin identitas "identity, sameness," ultimately from comb.

meanings.definitionCn: 未受协助的， 独立的

etymology.story: 1660s, from un- (1) "not" + past participle of aid (v.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 有雄心的， 有野心的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin ambitiosus "going around to canvass for office," from ambitio (see ambition).

meanings.definitionCn: 学术的；学校的，学院的；纯理论的

etymology.story: 1580s, "relating to an academy," also "collegiate, scholarly," from Latin academicus "of the Academy," from academia (see academy).

meanings.definitionCn: 举起，提升；直立；增加；募集；饲养，养育；引起；提出
行为不良； 过失， 失职

etymology.story: 1630s, from Latin delinquentia "fault, crime, delinquency," from delinquentem (see delinquent)..

meanings.definitionCn: 用管道输送；用线路系统传输
管子， 管道； 烟斗

etymology.story: Old English pipian "to play on a pipe," from Latin pipare "to peep, chirp"  (see pipe (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 柔软的； 温顺的； 易受影响的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French ploiant "bending, supple; compliant, fickle," as a noun, "turncoat" (13c.), present participle of ploier "to bend" (see ply (n.)).
1660s, from French révérer, from Latin revereri "revere, fear" (see reverence (n.), which also was the earlier form of the verb).

meanings.definitionCn: 典型的， 有代表性的

morphology.mnemonic: to place before + 有…倾向的

meanings.definitionCn: 偷偷摸摸的， 鬼鬼祟祟的

etymology.story: 1833, from sneak (v.) + -y (2).

morphology.mnemonic: to grow long, lengthen, + 状态/品质

etymology.story: late 14c., "length," from Latin longitudo "length, duration," from longus (see long (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 懒散的； 不严格的， 马虎的

etymology.story: 1951 as an abbreviation of lacrosse..

meanings.definitionCn: 比较， 对比； 比拟， 比喻

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French comparaison (12c.), from Latin comparationem (nominative comparatio), noun of action from past participle stem of comparare "make equal with, liken, bring together for a contest," literally "to couple together, to form in pairs," from com- "with" (see com-) + parare "prepare" (see pare)..

meanings.definitionCn: 产； 放置； 铺； 筹划， 设置； 提出， 提交

etymology.story: "uneducated, non-professional; non-clerical," early 14c., from Old French lai "secular, not of the clergy" (12c., Modern French laïque), from Late Latin laicus, from Greek laikos "of the people," from laos "(the common) folk, the people, the crowd; the military; a tribe," in the New Testament especially "the Jewish people," also "the laity," a word of unknown origin.

1590s, "compatriot," from Middle French patriote (15c.) and directly from Late Latin patriota "fellow-countryman" (6c.), from Greek patriotes "fellow countryman," from patrios "of one's fathers," patris "fatherland," from pater (genitive patros) "father" (see father (n.)); with -otes, suffix expressing state or condition.

meanings.definitionCn: 恢复； 归还， 归属

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + dwell居住 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-15c., "action or state of living together (especially as husband and wife)," from Middle French cohabitation (Old French cohabitacion "cohabitation, sexual intercourse"), from Late Latin cohabitationem (nominative cohabitatio), noun of action from past participle stem of cohabitare "to dwell together," from co- "with, together" (see co-) + habitare "to live, inhabit, dwell," frequentative of habere "to have, to hold, possess" (see habit (n.))..

"plant-eating animal," 1851, from Modern Latin Herbivora (1830) or French herbivore (1748), from neuter plural of Latin herbivorus, from herba "a plant" (see herb) + vorare "devour, swallow" (see voracity)..

meanings.definitionCn: 接替的， 继承的； 接连的， 连续的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Medieval Latin successivus "successive," from success-, stem of Latin succedere "to come after" (see succeed).

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 行为/过程/结果

meanings.definitionCn: 典礼， 仪式； 礼节

etymology.story: late 14c., cerymonye, from Old French ceremonie and directly from Medieval Latin ceremonia, from Latin caerimonia "holiness, sacredness; awe; reverent rite, sacred ceremony," an obscure word, possibly of Etruscan origin, or a reference to the ancient rites performed by the Etruscan pontiffs at Caere, near Rome.

meanings.definitionCn: 改善， 改进， 增进； 好转， 进步

etymology.story: late 15c., "to use to one's profit, to increase (income)," from Anglo-French emprouwer "to turn to profit" (late 13c.), from Old French en-, a causative prefix or from em-, + prou "profit," from Latin prode "advantageous" (see proud (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 田园诗般的， 田园风光的， 牧歌的

etymology.story: "full of natural, simple charm," 1831, literally "suitable for an idyll" from French idyllique or else from idyll + -ic.
1520s, from Latin exterior "outward, outer, exterior," comparative of exterus "on the outside, outward, outer, of another country, foreign," itself a comparative of ex "out of" (see ex-).
late 14c., Old French *captivite or directly from Latin captivitatem (nominative captivitas), from captivus (see captive (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 征收额； 税款； 征兵

etymology.story: "an act of levying," early 15c., from Anglo-French leve, Old French levée "a raising, lifting; levying," noun use of fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 有效的， 生效的； 显著的； 实际的， 事实上的

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + 做/制造 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: late 14c., "serving to effect the intended purpose," from Old French effectif, from Latin effectivus "productive, effective," from effect-, stem of efficere (see effect (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 肌肉； 体力， 力量， 实力； 影响力
慷慨的， 大方的； 大量的， 丰富的； 宽厚的， 宽宏大量的

etymology.story: 1580s, "of noble birth," from Middle French généreux (14c.), from Latin generosus "of noble birth," figuratively "magnanimous, generous," from genus (genitive generis) "race, stock" (see genus).

late 14c., "abnormally copious flow," from Old French flus "a flowing, a rolling; a bleeding" (Modern French flux), or directly from Latin fluxus (adj.) "flowing, loose, slack," past participle of fluere "to flow" (see fluent).

meanings.definitionCn: 探针； 探测器； 探索， 调查
新月； 新月形， 月牙形

etymology.story: late 14c., "crescent-shaped ornament," from Anglo-French cressaunt, from Old French creissant "crescent of the moon" (12c., Modern French croissant), from Latin crescentum (nominative crescens), present participle of crescere "come forth, spring up, grow, thrive, swell, increase in numbers or strength," from PIE root *ker- (3) "to grow" (source also of Latin Ceres, goddess of agriculture, creare "to bring forth, create, produce;" Greek kouros "boy," kore "girl;" Armenian serem "bring forth," serim "be born").

meanings.definitionCn: 充气， 膨胀； 鼓吹， 吹捧； 涨价

etymology.story: early 15c., "cause to swell," from Latin inflatus (source also of Italian enfiare, Spanish inflar, French enfler), past participle of inflare "to blow into, inflate" (see inflation).

meanings.definitionCn: 稳定的，安定的；牢固的；沉稳的

etymology.story: 1580s, "having quantity," from Medieval Latin quantitativus, from stem of Latin quantitas (see quantity).

meanings.definitionCn: 利用…产生动力；给上挽具

etymology.story: "to put a harness on a draught animal," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 煽动， 挑起， 激起

etymology.story: early 15c., "apply hot liquids," from Old French fomenter "apply hot compress (to a wound)"  (13c.), from Late Latin fomentare, from Latin fomentum "warm application, poultice," contraction of *fovimentum, from fovere "to warm; cherish, encourage" (see fever).

meanings.definitionCn: 回答， 答复； 作出反应； 响应
事情， 事件； 摩擦， 冲突

morphology.mnemonic: 落于/发生 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story:
late 15c., "likely to happen," from Latin incidentem (nominative incidens), present participle of 
incidere "to happen, befall" (see incident (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 成群， 成串； 丛生； 群集
猎物； 受害者， 牺牲品

etymology.story: "a nerve cell with appendages," 1891, from German Neuron, from Greek neuron (see neuro-).
"a vertebrate animal," 1826, from Latin vertebratus (Pliny), from vertebra "joint or articulation of the body, joint of the spine" (see vertebra).

suffix.meaning: thing or animal spoken about before.

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 脚/儿童 + thing or animal spoken about before. + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "military campaign; the act of rapidly setting forth," from Old French expedicion "an expediting, implementation; expedition, mission" (13c.) and directly from Latin expeditionem (nominative expeditio) "an enterprise against an enemy, a military campaign," noun of action from past participle stem of expedire "make ready, prepare" (see expedite).

meanings.definitionCn: 音乐会， 演奏会； 一致

etymology.story: 1660s, "agreement, accord, harmony," from French concert (16c.), from Italian concerto "concert, harmony," from concertare "bring into agreement," in Latin "to contend, contest, dispute," from com- "with" (see com-) + certare "to contend, strive," frequentative of certus, variant past participle of cernere "separate, decide" (see crisis).

meanings.definitionCn: 理解， 领会； 包括， 由…组成

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 抓住/理解

etymology.story: mid-14c., "to understand," from Latin comprehendere "to take together, to unite; include; seize" (of catching fire or the arrest of criminals); also "to comprehend, perceive" (to seize or take in the mind), from com- "completely" (see com-) + prehendere "to catch hold of, seize" (see prehensile).

meanings.definitionCn: 供给， 供应； 满足， 弥补

etymology.story: late 14c., "to help, support, maintain," also "fill up, make up for," from Old French soupplier "fill up, make full" (Modern French suppléer) and directly from Latin supplere "fill up, make full, complete," from sub "up from below" (see sub-) + plere "to fill" (see pleio-).

meanings.definitionCn: 涉及，关系到；使关心，使担忧
关心， 挂念； 关系； 有关的事， 负责的事； 公司， 企业
循环； 流通； 传播， 流传
孵化； 培育； 潜伏

etymology.story: 1640s (transitive), "to brood upon, watch jealously" (figurative); 1721 in literal sense "to sit on (eggs) to hatch them," from Latin incubatus, past participle of incubare "to lie in or upon," also in the figurative sense "brood" (see incubation).

meanings.definitionCn: 矫正， 纠正； 整顿

etymology.story: 1867, coined to serve as opposite of synonym, from Greek anti- "equal to, instead of, opposite" (see anti-) + -onym "name" (see name (n.)).

late 14c., from Old French refrain "chorus" (13c.), alteration of refrait, noun use of past participle of refraindre "repeat," also "break off," from Vulgar Latin *refrangere "break off," alteration of Latin refringere "break up, break open" (see refraction) by influence of frangere "to break." Influenced in French by cognate Provençal refranhar "singing of birds, refrain." The notion is of something that causes a song to "break off" then resume.

meanings.definitionCn: 协议， 条约， 公约

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French pacte "agreement, treaty, compact" (14c.), from Latin pactum "agreement, contract, covenant," noun use of neuter past participle of pacisci "to covenant, to agree, make a treaty," from PIE root *pag- "fix, join together, unite, make firm" (source also of Sanskrit pasa- "cord, rope," Avestan pas- "to fetter," Greek pegnynai "to fix, make firm, fast or solid," Latin pangere "to fix, to fasten," Slavonic paž "wooden partition," Old English fegan "to join," fon "to catch seize")..

morphology.mnemonic: notice, attention, reputation + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1560s, "explanation of a term," from Middle French notation and directly from Latin notationem (nominative notatio) "a marking, notation, designation; etymology; shorthand; explanation," noun of action from past participle stem of notare "to note" (see note (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 有责任的， 有义务的； 易于…的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "bound or obliged by law," probably from Anglo-French *liable, from Old French lier "to bind, tie up, fasten, tether; bind by obligation," from Latin ligare "to bind, to tie" (see ligament).

meanings.definitionCn: 陈述， 说明， 声明； 规定

etymology.story: 1650s, "freight loaded on a ship," from Spanish cargo "burden," from cargar "to load, impose taxes," from Late Latin carricare "to load on a cart" (see charge (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 压， 按， 挤； 压榨， 压迫； 催促， 逼迫
报刊；新闻界，新闻工作者；报道，评论；出版社；印刷厂；压，按，挤
杂乱的一堆， 混乱的一团
有关环境的， 自然环境的， 生态环境的； 环境的

morphology.mnemonic: environ + 结果/过程/状态 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1887, "environing, surrounding," from environment + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 挑剔的， 难以取悦的

morphology.mnemonic: to eat with small bites + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1867, from pick (v.) + -y (2).
1835, from Crustacea the class name.

1560s, back-formation from exclamation or else from Middle French exclamer (16c.), from Latin exclamare "cry out loud, call out," from ex- "out," or else here as an intensive prefix (see ex-) + clamare "cry, shout, call" (see claim (v.)).

late 14c., "placed on the other side of (something)," from Old French oposite "opposite, contrary" (13c.), from Latin oppositus "standing against, opposed, opposite," past participle of opponere "set against" (see opponent).

meanings.definitionCn: 抑制， 克制； 限制； 约束力； 管制措施

etymology.story: "action of restraining; means of restraint," early 15c., from Old French restreinte, noun use of fem.
1520s, originally a second crop of grass grown after the first had been harvested, from after + -math, a dialectal word, from Old English mæð "a mowing, cutting of grass" (see math (n.2)).

meanings.definitionCn: 污染物质， 有害物质

morphology.mnemonic: make physically foul + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1888, from pollute + -ant.

meanings.definitionCn: 未开发的， 未使用的

etymology.story: 1775, from un- (1) "not" + past participle of tap (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 流出物， 废水， 污水

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin effluentem "flowing out" (see effluence).

meanings.definitionCn: 未驯服的， 难驾驭的

etymology.story: mid-14c., from un- (1) "not" + past participle of tame (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 陈规；固定形式，老套；刻板印象
登记， 注册； 招收
彻底的，广泛的；〈贬〉笼统的，一概而论的
清扫， 扫除； 垃圾
热心的， 热情洋溢的

etymology.story: early 14c., of alcoholic distillates, brandy (ardent spirits), etc., from Old French ardant (13c.) "burning, hot; zealous," from Latin ardentem (nominative ardens) "glowing, fiery, hot, ablaze," also used figuratively of passions, present participle of ardere "to burn," from PIE root *as- "to burn, glow" (source also of Old English æsce "ashes;" see ash (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 黏着的； 使凝结的， 使内聚的
电池； 排炮； 一系列， 一批

etymology.story: 1530s, "action of battering," from Middle French batterie, from Old French baterie (12c.) "beating, thrashing, assault," from batre "beat," from Latin battuere "beat" (see batter (v.)).

Old English clæg "stiff, sticky earth; clay," from Proto-Germanic *klaijaz (source also of Old High German kliwa "bran," German Kleie, Old Frisian klai "clay," Old Saxon klei, Middle Dutch clei, Danish klæg "clay;" also Old English clæman, Old Norse kleima, Old High German kleiman "to cover with clay"), from PIE root *glei- "clay" (source also of Greek gloios "sticky matter;" Latin gluten "glue;" Old Church Slavonic glina "clay," glenu "slime, mucus;" Old Irish glenim "I cleave, adhere").

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + rever + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: "deficient in veneration or respect," mid-15c., from Old French irreverent or directly from Latin irreverentem "disrespectful, irreverent" (see irreverence).

meanings.definitionCn: 促进； 提升； 宣传， 推销

etymology.story: late 14c., "to advance (someone) to a higher grade or office," from Old French promoter and directly from Latin promotus, past participle of promovere "move forward, advance; cause to advance, push onward; bring to light, reveal," from pro- "forward" (see pro-) + movere "to move" (see move (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 与…离婚； 使分离， 使脱离

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French divorcer, from divorce (see divorce (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 分配， 分派， 把…拨给

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 地方 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1630s, from verbal used of adjective allocate (mid-15c.

meanings.definitionCn: 连接； 依附于； 系， 贴， 连接； 使依恋， 使喜爱 ； 认为有
墙角石， 奠基石； 基础

etymology.story: late 13c., from corner (n.) + stone (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 测验； 盘问， 查问， 询问

etymology.story: 1847, "to question," quies, perhaps from Latin qui es? "who are you?," first question in oral exams in Latin in old-time grammar schools.

meanings.definitionCn: 爱好， 意愿； 趋向， 趋势； 倾斜度

morphology.mnemonic: in + 倾斜 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: "condition of being mentally disposed" (to do something), late 14c., from Middle French inclination (14c.) and directly from Latin inclinationem (nominative inclinatio) "a leaning, bending," figuratively "tendency, bias, favor," noun of action from past participle stem of inclinare "to bend, turn; cause to lean" (see incline (v.)).

1530s, back-formation from participation, or else from Latin participatus, past participle of participare "to share, share in, participate in; to impart," from particeps "partaking, sharing," from parti, past participle of partir "to divide" (see part (n.)) + -cip-, weak form of stem of capere "to take" (see capable).

meanings.definitionCn: 成分， 要素； 原料

etymology.story: in early use also engredient, early 15c., "something forming part of a mixture," from Latin ingredientem (nominative ingrediens) "that which enters into" (a compound, recipe, etc.), present participle of ingredi "go in, enter," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + gradi "to step, go" (see grade (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 太阳/独自 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1510s, "to compact into one body," from Latin consolidatus, past participle of consolidare "to make solid," from com- "together" (see com-) + solidare "to make solid" (see solid).

meanings.definitionCn: 生物化学的， 生化的

morphology.mnemonic: 生命/生物 + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: also bio-chemical, 1851, after German biochemisch, from bio- + chemical.

meanings.definitionCn: 图书馆馆长； 图书管理员

etymology.story: "custodian of a library," 1713; see library + -an.

1660s, a term in painting and sculpture, from French contour "circumference, outline," from Italian and Medieval Latin contornare "to go around," from Latin com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + tornare "to turn (on a lathe);" see turn (v.).
"a lyric poem," 1580s, from Middle French lyrique "short poem expressing personal emotion," from Latin lyricus "of or for the lyre," from Greek lyrikos "singing to the lyre," from lyra (see lyre).

meanings.definitionCn: 收买， 买通， 唆使

etymology.story: "to procure unlawfully, to bribe to accomplish a wicked purpose, especially to induce a witness to perjury, "to lure (someone) to commit a crime," 1530s, from Middle French suborner "seduce, instigate, bribe" (13c.) and directly from Latin subornare "employ as a secret agent, incite secretly," originally "equip, fit out, furnish," from sub "under, secretly" (see sub-) + ornare "equip," related to ordo "order" (see order (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 家族， 宗族， 氏族； 帮派

etymology.story: early 15c., from Gaelic clann "family, stock, offspring," akin to Old Irish cland "offspring, tribe," both from Latin planta "offshoot" (see plant (n.)).
"a letting loose, surrender to natural impulses," 1822, from a sense in French abandon (see abandon (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 蛤， 蛤肉； 〈口〉守口如瓶的人， 寡言的人
经济的， 实惠的； 节俭的， 节约的； 精打细算的

morphology.mnemonic: 经济/管理 + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1570s, "pertaining to household management;" from economic + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 磨光， 擦亮； 修改， 润色
内阁； 储藏柜， 陈列柜

etymology.story: 1540s, "secret storehouse, treasure chamber," from Middle French cabinet "small room" (16c.), diminutive of Old French cabane "cabin" (see cabin); perhaps influenced by (or rather, from) Italian gabbinetto, diminutive of gabbia, from Latin cavea "stall, stoop, cage, den for animals" (see cave (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 顺序/等级 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., "regular, ordinary," from Old French ordinel and directly from Late Latin ordinalis ""showing order, denoting an order of succession," from Latin ordo (genitive ordinis) "row, series" (see order (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 保险； 保险费； 保险业

etymology.story: 1550s, "engagement to marry," a variant of ensurance "an assurance, pledge, guarantee," from Old French enseurance "assurance," from ensurer, from en- "make" (see en- (1)) + sur "safe, secure, undoubted" (see sure (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 原则； 原理； 道德准则， 行为规范； 信念

etymology.story: late 14c., "origin, source, beginning; rule of conduct; axiom, basic assumption; elemental aspect of a craft or discipline," from Anglo-French principle, Old French principe "origin, cause, principle," from Latin principium (plural principia) "a beginning, commencement, origin, first part," in plural "foundation, elements," from princeps (see prince).

meanings.definitionCn: 单调乏味的， 令人厌烦的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French tedieus, from Late Latin taediosus "wearisome, irksome, tedious," from Latin taedium (see tedium).

meanings.definitionCn: 源， 源泉； 来源， 出处； 信息来源

etymology.story: "obtain from a specified source," 1972, from source (n.).
1932, from French télécommunication (see tele- + communication).

1560s, from Middle French participant, from Latin participantem (nominative participans), present participle of participare "to share in, partake of" from particeps "sharing, partaking" (see participation)..

meanings.definitionCn: 发起， 开始， 创始； 接纳， 让…加入

morphology.mnemonic: 开始/初始 + 使…/具有…的

meanings.definitionCn: 国内的； 家庭的； 驯养的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French domestique (14c.) and directly from Latin domesticus "belonging to the household," from domus "house," from PIE *dom-o- "house," from root *dem- "house, household" (source also of Sanskrit damah "house;" Avestan demana- "house;" Greek domos "house," despotes "master, lord;" Latin dominus "master of a household;" Old Church Slavonic domu, Russian dom "house;" Lithuanian dimstis "enclosed court, property;" Old Norse topt "homestead").

meanings.definitionCn: 怒目而视； 发出刺眼的光
陆地的， 陆生的， 陆栖的； 地球的

etymology.story: late 14c., "of or pertaining to the earth," with + -al (1) + from Latin terrestris "earthly, of the earth, on land," from terra "earth" (see terrain).

meanings.definitionCn: 骗局； 诡计； 欺骗， 欺诈

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + 取/抓 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French déception (13c., decepcion) or directly from Late Latin deceptionem (nominative deceptio) "a deceiving," from Latin decept-, past participle stem of decipere (see deceive)..

meanings.definitionCn: 汇编； 编辑， 编纂

etymology.story: early 14c., from Old French compiler "compile, collect" (13c.), from Latin compilare "to plunder, rob," probably originally "bundle together, heap up;" hence "to pack up and carry off," from com- "together" (see com-) + pilare "to compress, ram down."

meanings.definitionCn: 辐射， 发射； 流露， 显示； 自中心向各方伸展

morphology.mnemonic: 射线/放射 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: "having rays, furnished with rays, shining," 1660s, from Latin radiatus (see radiate (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 紧张； 拉紧； 张力

morphology.mnemonic: 持有/保持 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1530s, "a stretched condition," from Middle French tension (16c.) or directly from Latin tensionem (nominative tensio) "a stretching" (in Medieval Latin "a struggle, contest"), noun of state from tensus, past participle of tendere "to stretch," from PIE root *ten- "stretch" (see tenet).

meanings.definitionCn: 纤细的； 稀薄的； 脆弱的

etymology.story: 1590s, "thin, unsubstantial," irregularly formed from Latin tenuis "thin, drawn out, meager, slim, slender," figuratively "trifling, insignificant, poor, low in rank," from PIE root *ten- "to stretch" (source also of Sanskrit tanuh "thin," literally "stretched out;" see tenet) + -ous.

meanings.definitionCn: 反映； 映像； 深思， 考虑； 回忆； 记录， 描述

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 弯曲 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., reflexion, in reference to surfaces throwing back light or heat, from Late Latin reflexionem (nominative reflexio) "a reflection," literally "a bending back," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin reflectere "to bend back, bend backwards, turn away," from re- "back" (see re-) + flectere "to bend" (see flexible).

"college grounds," 1774, from Latin campus "a field," probably properly "an expanse surrounded" (by woods, higher ground, etc.), from PIE *kampos "a corner, cove," from root *kamp- "to bend" (source also of Lithuanian kampus "corner," Polish kępa "cluster of trees or brush").

meanings.definitionCn: 最好的事物； 最大的或最先进的事物

etymology.story: 1650s, from Late Latin ultimatus, past participle of ultimare "to be final, come to an end," from Latin ultimus (fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 起诉， 控告， 检举； 担任控方律师； 继续从事

etymology.story: early 15c., "follow up, pursue" (some course or action), from Latin prosecutus, past participle of prosequi "follow after, accompany; chase, pursue; attack, assail, abuse" (see pursue).

meanings.definitionCn: 口头的； 言辞的； 动词的

morphology.mnemonic: 词 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., "dealing with words" (especially in contrast to things or realities), from Old French verbal (14c.) and directly from Late Latin verbalis "consisting of words, relating to verbs," from Latin verbum "word" (see verb).

meanings.definitionCn: 地区， 区域； 管区， 行政区

etymology.story: 1610s, "territory under the jurisdiction of a lord or officer," from French district (16c.), from Medieval Latin districtus "restraining of offenders, jurisdiction," then under the feudal system "area of jurisdiction," noun use of past participle of Latin distringere "hinder, detain" (see distress).
1660s, from Latin arboreus "pertaining to trees," from arbor "tree," which is of unknown origin, + -al (1)..

morphology.mnemonic: 过度/超越 + wholly, without limit

etymology.story: "everywhere," Old English ofer eall, from ofer "over" (see over) + eall (see all).

early 15c., assumpten "to receive up into heaven" (especially of the Virgin Mary), also assumen "to arrogate," from Latin assumere, adsumere "to take up, take to oneself, take besides, obtain in addition," from ad- "to, up" (see ad-) + sumere "to take," from sub "under" (see sub-) + emere "to take" (see exempt (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 撒胡椒粉； 大量给予
醇香； 柔和； 成熟
概念， 观念； 设想

etymology.story: 1550s, from Medieval Latin conceptum "draft, abstract," in classical Latin "(a thing) conceived," from concep-, past participle stem of concipere "to take in" (see conceive).

meanings.definitionCn: 没有人情味的， 冷淡的； 客观的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + person + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: mid-15c., a grammatical term, from Late Latin impersonalis, from assimilated form of in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + personalis "personal" (see personal).
1630s, from Modern Latin millennium, from Latin mille "thousand" (see million) + annus "year" (see annual); formed on analogy of biennium, triennium, etc.

meanings.definitionCn: 抵抗的， 有抵抗力的； 抵制的； 抗…的， 耐…的

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 站立/设置 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: mid-14c., "legendary lizard-like creature that can live in fire," from Old French salamandre "legendary fiery beast," also "cricket" (12c.), from Latin salamandra, from Greek salamandra, probably of eastern origin.

1590s, from Middle French désastre (1560s), from Italian disastro "ill-starred," from dis-, here merely pejorative (see dis-) + astro "star, planet," from Latin astrum, from Greek astron (see star (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 赤裸的；严酷的，严峻的；明显的，鲜明的；十足的，极端的

etymology.story: Old English stearc "stiff, strong, rigid, obstinate; stern, severe, hard; harsh, rough, violent," from Proto-Germanic *starkaz (source also of Old Norse sterkr, Danish, Old Frisian sterk, Middle Dutch starc, Old High German starah, German stark, Gothic *starks), from PIE root *ster- (1) "stiff, rigid" (see stereo-).

morphology.mnemonic: 通过/彻底 + 取/抓 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1852; see percept + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 毛皮似的； 盖着毛皮的； 生苔的

etymology.story: 1670s, "made of fur, covered with fur," from fur + -y (2).

late 13c., anhaunsen "to raise, make higher," from Anglo-French enhauncer, probably from Old French enhaucier "make greater, make higher or louder; fatten, foster; raise in esteem," from Vulgar Latin *inaltiare, from Late Latin inaltare "raise, exalt,"  from altare "make high," from altus "high" (see old).

meanings.definitionCn: 会议； 习俗， 惯例； 公约

etymology.story: early 15c., "a formal agreement," also "a formal meeting or convention" (of rulers, etc.), also "a secret agreement," from Middle French convention and directly from Latin conventionem (nominative conventio) "meeting, assembly, covenant," noun of action from past participle stem of convenire (see convene)..
1779, from Modern Latin cranium, from Greek kranion "skull" (see cranium) + -al (1)..

meanings.definitionCn: 保持， 维持； 支持， 支撑； 经受， 承受
社会道德沦丧， 失范； 混乱

etymology.story: "absence of accepted social values," 1933, from Durkheim's "Suicide" (1897); a reborrowing with French spelling of anomy..

meanings.definitionCn: 勉力完成； 应付； 操纵， 控制； 管理

etymology.story: 1560s, probably from Italian maneggiare "to handle," especially "to control a horse," ultimately from Latin noun manus "hand" (see manual (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 同等的人； 相等的事物
漂亮的， 时髦的； 高雅的， 有格调的

etymology.story: "conformable to approved fashion or taste," 1795, from style (n.) + -ish.

meanings.definitionCn: 令人惊异的人， 奇迹； 不凡的成果

etymology.story: 1811, from silica + -ate (3)..

meanings.definitionCn: 重力； 严重性； 严肃， 庄严

etymology.story: 1550s, from Middle French récipient (16c.) and directly from Latin recipientem (nominative recipiens), present participle of recipere (see receive).

meanings.definitionCn: 施催眠术； 使入迷， 迷住

etymology.story: 1829, back-formation from mesmerism.

meanings.definitionCn: 机动车停放； 停车场

etymology.story: "act of putting (a vehicle) in a certain place," 1915, verbal noun from park (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 工作室， 画室； 摄影室， 演播室， 录音室； 练习室； 电影公司

etymology.story: 1819, "work-room of a sculptor or painter," usually one with windows to admit light from the sky, from Italian studio "room for study," from Latin studium (see study (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 招致， 招惹， 遭受

morphology.mnemonic: swallow hastily + to parry, to fend off

etymology.story: late 15c., "blunt, dull" (in manners), from Dutch plomp "blunt, thick, massive, stumpy," probably related to plompen "fall or drop heavily" (see plump (v.)).
mid-15c., on brest, from a- (1) + breast (n.); the notion is of "with breasts in line." To keep abreast in figurative sense of "stay up-to-date" is from 1650s..

meanings.definitionCn: 弹回；后退，退缩；产生后坐力
后退， 退缩； 反冲， 后坐力
支配， 统治； 控制； 盛行

etymology.story: 1610s, from Latin dominatus, past participle of dominari "to rule, dominate, to govern," from dominus (see domain).

meanings.definitionCn: 交互式的； 合作的， 互相配合的

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + 做/驱动 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: "acting upon or influencing each other," 1832, from interact (v.), probably on model of active.

meanings.definitionCn: 倦怠的， 没精打采的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Middle English liste "pleasure, joy, delight" (see list (v.4)) + -less.

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 填充/补充 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1620s, "ceremonious," from complement + -ary.

meanings.definitionCn: 浓度， 密度； 一致性， 协调； 连接， 结合

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 站立/设置 + ancy

etymology.story: 1590s, "firmness of matter," from Medieval Latin consistentia or directly from Latin consistentem, from consistere (see consist).

Old English ordel, ordal, "trial by physical test," literally "judgment, verdict," from Proto-Germanic noun *uz-dailjam (source also of Old Saxon urdeli, Old Frisian urdel, Dutch oordeel, German urteil "judgment"), literally "that which is dealt out" (by the gods), from *uzdailijan "share out," related to Old English adælan "to deal out" (see deal (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 灵长类， 灵长目动物； 大主教

morphology.mnemonic: thin, filmy portion + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1717, from wisp + -y (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 房屋和地基， 经营场址； 前提， 假设
聚居于， 大批生长于； 开拓殖民地， 移居于

etymology.story: 1620s, "to settle with colonists," from stem of Latin colonus "tiller of the soil, farmer" (see colony); in sense "to make another place into a national dependency" without regard for settlement there by 1790s (such as in reference to French activity in Egypt or British work in India), and probably directly from colony.

meanings.definitionCn: 季节； 时节； 时期

etymology.story: "improve the flavor of by adding spices," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 引起争论的， 有争议的

etymology.story: 1580s, from Late Latin controversialis "pertaining to controversy," from Latin controversia (see controversy)..

morphology.mnemonic: 持有/保持 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: early 14c., "person who holds lands by title or by lease," from Anglo-French tenaunt (late 13c.), Old French tenant "possessor; feudal tenant" (12c.), noun use of present participle of tenir "to hold," from Latin tenere "hold, keep, grasp" (see tenet).
mid-15c., "covered walk or passageway, narrow and partly open passageway along a wall," from Old French galerie "a long portico" (14c.), from Medieval Latin galeria, of unknown origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 恢复； 整修， 修复； 复位， 复原； 归还

root.meaning: place or thing that is standing

morphology.mnemonic: place or thing that is standing + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "a means of healing or restoring health; renewing of something lost," from Old French restoration (Modern French restauration) and directly from Late Latin restorationem (nominative restoratio), noun of action from past participle stem of Latin restaurare (see restore).

meanings.definitionCn: 变得苍白， 变得暗淡
苍白的；淡色的；暗淡的，微弱的

etymology.story: late 14c., "become pale; appear pale" (also, in Middle English, "to make pale"), from Old French paleir (12c.) or from pale (adj.).

meanings.definitionCn: 五金制品， 金属制品； 硬件

etymology.story: mid-15c., "small metal goods," from hard (adj.) + ware (n.).
"flour, eggs, and milk beaten together," late 14c., from Old French batteure "a beating," from Latin battuere "to beat, knock" (see batter (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 不幸的人； 恶棍， 无赖

etymology.story: Old English wrecca "wretch, stranger, exile," from Proto-Germanic *wrakjon "pursuer; one pursued" (source also of Old Saxon wrekkio, Old High German reckeo "a banished person, exile," German recke "renowned warrior, hero"), related to Old English wreccan "to drive out, punish" (see wreak).

meanings.definitionCn: 优先权， 优先； 优先考虑的事， 最重要的事

etymology.story: late 14c., "state of being earlier," from Old French priorite (14c.), from Medieval Latin prioritatem (nominative prioritas) "fact or condition of being prior," from Latin prior (see prior (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 金属般的； 金属制的； 含金属的

etymology.story: 1560s, from Middle French métallique or directly from Latin metallicus, from Greek metallikos, from metallon (see metal)..

meanings.definitionCn: 有创造力的， 创造性的

etymology.story: 1670s, "having the quality of creating," from create + -ive.

meanings.definitionCn: 神话； 神话学； 虚幻的想法

etymology.story: early 15c., "exposition of myths," from Middle French mythologie and directly from Late Latin mythologia, from Greek mythologia "legendary lore, a telling of mythic legends; a legend, story, tale," from mythos "myth" (of unknown origin) + -logy "study." Meaning "a body of myths" first recorded 1781..

meanings.definitionCn: 穿梭往返；短程往返运送
航天飞机； 梭子； 航班

etymology.story: Old English scytel "a dart, arrow," from Proto-Germanic *skutilaz (source also of Old Norse skutill "harpoon"), from PIE *skeud- "to shoot, to chase, to throw, to project" (see shoot (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 分配， 分发； 散布

etymology.story: early 15c., "to deal out or apportion," from Latin distributus, past participle of distribuere "to divide, distribute" (see distribution).

1919, from Yiddish beygl, from Middle High German boug- "ring, bracelet," from Old High German boug "a ring," related to Old English beag "ring" (in poetry, an Anglo-Saxon lord was beaggifa "ring-giver"), from Proto-Germanic *baugaz, from PIE root *bheug- (3) "to bend," with derivatives referring to bent, pliable, or curved objects (such as Old High German biogan "to bend;" see bow (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 基本的， 基础的； 初步的， 初级的

etymology.story: 1832, originally in chemistry, from base (n.) + -ic..

meanings.definitionCn: 原木，木材；日志，航海日志，飞行日志
企业， 公司； 事业

etymology.story: early 15c., "an undertaking," formerly also enterprize, from Old French enterprise "an undertaking," noun use of fem.

mid-13c., faucon, from Old French faucon "falcon" (12c.), from Late Latin falconem (nominative falco) "falcon" (source also of Old Spanish falcon, Portuguese falcão, Italian falcone, Old High German falcho, German Falke, Dutch valk), probably from Latin falx (genitive falcis) "curved blade, pruning hook, sickle, war-scythe" (see falcate); the bird said to be so called for the shape of its talons, legs, or beak, but also possibly from the shape of its spread wings.

meanings.definitionCn: 通风设备； 空气流通

root.meaning: supply a room with fresh air

morphology.mnemonic: supply a room with fresh air + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: "process of replacing foul air in an enclosed place with fresh, pure air," 1660s, from Latin ventilationem (nominative ventilatio) "an exposing to the air," noun of action from past participle stem of ventilare (see ventilate)..

meanings.definitionCn: 太阳的， 日光的； 太阳能的

morphology.mnemonic: 独自/太阳 + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "pertaining to the sun," from Latin solaris "of the sun," from sol "sun" (see sol).

meanings.definitionCn: 消耗， 损耗； 消费； 吃， 喝； 使充满； 烧毁， 毁灭

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French consumer "to consume" (12c.) and directly from Latin consumere "to use up, eat, waste," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + sumere "to take," from sub- "under" + emere "to buy, take" (see exempt (adj.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 形成， 构成； 形成物； 编队

morphology.mnemonic: 形状/形态 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "vital force in plants and animals;" early 15c., "act of creating or making," from Old French formacion "formation, fashioning, creation" (12c.) or directly from Latin formationem (nominative formatio) "a forming, shaping," noun of action or condition from past participle stem of formare "to form," from forma "form, shape" (see form (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 心理分析， 精神分析

etymology.story: 1906, from Psychoanalyse, coined 1896 in French by Freud from Latinized form of Greek psykhe- "mental" (see psyche) + German Analyse, from Greek analysis (see analysis).
1873, from bilingual + -ism..

meanings.definitionCn: 能力， 胜任； 权限； 技能

etymology.story: 1590s, "rivalry" (based on compete); c.

meanings.definitionCn: 融合；熔接；熔化；因保险丝烧断而断电；合并
保险丝； 引信； 导火线

etymology.story: "combustible cord or tube for lighting an explosive device," also fuze, 1640s, from Italian fuso, literally "spindle" (the ignition device so called for its shape, because the originals were long, thin tubes filled with gunpowder), from Latin fusus "a spindle," which is of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 琐碎的， 无关紧要的； 愚蠢的， 可笑的； 无聊的， 不严肃的， 轻佻的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin frivolus "silly, empty, trifling, worthless," diminutive of *frivos "broken, crumbled," from friare "break, rub away, crumble" (see friable).

1510s, from French prétexte, from Latin praetextum "a pretext, outward display," noun use of neuter past participle of praetexere "to disguise, cover," literally "weave in front" (for sense, compare pull the wool over (someone's) eyes); from prae- "in front" (see pre-) + texere "to weave," from PIE root *teks- "to weave, to make" (see texture (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 突现的； 新兴的； 紧急的

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + 沉入/合并 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: late 14c., "rising from what surrounds it, coming into view," from Latin emergentem (nominative emergens), present participle of emergere "to rise out or up" (see emerge)..

late 14c., from Old French asseurer (12c., Modern French assurer) "to reassure, calm, protect, to render sure," from Vulgar Latin *assecurar, from Latin ad- "to" (see ad-) + securus "safe, secure" (see secure (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 请教； 商议； 参考， 翻阅

etymology.story: 1520s, from Middle French consulter (16c.), from Latin consultare "consult, take the advice of," frequentative of consulere "to take counsel, meet and consider" (see consultation).

meanings.definitionCn: 基金， 资金； 提供资金

etymology.story: 1776, verbal noun from fund (v.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 幸免， 幸存； 继续存在， 存货； 艰难度过； 比…长命

morphology.mnemonic: 开始/初始 + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 未充分发展的， 原始的； 基本的， 基础的

morphology.mnemonic: 原始，粗野 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1827; see rudiment + -ary.

mid-14c., aduersere, from Anglo-French adverser (13c.), Old French adversaire "adversary, opponent, enemy," or directly from Latin adversarius "opponent, adversary, rival," noun use of adjective meaning "opposite, hostile, contrary," literally "turned toward one," from adversus "turned against" (see adverse).
1580s, "a request, demand," from Latin postulatum "demand, request," properly "that which is requested," noun use of neuter past participle of postulare (see postulate (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 大批滋生， 大批出没于

etymology.story: late 15c., "to attack, assail, hurt, distress, annoy," from Old French infester (14c.), from Latin infestare "to attack, disturb, trouble," from infestus "unsafe, hostile, threatening, dangerous," originally "inexorable, not able to be handled," from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + -festus "(able to be) seized." Sense of "swarm over in large numbers, attack parasitically" first recorded c.

late 14c., from Old French eternel "eternal," or directly from Late Latin aeternalis, from Latin aeternus "of an age, lasting, enduring, permanent, everlasting, endless," contraction of aeviternus "of great age," from aevum "age" (see eon).

meanings.definitionCn: 变皱， 起皱； 破裂； 崩溃， 垮台
讽刺文学， 讽刺作品； 讽刺

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + 强壮/价值 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: early 15c., "having great power or force," from Latin praevalentem (nominative praevalens) "of superior strength; mighty," present participle of praevalere "to be more able" (see prevail).

meanings.definitionCn: 附着， 黏住； 紧紧抓住； 挨近

etymology.story: Old English clingan "hold fast, adhere closely; congeal, shrivel" (strong verb, past tense clang, past participle clungen), from Proto-Germanic *klingg- (source also of Danish klynge "to cluster;" Old High German klinga "narrow gorge;" Old Norse klengjask "press onward;" Danish klinke, Dutch klinken "to clench;" German Klinke "latch").

meanings.definitionCn: 夹子， 回形针， 别针； 弹夹， 弹仓； 电影片段， 剪报

etymology.story: 1610s, from French oasis (18c.) and directly from Late Latin oasis, from Greek oasis, probably from Hamitic (compare Coptic wahe, ouahe "oasis," properly "dwelling place," from ouih "dwell").

"a nutritious substance," 1828, noun use of adjective (1640s) meaning "providing nourishment," from Latin nutrientem (nominative nutriens), present participle of nutrire "to nourish, suckle, feed, foster" (see nourish)..
early 15c., from Latin compactus, past participle of compingere "to fasten together" (see compact (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 罪犯， 歹徒， 亡命之徒； 被剥夺法律权益者

etymology.story: Old English utlagian "to outlaw, banish," from utlaga "an outlaw" (see outlaw (n.)).

"supply land with water," 1610s, from Latin irrigatus, past participle of irrigare "lead water to, refresh, irrigate, flood," from assimilated form of in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + rigare "to water, to moisten," of uncertain origin.

1560s (implied in shriveled), of unknown origin, not found in Middle English; perhaps from a Scandinavian source (compare Swedish skryvla "to wrinkle, to shrivel"), perhaps ultimately connected with shrimp (n.) and shrink (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 在表面形成硬壳， 结壳； 用…装饰外层

etymology.story: also incrust, 1640s, from Middle French encruster, incruster (Modern French encroûter), from Latin incrustare "to coat or cover with crust," from in- (see in- (2)) + crusta (see crust (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 向上/提高 + rise high and roll forcefully

etymology.story: 1916, from up (adv.) + surge (n.)..
mid-13c., blomen, from the noun (see bloom (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 表示， 意指； 标示， 预示， 象征

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + notice, attention, reputation

etymology.story: 1590s, from Middle French dénoter (14c.), from Latin denotare "denote, mark out," from de- "completely" (see de-) + notare "to mark" (see note (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 将…划做特殊区域；分区，划分地带
地区， 地域， 地带； 气候带

etymology.story: 1520s, from Middle French audible and directly from Late Latin audibilis, from Latin audire "to hear" (see audience).

meanings.definitionCn: 劝告， 规劝， 告诫
繁殖； 散布， 传播

etymology.story: 1560s, "to cause to multiply," from Latin propagatus, past participle of propagare "to set forward, extend, procreate" (see propagation).

meanings.definitionCn: 格子花纹的； 棋盘状的

etymology.story: early 15c., "ornamented with a checkered design," past participle adjective from check (v.2)..
1620s, from Medieval Latin necessitatus, past participle of necessitare "to render necessary," from Latin necessitas (see necessity).
