etymology.story: early 15c., "to give a part of (one's possessions);" late 15c., "to share, take part in," from Old French empartir, impartir "assign, allot, allocate, share out" (14c.), from Late Latin impartire (also impertire) "to share in, divide with another; communicate," from assimilated form of in- "into, in" (see in- (2)) + partire "to divide, part" (see part (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 鼓舞， 激励； 给…以灵感

etymology.story: mid-14c., enspiren, "to fill (the mind, heart, etc., with grace, etc.);" also "to prompt or induce (someone to do something)," from Old French enspirer (13c.), from Latin inspirare "blow into, breathe upon," figuratively "inspire, excite, inflame," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + spirare "to breathe" (see spirit (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 官僚主义， 官僚作风； 政府机构， 官僚机构

etymology.story: 1818, from French bureaucratie, coined by French economist Jean Claude Marie Vincent de Gournay (1712-1759) on model of democratie, aristocratie, from bureau "office," literally "desk" (see bureau) + Greek suffix -kratia denoting "power of" (see -cracy)..

morphology.mnemonic: 地球 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: early 14c., also gemetrie, gemetry, from Old French geometrie (12c., Modern French géométrie), from Latin geometria, from Greek geometria "measurement of earth or land; geometry," from comb.
1580s, from Latin devotus, past participle of devovere (see devotion).

meanings.definitionCn: 美味可口的； 有吸引力的； 令人喜爱的， 赏心悦目的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French delectable, from Latin delectabilis "delightful," from delectare (see delight (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 鱼钩， 倒钩； 挖苦的话

etymology.story: late 15c., "to clip, mow;" see barb (n.).
1640s, from Latin alumnus "a pupil," literally "foster son," vestigial present passive participle of alere "to nourish" (see old), with ending akin to Greek -omenos.
also far fetched, farfetched, 1560s, "brought from afar," from far (adv.) + past participle of fetch (v.).

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 近/接近 + 使…/具有…的

meanings.definitionCn: 能工巧匠， 手艺人， 工艺师

etymology.story: mid-14c., craftes man, originally "a member of a craft guild," from genitive of craft (n.) + man (n.1).

meanings.definitionCn: 放映； 规划； 预测， 推想
方案； 课题， 项目； 工程

etymology.story: late 15c., "to plan," from Latin proiectus, past participle of proicere (see project (n.)).
1798, originally in natural history, from Latin aberrantem (nominative aberrans), present participle of aberrare "to wander away, go astray" (see aberration)..
"to run with long strides," early 15c.; earlier "to leap, jump, spring" (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 侧面的， 旁边的； 横向的

etymology.story: "of or pertaining to the side," early 15c., from Olde French latéral (14c.) and directly from Latin lateralis "belonging to the side," from latus (genitive lateris) "the side, flank; lateral surface" (see oblate (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 内地， 腹地； 内地贸易区

etymology.story: 1890, from German Hinterland, from hinter "behind" (see hinder (adj.)) + Land "country" (see land (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 任命， 指定； 提名， 推荐

etymology.story: 1540s, "to call by name," back-formation from nomination or else from Latin nominatus, past participle of nominare "to name, call by name, give a name to," also "name for office,"" from nomen "name" (see name (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 轮胎面； 脚步声； 步法

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French diametre, from Latin diametrus, from Greek diametros (gramme) "diagonal of a circle," from dia- "across, through" (see dia-) + metron "a measure" (see meter (n.2))..

meanings.definitionCn: 静止的； 固定的， 稳定的， 不变的

morphology.mnemonic: 站立/设置 + 状态/行为/过程 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: late 14c., "having no apparent motion" (in reference to planets), from Middle French stationnaire "motionless" and directly from Latin stationarius, from the stem of statio "a standing, post, job, position" (see station (n.)).

Old English bereærn "barn," literally "barley house," from bere "barley" (see barley) + aern "house," metathesized from *rann, *rasn (source also of Old Norse rann, Gothic razn "house," Old English rest "resting place").
in reference to a dog sound, Old English beorcan "to bark," from Proto-Germanic *berkan (source also of Old Norse berkja "to bark"), of echoic origin.
1753, from French ellipse (17c.), from Latin ellipsis "ellipse," also, "a falling short, deficit," from Greek elleipsis (see ellipsis).

meanings.definitionCn: 赤裸的；光秃的，无遮盖的；最基本的

etymology.story: "path for pedestrians on the side of a street," 1739, from side (adj.) + walk (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 难对付的； 强大的； 令人敬畏的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "causing fear," from Middle French formidable (15c.), from Latin formidabilis "causing fear, terrible," from formidare "to fear," from formido "fearfulness, fear, terror, dread." Sense has softened somewhat over time, in the direction of "so great (in strength, size, etc.) as to discourage effort."

meanings.definitionCn: 富于； 大量存在； 充满

etymology.story: early 14c., from Old French abonder "to abound, be abundant, come together in great numbers" (12c.), from Latin abundare "overflow, run over," from Latin ab- "off" (see ab-) + undare "rise in a wave," from unda "a wave," from PIE *unda-, nasalized form of root *wed- (1) "water, wet" (see water (n.1)).
1898, from suit (n.) + case (n.2).

meanings.definitionCn: 分离的， 个别的； 离散的

etymology.story: "separate, distinct," late 14c., from Old French discret, discre, and directly from Latin discretus "separated;" see discreet.

meanings.definitionCn: 气候温和的， 温带的； 温和的， 自我克制的

root.meaning: degree of hardness and resiliency in steel

morphology.mnemonic: degree of hardness and resiliency in steel + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: late 14c., of persons, "modest, forbearing, self-restrained, not swayed by passion;" of climates or seasons, "not liable to excessive heat or cold," from Latin temperatus "restrained, regulated, limited, moderate, sober, calm, steady," from past participle of temperare "to moderate, regulate" (see temper (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 不可改变的， 永恒不变的

etymology.story: early 15c., "unchanging, unalterable," from Old French immutable (Modern French immuable), and directly from Latin immutabilis "unchangeable, unalterable," from assimilated form of in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + mutabilis "changeable," from mutare "to change" (see mutable).

meanings.definitionCn: 使中断； 扰乱； 使分裂， 使瓦解

etymology.story: 1650s, but rare before c.

meanings.definitionCn: 给…留下疤痕；给…留下精神创伤；损害…的外观
夭折， 中止； 使流产

etymology.story: 1570s, "to miscarry," from Latin abortus, past participle of aboriri "to miscarry" (see abortive); 1610s as "to deliberately terminate" anything, but especially a pregnancy, which seems to be the literal sense.

morphology.mnemonic: 低于/不足 + 神 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1580s, from Latinized form of Greek hypothetikos "pertaining to a hypothesis," from hypothesis (see hypothesis).
"exact opposite," 1560s, from Latin conversus "turn around," past participle of convertere "to turn about" (see convert).

meanings.definitionCn: 准则， 信条； 绝对真理； [G-]福音； 福音音乐； 训示

etymology.story: Old English godspel "glad tidings announced by Jesus; one of the four gospels," literally "good spell," from god "good" (see good (adj.)) + spel "story, message" (see spell (n.1)).

root.meaning: to rest as at a center

morphology.mnemonic: ec + to rest as at a center + ic

etymology.story: 1550s, from Middle French eccentrique and directly from Medieval Latin eccentricus (noun and adjective; see eccentric (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 用具， 器具； 装置

root.meaning: seek a job by submitting an application for one

morphology.mnemonic: seek a job by submitting an application for one + ance

etymology.story: 1560s, "action of putting into use," from apply + -ance.

meanings.definitionCn: 昏昏欲睡的； 催眠的

etymology.story: 1520s, probably ultimately from Old English drusan, drusian "sink," also "become languid, slow, or inactive" (related to dreosan "to fall"), from Proto-Germanic *drus- (see dreary).

meanings.definitionCn: 学问， 学识； 传说， 传统

etymology.story: Old English lar "learning, what is taught, knowledge, science, doctrine, art of teaching," from Proto-Germanic *laizo (Old Saxon lera, Old Frisian lare, Middle Dutch lere, Dutch leer, Old High German lera, German Lehre "teaching, precept, doctrine"), from PIE *leis- (1) "track, furrow" (see learn)..

meanings.definitionCn: 船桅； 旗杆； 天线塔
质量；块，团；众多；群众，平民百姓

etymology.story: 1731, from mugen "to drizzle" (late 14c.), from a Scandinavian source such as Old Norse mugga "drizzling mist," possibly from PIE *meug- "slimy, slippery" (see mucus)..

meanings.definitionCn: 撤销， 撤回； 取消， 废除

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French revoquer (13c.), from Latin revocare "rescind, call back," from re- "back" (see re-) + vocare "to call" (see voice (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 正确的； 合乎体统的， 适当的； 固有的， 特有的
[the R-] 文艺复兴， 文艺复兴时期； 复兴， 再生

etymology.story: "great period of revival of classical-based art and learning in Europe that began in the fourteenth century," 1840, from French renaissance des lettres, from Old French renaissance, literally "rebirth," usually in a spiritual sense, from renastre "grow anew" (of plants), "be reborn" (Modern French renaître), from Vulgar Latin *renascere, from Latin renasci "be born again, rise again, reappear, be renewed," from re- "again" (see re-) + nasci "be born" (Old Latin gnasci; see genus).

meanings.definitionCn: 有毒的， 有害的； 恶毒的

etymology.story: 1570s, from poison (n.) + -ous.

meanings.definitionCn: 分配， 配给， 拨给

etymology.story: late 15c., from Old French aloter (Modern French allotir) "to divide by lots, to divide into lots," from à "to" (see ad-) + loter "lot," a word of Germanic origin (cognates: Gothic hlauts, Old High German hloz, Old English hlot; see lot).

meanings.definitionCn: 推进， 驱使； 激励

etymology.story: mid-15c., "to drive away, expel," from Latin propellere "push forward, drive forward, drive forth; move, impel," from pro- "forward" (see pro-) + pellere "to push, drive" (see pulse (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 承认； 让步； 允许

etymology.story: 1630s, from Middle French concéder or directly from Latin concedere "give way, yield, go away, depart, retire," figuratively "agree, consent, give precedence," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + cedere "to go, grant, give way" (see cede).

meanings.definitionCn: 猖獗的， 肆虐的； 蔓生的

etymology.story: late 14c., "standing on the hind legs" (as a heraldic lion often does), thus, also, "fierce, ravenous" (late 14c.), from Old French rampant, present participle of ramper "to climb, scale, mount" (see rampage (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 兴趣，关注；业余爱好；利息；利害关系
使感兴趣， 引起…关注

etymology.story: "cause to be interested, engage the attention of," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 不平均的； 不平坦的； 不规则的； 不势均力敌的； 不公平的

etymology.story: Old English unefen "unequal, unlike, anomalous, irregular," from un- (1) "not" + even (adj.).

Old English twig "twig, branch, shoot, small tree," from Proto-Germanic *twigga "a fork" (source also of Middle Dutch twijch, Dutch twijg, Old High German zwig, German Zweig "branch, twig"), from PIE *dwi-ko-, from *dwo- "two" (see two).

meanings.definitionCn: 未成熟的， 未充分发展的

etymology.story: 1540s, "untimely, premature," from Latin immaturus "untimely, unripe," from assimilated form of in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + maturus "ripe, timely, early" (see mature (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 有机的； 器官的； 有机体的

etymology.story: 1510s, "serving as an organ or instrument," from Latin organicus, from Greek organikos "of or pertaining to an organ, serving as instruments or engines," from organon "instrument" (see organ).

meanings.definitionCn: 贞洁的； 简朴的， 朴实的

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + 空 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

meanings.definitionCn: 资助人， 赞助人； 老主顾， 顾客

etymology.story: "a lord-master, a protector," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 申请， 请求； 适用； 应用， 运用； 涂， 敷， 施

etymology.story: late 14c., "to put (one's faculties, etc.) to some task or career," late 14c., from Old French aploiier "apply, use, attach" (12c., Modern French appliquer), from Latin applicare "attach to, join, connect;" figuratively, "devote (oneself) to, give attention," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + plicare "fold" (see ply (v.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 炖， 煨； 思考； 不安， 担忧

etymology.story: late 14c., transitive "to bathe (a person or a body part) in a steam bath," from Old French estuver "have a hot bath, plunge into a bath; stew" (Modern French étuver), of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 俯瞰， 眺望； 忽略； 对…不予考虑

morphology.mnemonic: 过度/超越 + appearance of a person

etymology.story: mid-14c., "to examine, scrutinize, inspect," from over- + look (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 惊人的， 难以置信的

etymology.story: early 15c., "unbelievable, surpassing belief as to what is possible," from Latin incredibilis "not to be believed, extraordinary," from in- "not" (see in- (1)) + credibilis "worthy of belief" (see credible).

meanings.definitionCn: 编排， 版面设计； 规划， 布局

etymology.story: also lay-out, "configuration, arrangement," 1852, from the verbal phrase; see lay (v.) + out (adv.).

meanings.definitionCn: 登陆； 着陆， 降落
公式， 方程式； 分子式； 配方

etymology.story: 1630s, "words used in a ceremony or ritual" (earlier as a Latin word in English), from Latin formula "form, draft, contract, regulation;" in law, "a rule, method;" literally "small form," diminutive of forma "form" (see form (n.)).

1763, from French avalanche (17c.), from Romansch (Swiss) avalantze "descent," altered (by metathesis of -l- and -v-, probably influenced by Old French avaler "to descend, go down") from Savoy dialect lavantse, from Provençal lavanca "avalanche," perhaps from a pre-Latin Alpine language (the suffix -anca suggests Ligurian).

meanings.definitionCn: 反叛，反抗；违抗；厌恶，反感
起义， 叛乱， 反抗

etymology.story: 1550s, from Middle French révolte (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 贡品； 颂词， 称赞； 悼念， 致哀； 礼物

etymology.story: mid-14c., "stated sum of money or other valuable consideration paid by one ruler or country to another in acknowledgment of submission or as the price of peace or protection," from Anglo-French tribute, Old French tribut and directly from Latin tributum "tribute, a stated payment, a thing contributed or paid," noun use of neuter of tributus, past participle of tribuere "to pay, assign, grant," also "allot among the tribes or to a tribe," from tribus (see tribe).
late 15c., from en- (1) "make, put in" + danger.

Old English stemn, stefn "stem of a plant, trunk of a tree," also "either end-post of a ship," from Proto-Germanic *stamniz (source also of Old Saxon stamm, Old Norse stafn "stem of a ship;" Danish stamme, Swedish stam "trunk of a tree;" Old High German stam, German Stamm), from suffixed form of PIE root *stā- "to stand" (see stet).

meanings.definitionCn: 使处于次要地位， 使从属于

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之下 + 顺序/等级 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: "manual laborer," late 14c., from Latin mechanicus, from Greek mekhanikos "an engineer," noun use of adjective meaning "full of resources, inventive, ingenious" (see mechanic (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 革命； 巨变； 旋转

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + volve + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., originally of celestial bodies, from Old French revolucion "course, revolution (of celestial bodies)" (13c.), or directly from Late Latin revolutionem (nominative revolutio) "a revolving," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin revolvere "turn, roll back" (see revolve).
1560s, raliabill, Scottish; see rely + -able.

meanings.definitionCn: 叙述， 讲述； 有关联

etymology.story: 1520s, "to recount, tell," from Middle French relater "refer, report" (14c.) and directly from Latin relatus, used as past participle of referre "bring back, bear back" (see refer), from re- "back, again" + latus (see oblate (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 初步做法， 起始行为

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + limin + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1660s, from French préliminaire and directly from Medieval Latin praeliminaris, from Latin prae- "before" (see pre-) + limen (genitive liminis) "threshold" (see limit (n.)).

mid-14c., from Old French obstacle, ostacle "opposition, obstruction, hindrance" (13c.) or directly from Latin obstaculum "a hindrance, obstacle," with instrumental suffix *-tlom + obstare "stand before, stand opposite to, block, hinder, thwart," from ob "against" (see ob-) + stare "to stand," from PIE root *stā- "to stand" (see stet).
also left-over, "remaining, not used up," 1890, from left + over.

meanings.definitionCn: 组成； 设立； 制定

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 建立/放置

etymology.story: mid-15c., verb use of adjective constitute, "made up, formed" (late 14c.), from Latin constitutus "arranged, settled," past participle adjective from constituere "to cause to stand, set up, fix, place, establish, set in order; form something new; resolve," of persons, "to appoint to an office," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + statuere "to set," from PIE root *stā- "to stand," with derivatives meaning "place or thing that is standing" (see stet).

meanings.definitionCn: 控诉， 起诉， 控告
送急件的人， 信使； 旅游服务员

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Anglo-French courrier, from Old French coreor, ultimately an agent noun from Latin currere "to run" (see current (adj.))..
1520s, "to overcome in argument," from Latin convincere "to overcome decisively," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + vincere "to conquer" (see victor).

meanings.definitionCn: 配偶；朋友，伙伴；同事；大副
逃窜， 狂奔； 热潮， 风尚

morphology.mnemonic: 终止/界限 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: early 15c., "bring to an end," from Latin terminatus, past participle of terminare "to mark the end or boundary," from terminus "end, limit" (see terminus).

meanings.definitionCn: 怪癖， 癖性； 过分的独特风格

morphology.mnemonic: customary practice + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: "excessive use of distinctive methods in art or literature," 1803, from manner + -ism.

meanings.definitionCn: 理论， 原理； 学说； 见解， 看法

morphology.mnemonic: 神 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1590s, "conception, mental scheme," from Late Latin theoria (Jerome), from Greek theoria "contemplation, speculation; a looking at, viewing; a sight, show, spectacle, things looked at," from theorein "to consider, speculate, look at," from theoros "spectator," from thea "a view" (see theater) + horan "to see," possibly from PIE root *wer- (4) "to perceive" (see ward (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 联盟， 同盟， 联邦； 私党

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 联盟 + acy

etymology.story: late 14c., from Anglo-French confederacie (Old French confederacie), from stem of Latin confoederatio, from confoederare (see confederate).

meanings.definitionCn: 延长； 延伸； 电话分机， 分机号码

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 伸展/趋向 + 状态/行为/过程

meanings.definitionCn: 内在的， 内部的； 国内的， 内政的； 内心的

morphology.mnemonic: in + tern + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., "extending toward the interior," from Medieval Latin internalis, from Latin internus "within, inward, internal," figuratively "domestic," expanded from pre-Latin *interos, *interus "on the inside, inward," from PIE *en-ter- (source also of Old Church Slavonic anter, Sanskrit antar "within, between," Old High German unter "between," and the "down" sense of Old English under); suffixed (comparative) form of *en "in" (see in (adv.)).
"to bring forth," late 14c., from Old French gendrer, genrer "engender, beget, give birth to," from Latin generare "to engender, beget, produce" (see generation).

meanings.definitionCn: 文学的； 文人的， 书卷气的

etymology.story: 1640s, "pertaining to alphabet letters," from French littéraire, from Latin literarius/litterarius "belonging to letters or learning," from littera/litera "letter" (see letter (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 建筑师； 设计师， 缔造者

etymology.story: 1550s, from Middle French architecte, from Latin architectus, from Greek arkhitekton "master builder, director of works," from arkhi- "chief" (see archon) + tekton "builder, carpenter" (see texture).
"cut out," 1570s, from Middle French exciser, from Latin excisus, past participle of excidere "cut out, cut down, cut off," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + -cidere, comb.

meanings.definitionCn: 重拳击打； 冲床， 打孔机

etymology.story: "to thrust, push; jostle;" also, "prod, to drive (cattle, etc.) by poking and prodding," late 14c., from Old French ponchonner "to punch, prick, stamp," from ponchon "pointed tool, piercing weapon" (see punch (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 穿过， 越过； 在对面

etymology.story: early 14c., acros, earlier a-croiz (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 灵活性； 弹性， 韧性； 适应性

etymology.story: 1610s, of physical things, from French flexibilité (in Old French, "weakness, vacillation") or directly from Late Latin flexibilitatem (nominative flexibilitas), from Latin flexibilis "pliant, yielding" (see flexible).

meanings.definitionCn: 抵押贷款，按揭；抵押证书
身份； 特征， 特性； 同一性
联邦的； 联邦制的； 联邦政府的

morphology.mnemonic: 联盟 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1640s, as a theological term (in reference to "covenants" between God and man), from French fédéral, an adjective formed from Latin foedus (genitive foederis) "covenant, league, treaty, alliance," from PIE *bhoid-es-, from root *bheidh- "to trust" (which also is the source of Latin fides "faith;" see faith).

early 14c., from Old French verifier "substantiate, find out the truth about" (14c.), from Medieval Latin verificare "make true," from Latin verus "true" (see very) + root of facere "to make" (see factitious)..

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + to swear profanely + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1570s, "a deviation in argument," also "a military sally," from Latin excursionem (nominative excursio) "a running forth, sally, excursion, expedition," figuratively "an outset, opening," noun of action from past participle stem of excurrere "run out, run forth, hasten forward; project, extend," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + currere "to run" (see current (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 为…举行就职典礼； 为…举行开幕式； 开始， 开创

etymology.story: "induct into office by formal ceremony," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 学会， 研究院； 专科院校

etymology.story: late 15c., "the classical Academy," from French Académie, from Latin Academia, from Greek Akademeia "grove of Akademos," a legendary Athenian of the Trojan War tales (his name apparently means "of a silent district"), whose estate, six stadia from Athens, was the enclosure where Plato taught his school.

meanings.definitionCn: 孤独， 单独； 独处， 独居； 隐居处

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French solitude "loneliness" (14c.) and directly from Latin solitudinem (nominative solitudo) "loneliness, a being alone; lonely place, desert, wilderness," from solus "alone" (see sole (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 奖赏， 奖励， 给以报酬

etymology.story: ball game played with a curved stick or club, 1527, implied in a document from Ireland ("The horlinge of the litill balle with hockie stickes or staves ..."), of unknown origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 吞，咽；吞没，侵吞；轻信，轻易接受；默默忍受；使不流露，抑制

etymology.story: "ingest through the throat" (transitive), Old English swelgan "swallow, imbibe, absorb" (class III strong verb; past tense swealg, past participle swolgen), from Proto-Germanic *swelgan/*swelhan (source also of Old Saxon farswelgan, Old Norse svelgja "to swallow," Middle Dutch swelghen, Dutch zwelgen "to gulp, swallow," Old High German swelahan "to swallow," German schwelgen "to revel"), probably from PIE root *swel- (1) "to eat, drink" (source also of Iranian *khvara- "eating").

meanings.definitionCn: 津贴， 利益； 特权

etymology.story: mid-15c., "property acquired other than by inheritance," from Medieval Latin perquisitum "thing gained, profit," in classical Latin, "thing sought after," noun use of neuter past participle of perquirere "to seek, ask for," from per- "thoroughly" (see per) + quærere "to seek" (see query (v.)).
"one of the family of mammals represented by man," 1889, from Modern Latin Hominidæ the biological family name (1825), from Latin homo (genitive hominis) "man" (see homunculus) + -id.
Old English stincg, steng "act of stinging, puncture, thrust," from the root of sting (v.).
late 13c., from Old French haster "hurry, make haste; urge, hurry along" (Modern French hâter), from haste "haste, urgency" (see haste).

meanings.definitionCn: 养育，养护，培养；扶植，支持；滋长，助长
扣紧； 变形， 弯曲

etymology.story: "spiked metal ring for holding a belt, etc., c.

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + gener + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1540s, from Latin degeneratus, past participle of degenerare "fall from ancestral quality" (see degenerate (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 破坏性的， 毁灭性的

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + 建造 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: late 15c., from Old French destructif (14c.), from Late Latin destructivus, from destruct-, past participle stem of Latin destruere (see destroy)..

morphology.mnemonic: 警告/提醒 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: early 13c., from Old French devot "pious, devoted, assiduous," from Latin devotus "given up by vow, devoted," past participle of devovere "dedicate by vow" (see devotion)..

meanings.definitionCn: 狼吞虎咽地吃， 吞食； 吞没， 吞噬； 如饥似渴地读

etymology.story: early 14c., from Old French devorer (12c.) "devour, swallow up, engulf," from Latin devorare "swallow down, accept eagerly," from de- "down" (see de-) + vorare "to swallow" (see voracity).

meanings.definitionCn: 搅拌；煽动；激发，打动；行动，活动
搅拌， 搅动； 激动， 震动

etymology.story: "commotion, disturbance, tumult," late 14c.

meanings.definitionCn: 存在， 实在； 生存， 生活

etymology.story: late 14c., "reality," from Old French existence, from Medieval Latin existentia/exsistentia, from existentem/exsistentem (nominative existens/exsistens) "existent," present participle of Latin existere/exsistere "stand forth, come out, emerge; appear, be visible, come to light; arise, be produced; turn into," and, as a secondary meaning, "exist, be;" from ex- "forth" (see ex-) + sistere "cause to stand" (see assist)..
Old English cræftan "to exercise a craft, build," from the same source as craft (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 轴； 轴线， 中心线

morphology.mnemonic: to remove, severely reduce, + 过程/状态

etymology.story: 1540s, "imaginary straight line around which a body (such as the Earth) rotates," from Latin axis "axle, pivot, axis of the earth or sky," from PIE *aks- "axis" (source also of Old English eax, Old High German ahsa "axle;" Greek axon "axis, axle, wagon;" Sanskrit aksah "an axle, axis, beam of a balance;" Lithuanian aszis "axle").

meanings.definitionCn: 迷宫；复杂难懂的细节；困惑，迷惘
使混乱； 使困惑， 迷惑
漂浮， 浮动； 飘动

etymology.story: apparently an early Middle English merger of three related Old English nouns, flota "boat, fleet," flote "troop, flock," flot "body of water, sea;" all from the source of float (v.).
"in the form of a gas," 1799, from gas (n.) + -ous.

meanings.definitionCn: 忽略的； 疏忽的； 粗心大意的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French negligent "careless, negligent" (13c.) or directly from Latin negligentem "heedless, careless, unconcerned" (see negligence).

meanings.definitionCn: 有表现力的， 富有表情的； 表示…的， 表现…的

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + device for pressing cloth + 有…倾向的
共同/联合 + 引导 + 有…倾向的 + ity

meanings.definitionCn: 荒凉的， 无人烟的； 不幸的， 忧伤的
使感到悲惨， 使感到凄凉； 使荒芜

etymology.story: early 15c., cors "ordinary" (modern spelling is from late 16c.), probably adjectival use of noun cours (see course (n.)), originally referring to rough cloth for ordinary wear.

meanings.definitionCn: 逃避， 回避； 躲开， 避开

etymology.story: 1510s, "escape," from Middle French evader, from Latin evadere "to escape, get away," from assimilated form of ex- "away" (see ex-) + vadere "to go, walk" (see vamoose).

meanings.definitionCn: 联想的； 结合的； 关联的

etymology.story: 1812, from associate (v.) + -ive..

1530s, "to pile up, accumulate," from Latin exaggeratus, past participle of exaggerare "heighten, amplify, magnify," literally "to heap, pile, load, fill," from ex- "thoroughly" (see ex-) + aggerare "heap up, accumulate," figuratively "amplify, magnify," from agger (genitive aggeris) "heap," from aggerere "bring together, carry toward," from assimilated form of ad- "to, toward" (see ad-) + gerere "carry" (see gest).
1650s, "outer shell, husk," from Latin cortex "bark of a tree" (see corium).

meanings.definitionCn: 意味着， 暗示； 说明， 表明； 使有必要

etymology.story: late 14c., implien, emplien "to enfold, enwrap, entangle" (the classical Latin sense), from Old French emplier, from Latin implicare "involve, enfold, entangle" (see implication).
1550s, "act of setting off" (on a journey, etc.), from off + set (adj.).
as a tennis stroke, 1650s, from back (adv.) + hand.

meanings.definitionCn: 一系列，一连串；顺序，次序，序列
供应， 供应品； 条款； 准备， 预备

morphology.mnemonic: 向前/支持 + 看见 + 状态/行为/过程

meanings.definitionCn: 戳， 刺； 要旨； 驱动力
废除， 废止； 废奴运动

morphology.mnemonic: to grow, + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1520s, from Middle French abolition or directly from Latin abolitionem (nominative abolitio) "an abolition," noun of action from past participle stem of abolere "destroy" (see abolish).

meanings.definitionCn: 偶发的， 间或出现的， 零星的

etymology.story: 1680s, from Medieval Latin sporadicus "scattered," from Greek sporadikos "scattered," from sporas (genitive sporados) "scattered, dispersed," from spora "a sowing" (see spore).

meanings.definitionCn: 出席人数； 产量， 产额
布局， 格局； 结构， 构造， 形状； 配置

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + to shape into + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1550s, from Latin configurationem (nominative configuratio), noun of action from past participle stem of configurare (see configure)..

meanings.definitionCn: 科学的； 细致严谨的

morphology.mnemonic: 知道 + …的人/处于…状态 + ic

etymology.story: 1580s, from Middle French scientifique, from Medieval Latin scientificus "pertaining to science," from Latin scientia "knowledge" (see science) + -ficus "making" + facere "to make" (see factitious).

root.meaning: range of something, place or scene of activity

morphology.mnemonic: range of something, place or scene of activity + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1520s, from sphere + -ical.

meanings.definitionCn: 厚片， 大块； 相当大的部分； 矮胖的人

etymology.story: 1802, coined probably by German-born English astronomer William Herschel (1738-1822) from Greek asteroeides "star-like," from aster "star" (see astro-) + -eidos "form, shape" (see -oid)..
1540s, "a statement generally accepted," literal translation of Latin locus communis, from Greek koinos topos "general topic." See common (adj.) + place (n.).
mid-15c., "make an initial statement setting out a plaintiff's case" (modern sense from 1560s), from libel (n.), q.v.

meanings.definitionCn: 参量， 参数； 界限， 范围

etymology.story: 1650s in geometry, from Modern Latin parameter (1630s), from Greek para- "beside, subsidiary" (see para- (1)) + metron "measure" (see meter (n.2)).

meanings.definitionCn: 灭绝的， 不存在的； 不再活跃的

etymology.story: early 15c., "extinguished, quenched," from Latin extinctus/exstinctus, past participle of extinguere/exstinguere "to put out, quench; go out, die out; kill, destroy" (see extinguish).

meanings.definitionCn: 先驱者， 开路人； 先兆， 预兆

morphology.mnemonic: 在前/预先 + continuous stretch + er
cut in cubes, + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: 1911, from French cubisme, from cube (see cube (n.)), said to have been coined by French art critic Louis Vauxcelles at the 1908 Salon des Indépendants in reference to a work by Georges Braque.

meanings.definitionCn: 苦苦思索； 焦虑不已

etymology.story: 1580s, "to torture," from Middle French agoniser or directly from Medieval Latin agonizare, from Greek agonizesthai "contend in the struggle" (see agony).

meanings.definitionCn: 过滤；渗入，渗透；逐渐流传，传开

etymology.story: 1620s, a back-formation from percolation, or else from Latin percolatus, past participle of percolare "to strain through." Figurative sense by 1670s.

meanings.definitionCn: 不透明的， 不透光的； 难懂的， 晦涩的

etymology.story: early 15c., opake, from Latin opacus "shaded, in the shade, shady, dark, darkened, obscure," of unknown origin.

early 14c., avantage, "position of being in advance of another," from Old French avantage "advantage, profit, superiority," from avant "before," probably via an unrecorded Late Latin *abantaticum, from Latin abante (see advance).
mid-15c., "helpful, of assistance," from Middle French assistent (see assistant (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 湿的， 潮湿的； 湿润的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French humide, umide "damp, wet" (15c.) or directly from Latin humidus "moist, wet," variant (probably by influence of humus "earth") of umidus, from umere "be moist, be wet," from Proto-Italic *umo- "wet" (also source of Latin umidus "wet, moist," umiditas "moisture," umor "moisture, fluid," umectus "moist, wet"), perhaps from PIE *uhrmo- "wet," from the same source as Latin urina [de Vaan]..

meanings.definitionCn: 代替， 顶替； 反而， 却

etymology.story: 1590s, contraction of Middle English prepositional phrase ine stede (early 13c.; see stead), itself a loan-translation of Latin in loco (French en lieu de).

late 14c., from Old French controversie or directly from Latin controversia, from controversus "turned in an opposite direction, disputed, turned against," from contra- "against" (see contra) + versus (see verse)..

meanings.definitionCn: 命令； 指挥， 统率
必须做的， 义务的； 强制性的； 必修的

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 推/驱动 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1580s, from Medieval Latin compulsorius, from Latin compulsus, past participle of compellere "to drive together, force, compel" (see compel)..

root.meaning: to cause a woman to miscarry

morphology.mnemonic: to cause a woman to miscarry + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: late 14c., "born prematurely or dead," from Latin abortivus "pertaining to miscarriage; causing abortion," from abort-, past participle stem of aboriri "disappear, miscarry," from ab- "amiss" (see ab-) + oriri "appear, be born, arise" (see orchestra); the compound word used in Latin for deaths, miscarriages, sunsets, etc.

meanings.definitionCn: 密集， 云集； 成群地飞行

etymology.story: "cloud of bees or other insects," Old English swearm "swarm, multitude," from Proto-Germanic *swarmaz (source also of Old Saxon, Middle Low German swarm, Danish sværm "a swarm," Swedish svärm, Middle Dutch swerm, Old High German swaram, German Schwarm "swarm;" Old Norse svarmr "tumult"), by Watkins, etc., derived from PIE imitative root *swer- (2) "to buzz, whisper" (see susurration) on notion of humming sound, and thus probably originally of bees.

meanings.definitionCn: 预见， 预期； 先于…行动

etymology.story: 1530s, "to cause to happen sooner," a back-formation from anticipation, or else from Latin anticipatus, past participle of anticipare "take (care of) ahead of time," literally "taking into possession beforehand," from ante "before" (see ante) + capere "to take" (see capable).

meanings.definitionCn: 寺庙， 教堂； 太阳穴
砍痕， 伤痕； 斜线号

etymology.story: mid-15c., "to fashion, make, build," from Latin fabricatus, past participle of fabricare "to make, construct, fashion, build," from fabrica (see fabric).
1590s (implied in uprooted), in the figurative sense, from up (adv.) + root (v.).
mid-15c., "keep an account by tally," from Medieval Latin talliare "to tax," from tallia (see tally (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 风景，景色；地貌，地形；风景画

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 取/抓 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Anglo-French excepcioun, Old French excepcion, from Latin exceptionem (nominative exceptio) "an exception, restriction, limitation; an objection," noun of action from past participle stem of excipere "to take out" (see except).

meanings.definitionCn: 普遍的； 流行的； 一地常刮的， 盛行的

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + 强壮/价值

etymology.story: 1590s, "vigorous;" 1680s, "widely accepted," present participle adjective from prevail (v.)..

"lasting only for a time," 1540s, from Latin temporarius "of seasonal character, lasting a short time," from tempus (genitive temporis) "time, season" (see temporal, late 14c., which was the earlier word for "lasting but for a time").

"area or region where a plant or animal naturally grows or lives," 1762, originally a technical term in Latin texts on English flora and fauna, literally "it inhabits," third person singular present indicative of habitare "to live, inhabit, dwell," frequentative of habere "to have, to hold, possess" (see habit (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 眼/视 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: "most favorable," 1890, from Latin optimus (see optimum).

meanings.definitionCn: 绝望的； 不顾死活的， 拼命的； 极度渴望的

etymology.story: early 15c., "despairing, hopeless," from Latin desperatus "given up, despaired of," past participle of desperare (see despair (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 不舒服的； 糟糕的； 可怕的； 非常的
可用的； 易使用的， 便利的； 手巧的

morphology.mnemonic: to pass (something to someone) + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: early 14c., intransitive and reflexive; mid-14c., transitive, from Old French anguissier (Modern French angoisser), from anguisse (see anguish (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 打破 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1570s, from Late Latin refractionem (nominative refractio) "a breaking up," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin refringere "to break up," from re- "back" (see re-) + comb.

morphology.mnemonic: 看见 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., "pertaining to the faculty of sight;" also "coming from the eye or sight" (as a beam of light was thought to do), from Late Latin visualis "of sight," from Latin visus "a sight, a looking; power of sight; things seen, appearance," from visus, past participle of videre "to see" (see vision).

meanings.definitionCn: 禁猎区， 动物保护区； 庇护所， 避难所； 圣所， 圣殿

etymology.story: early 14c., "building set apart for holy worship," from Anglo-French sentuarie, Old French saintuaire "sacred relic, holy thing; reliquary, sanctuary," from Late Latin sanctuarium "a sacred place, shrine" (especially the Hebrew Holy of Holies; see sanctum), also "a private room," from Latin sanctus "holy" (see saint (n.)).
method of exercise and a fad in early 1980s, American English, coined 1968 by Kenneth H.

meanings.definitionCn: 动人的， 诱人的； 引人注目的

etymology.story: "attractive, alluring," c.

early 15c., "to tell, inform, impart knowledge or information," also "furnish with authoritative directions," from Latin instructus, past participle of instruere "arrange, prepare, set in order; inform, teach," literally "to build, erect," from in- "on" (see in- (2)) + struere "to pile, build" (see structure (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 使人愉快的； 欣然同意的

root.meaning: to be in harmony in opinions

morphology.mnemonic: to be in harmony in opinions + able

etymology.story: late 14c., "to one's liking," from Old French agreable (12c., Modern French agréable) "pleasing, in agreement, consenting, thankful," from agreer "to please" (see agree).

meanings.definitionCn: 社会； 社团； 群落； 共同体

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French comunité "community, commonness, everybody" (Modern French communauté), from Latin communitatem (nominative communitas) "community, society, fellowship, friendly intercourse; courtesy, condescension, affability," from communis "common, public, general, shared by all or many," (see common (adj.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 结果/过程/状态

meanings.definitionCn: 译文， 译本； 版本

etymology.story: 1580s, "a translation," from Middle French version, from Medieval Latin versionem (nominative versio) "a turning, a translation," from past participle stem of Latin vertere "to turn, change, alter, translate" (see versus).

meanings.definitionCn: 蒙受神恩的； 有超凡魅力的

etymology.story: 1851, in Bible commentary and theology, in reference to the operation of the Holy Spirit and prophetic ecstasy in the early Church (from the use of Greek kharismata in Rom.

meanings.definitionCn: 使超载， 使过载； 使超负荷； 给…增加负担

etymology.story: 1550s, "to place too great a burden on," from over- + load (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 青铜；青铜色；青铜制品；铜牌

etymology.story: 1640s, literally, 1726 figuratively, from French bronzer (16c.) or else from bronze (n.).
early 15c., "impervious to water," from Old French estanche "firm, watertight," fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 选择， 挑选； 精选； 被挑选出来的人， 精选品； 可供选择的事物

etymology.story: 1620s, "act of selecting," from Latin selectionem (nominative selectio) "a choosing out, choice, selection," noun of action from past participle stem of seligere (see select (adj.)).

"age following childhood" (especially the period from the 15th to the 21st year), early 15c., from Middle French adolescence (14c.), from Latin adolescentia "youth," noun of state from adolescentem (see adolescent (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 亚文化行为观念， 次文化

etymology.story: 1886, in reference to bacterial cultures, from sub- + culture (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 拨款； 占用， 挪用

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + propri + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: "specially suitable, proper," early 15c., from Latin appropriatus, past participle of appropriare (see appropriate (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 最小； 最少； 微不足道
最小的；最少的；最不重要的

etymology.story: Old English læst, earlier læsest "smallest, lowest in power or position" (superlative of little), from Proto-Germanic superlative *laisista-, from PIE root *leis- (2) "small" (see less).

meanings.definitionCn: 功用， 效用； 公用事业

etymology.story: late 14c., "fact of being useful," from Old French utilite "usefulness" (13c., Modern French + utilité), earlier utilitet (12c.), from Latin utilitatem (nominative utilitas) "usefulness, serviceableness, profit," from utilis "usable," from uti "make use of, profit by, take advantage of" (see use (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1650s, from Medieval Latin abrasionem (nominative abrasio) "a scraping," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin abradere "to scrape away, shave off," from ab- "off" (see ab-) + radere "to scrape" (see raze)..

meanings.definitionCn: 交响乐， 交响曲； 和谐， 协调

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/一起 + 声音 + 具有…特征/名词化
出/从 + 说话 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French eloquent, from Latin eloquentem (nominative eloquens) "speaking, having the faculty of speech; eloquent," present participle of eloqui "to speak out" (see eloquence).

meanings.definitionCn: 天文台， 天文观测站

root.meaning: to attend to in practice, to keep, follow

morphology.mnemonic: to attend to in practice, to keep, follow + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: "building for observing astronomical phenomena," 1670s (in reference to Greenwich), from French observatoire, from observer (v.); see observe..
1845, from optimist + -ic.
1580s, "one who brings forth a proposition or argument," from Latin proponentem (nominative proponens), present participle of proponere "put forward" (see propound).

meanings.definitionCn: 财力不足的， 破产的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 太阳/独自 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1590s, "unable to pay one's debts," from in- (1) "not" + Latin solventem "paying" (see solvent).

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之下 + collective shipping of a country

etymology.story: 1650s, in the medical sense, from empiric + -ism.

meanings.definitionCn: 光滑而有光泽的；线条流畅的；时髦的

etymology.story: "make sleek," mid-15c., a variant of slick (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 微粒， 颗粒； 【语】小品词

etymology.story: late 14c., "small part or division of a whole, minute portion of matter," from Latin particula "little bit or part, grain, jot," diminutive of pars (genitive partis) "part;" see part (n.).
late 14c., "one skilled in music," from Old French musicien (14c.), or a native formation from music + -ian.

meanings.definitionCn: 不景气的； 停滞的； 迟钝的； 因不流动而污浊的

etymology.story: 1660s, from French stagnant (early 17c.), from Latin stagnantem (nominative stagnans), present participle of stagnare "to stagnate" (see stagnate).

meanings.definitionCn: 感知的， 知觉的； 有洞察力的， 理解力强的

morphology.mnemonic: 通过/彻底 + 取/抓 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1650s, from Latin percept-, past participle stem of percipere (see perceive) + -ive.

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + notice, attention, reputation + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin annotationem (nominative annotatio), noun of action from past participle stem of annotare "to add notes to," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + notare "to note, mark" (see note (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 将…减到最少， 使最小化； 降低， 贬低

etymology.story: 1802, first recorded in Bentham; see minimum + -ize..

meanings.definitionCn: 打扫；席卷，横扫；挥动，舞动；迅速传播；
遗传； 下降； 起源； 降临， 来临
机械学， 力学； 技巧， 方法

etymology.story: 1640s, based on Late Latin mechanica, from Greek mekhanike, mekhanika (see mechanic (adj.)); also see -ics..
1973, contraction of magnetic levitation..

meanings.definitionCn: 逻辑的； 符合逻辑的， 合理的； 有逻辑头脑的

morphology.mnemonic: logical argumentation + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., "based on reason," from logic + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 以…为特征； 刻画； 描述

root.meaning: sum of qualities that define a person

morphology.mnemonic: sum of qualities that define a person + ize

etymology.story: 1590s, "to engrave, write," back-formation from characterization, or else from Medieval Latin characterizare, from Greek kharakterizein "to designate by a characteristic mark," from kharakter (see character).

meanings.definitionCn: 血统， 世系； 下降
特技表演； 噱头； 愚蠢行为

etymology.story: "feat to attract attention," 1878, American English college sports slang, of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 笑剧， 滑稽剧； 闹剧

etymology.story:
late 14c., "force-meat, stuffing;" 1520s, in the dramatic sense "ludicrous satire; low comedy," from Middle French farce "comic interlude in a mystery play" (16c.), literally "stuffing," from Old French farcir "to stuff," (13c.), from Latin farcire "to stuff, cram," which is of uncertain origin, perhaps from PIE *bhrekw- "to cram together," and thus related to frequens "crowded."

According to OED and other sources, the pseudo-Latin farsia was applied 13c.
1540s, ridyculouse, from Latin ridiculus "laughable, funny, absurd," from ridere "to laugh" (see risible).

meanings.definitionCn: 侵扰， 干扰； 侵犯； 侵入， 闯入

morphology.mnemonic: in + 推/挤 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "unjust invasion of property or usurpation of office," from Old French intrusion (14c.), from Medieval Latin intrusionem (nominative intrusio) "a thrusting in," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin intrudere "to thrust in, force in," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + trudere "to thrust, push," from PIE *treud- "to press, push, squeeze" (see threat).

1510s, "refuse, reject," from Middle French réfuter (16c.) and directly from Latin refutare "drive back; rebut, disprove; repress, repel, resist, oppose," from re- "back" (see re-) + -futare "to beat," probably from PIE root *bhau- "to strike down" (see bat (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 严重的； 严厉的； 剧烈的； 严峻的， 艰巨的； 朴素的， 不加装饰的

etymology.story: 1540s, from Middle French severe (12c., Modern French sévère) or directly from Latin severus "serious, grave, strict, austere" (see severity).

meanings.definitionCn: 是…的典型； 例示， 举例证明

morphology.mnemonic: that which is taken out, + 使…化/造成

etymology.story: early 15c., "to illustrate by examples, to instruct by (good) example," from Medieval Latin exemplificare "to illustrate," from Latin exemplum "example, pattern, model" (see example).

meanings.definitionCn: 显露， 暴露； 出来， 现出

etymology.story: 1560s, from Middle French émerger and directly from Latin emergere "bring forth, bring to light," intransitively "arise out or up, come forth, come up, come out, rise," from assimilated form of ex- "out" (see ex-) + mergere "to dip, sink" (see merge).

late 14c., "legal contract conveying property, usually for a fixed period of time and with a fixed compensation," from Anglo-French les (late 13c.), Old French lais, lez "a lease, a letting, a leaving," verbal noun from Old French laissier "to let, allow, permit; bequeath, leave" (see lease (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 开发； 利用； 剥削
英勇行为， 业绩， 功绩

etymology.story: late 13c., "strenuous, ardent, fierce, angry," from Old French aigre "sour, acid; harsh, bitter, rough; eager greedy; lively, active, forceful," from Latin acrem (nominative acer) "keen, sharp, pointed, piercing; acute, ardent, zealous" (see acrid).

meanings.definitionCn: 证据； 迹象； 根据

etymology.story: Old English ærende "message, mission; answer, news, tidings," a common Germanic word (cognates: Old Saxon arundi, Old Norse erendi, Danish ærende, Swedish ärende, Old Frisian erende, Old High German arunti "message"), which is of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 艳丽的， 绚丽夺目的； 炫耀的

etymology.story: 1832, originally in reference to a 15c.-16c.

meanings.definitionCn: 致命的； 破坏性极大的， 极其有害的

etymology.story: 1580s, from Late Latin lethalis, alteration of Latin letalis "deadly, fatal," from letum "death," of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 重复， 反复； 重复的事

etymology.story: early 15c., "act of saying over again," from Old French repetition and directly from Latin repetitionem (nominative repetitio) "a repeating," noun of action from past participle stem of repetere "do or say again" (see repeat (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 一夫多妻的， 一妻多夫的

morphology.mnemonic: 多 + 婚姻 + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: 1610s, from polygamy + -ous, or else from Late Greek polygamos "often married."

meanings.definitionCn: 担心， 焦虑； 悬念， 不确定

etymology.story: Old English gimstan; see gem + stone (n.)..

1748, back-formation from narration or else from Latin narratus, past participle of narrare "to tell, relate, recount." "Richardson and Johnson call it Scottish" [OED], a stigma which kept it from general use until 19c.

meanings.definitionCn: 跳跃；涌现，突然出现；突然提出
泉； 弹簧， 发条； 春天； 跳， 跃

etymology.story: Old English springan "to leap, burst forth, fly up; spread, grow," (class III strong verb; past tense sprang, past participle sprungen), from Proto-Germanic *sprengan (source also of Old Norse, Old Frisian springa, Middle Dutch springhen, Dutch

meanings.definitionCn: 自治， 自治权； 自主， 自主权

morphology.mnemonic: 自我/自动 + name名 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1620s, of states, from Greek autonomia "independence," noun of quality from autonomos "independent, living by one's own laws," from auto- "self" (see auto-) + nomos "custom, law" (see numismatics).

meanings.definitionCn: 健康， 健壮； 适合

etymology.story: 1570s, "state or quality of being suitable," from fit (adj.) + -ness.

Old English screadian "to peel, prune, cut off," from Proto-Germanic *skrauth- (source also of Middle Dutch scroden, Dutch schroeien, Old High German scrotan, German schroten "to shred"), from root of shred (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 点燃， 开始燃烧； 激起
投降； 屈服， 屈从； 放弃

etymology.story: early 15c., in law, "a giving up" (of an estate, land grant, interest in property, etc.), from Anglo-French surrendre, Old French surrendre noun use of infinitive, "give up, deliver over" (see surrender (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 模糊的，不清楚的；无力的，虚弱的；眩晕的

etymology.story: Old English elm, from Proto-Germanic *elmaz (source also of Danish elm, Old Norse almr, Old High German elme), perhaps from PIE root *el- (2) "red, brown" (see elk); cognate with Latin ulmus, Old Irish lem.

meanings.definitionCn: 阴凉处，遮光物；阴暗；色度；差别
碰撞， 互撞； 冲突， 抵触

etymology.story: 1620s, from Latin collidere "strike together," from com- "together" (see com-) + laedere "to strike, injure by striking," which is of unknown origin.

"chief, pivotal," early 14c., from Latin cardinalis "principal, chief, essential," from cardo (genitive cardinis) "that on which something turns or depends; pole of the sky," originally "door hinge," which is of unknown origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 坚持要求； 坚决主张

etymology.story: 1580s, from French insister (14c.) or directly from Latin insistere "take a stand, stand on, stand still; follow, pursue; insist, press vigorously, urge, dwell upon," from in- "upon" (see in- (2)) + sistere "take a stand" (see assist (v.)).
1640s in reference to trees and shrubs, from ever + green (adj.).

meanings.definitionCn: 交错的， 错开的； 震惊的
体积， 容积， 容量； 音量； 卷， 册； 书

etymology.story: late 14c., "roll of parchment containing writing; a bound book," from Old French volume "scroll, book; work, volume; girth, size" (13c.) and directly from Latin volumen (genitive voluminis) "roll (of a manuscript); coil, wreath," literally "that which is rolled," from volvere "to turn around, roll" (see volvox).

meanings.definitionCn: 交易； 执行， 处理

etymology.story: 1580s, back-formation from transaction, or else from Latin transactus, past participle of transigere "to drive through, accomplish, bring to an end, settle."

meanings.definitionCn: 催化剂， 触媒； 促使变化的人

etymology.story: "substance which speeds a chemical reaction but itself remains unchanged," 1902, formed in English (on analogy of analyst) from catalysis.
1785, "line marked on a surface before cutting," from guide + line (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 松散的， 宽松的； 不精确的， 不严密的； 不牢固的； 自由的， 散漫的
令人厌恶的， 讨厌的

etymology.story: Old English flint "flint; a type of rock noted for hardness and for giving off sparks when struck," common Germanic (cognates Middle Dutch vlint, Old High German flins, Danish flint), from PIE *splind- "to split, cleave," from root *(s)plei- "to splice, split" (source also of Greek plinthos "brick, tile," Old Irish slind "brick"), perhaps a variant of *spel- (1) "to split, break off." Transferred senses (hardness, etc.) were in Old English..

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + dign + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1580s, from Latin indignantem (nominative indignans) "impatient, reluctant, indignant," present participle of indignari "to be displeased at, be offended, resent, deem unworthy" (see indignation).

meanings.definitionCn: 几乎不， 简直不； 刚刚， 仅仅

root.meaning: with effort or energy, with difficulty

morphology.mnemonic: with effort or energy, with difficulty + 以…方式/副词化

etymology.story: "continuing in time," late Old English, present participle adjective from last (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 愤世嫉俗的， 冷嘲热讽的

morphology.mnemonic: sneering sarcastic person + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1580s, "resembling Cynic philosophers," from cynic + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 技术的， 工艺的； 专业的

morphology.mnemonic: 技术 + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1610s, "skilled in a particular art or subject," formed in English from technic + -al (1), or in part from Greek tekhnikos "of art; systematic," in reference to persons "skillful, artistic," from tekhne "art, skill, craft" (see techno-).

meanings.definitionCn: 最外面的， 离中心最远的

etymology.story: 1580s, from outer + -most..

meanings.definitionCn: 达到顶点； 告终； 【天】到中天

etymology.story: 1640s, from Late Latin culminatus past participle of culminare "to top, to crown," from Latin culmen (genitive culminis) "top, peak, summit, roof, gable," also used figuratively, contraction of columen (see column).

meanings.definitionCn: 折磨人的， 令人痛苦的； 曲折的， 蜿蜒的； 含混不清的， 拐弯抹角的

etymology.story: late 14c., "full of twists and turns," from Anglo-French tortuous (12c.), Old French tortuos, from Latin tortuosus "full of twists, winding," from tortus "a twisting, winding," from stem of torquere "to twist, wring, distort" (see torque (n.)).

late 14c., from Old French paucité (14c.) and directly from Latin paucitatem (nominative paucitas) "fewness, scarcity, a small number," from paucus "few, little," from PIE *pau-ko-, from root *pau- (1) "few, little" (source also of Latin paullus "little;" Old English feawe "few;" see few (adj.))..

Old English streccan (transitive and intransitive) "to stretch, spread out, prostrate; reach, extend" (past tense strehte, past participle streht), from Proto-Germanic *strakjanan (source also of Danish strække, Swedish sträcka, Old Frisian strekka, Old High German strecchan, Middle Low German, Middle Dutch, Old High German, German strecken "to stretch, draw out"), perhaps a variant of the root of stark, or else from PIE root *strenk- "tight, narrow; pull tight, twist" (see string (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 达到； 完成； 实现

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French acompliss-, present participle stem of acomplir "to fulfill, fill up, complete" (12c.), from Vulgar Latin *accomplere, from Latin ad- "to" (see ad-) + complere "fill up" (see complete (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 有宪法制定或修改权的； 组成的， 构成的

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 建立/放置 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1620s, "one who appoints or elects a representative," from Latin constituentem (nominative constituens), present participle of constituere (see constitute).

morphology.mnemonic: in + 伸展/趋向 + 状态/行为/过程 + 与…有关的/形容词 + 以…方式/副词化

etymology.story: "on purpose," 1660s; see intentional + -ly (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 激怒， 使烦躁； 刺激

morphology.mnemonic: 激怒/刺激 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1530s, "stimulate to action, rouse, incite," from Latin irritatus, past participle of irritare "excite, provoke, annoy;" according to de Vaan, probably a verb from Proto-Italic *rito- "stirred," from the same PIE root that produced English run (v.).
early 15c., from the expression cry havoc "give the signal to pillage" (Anglo-French crier havok, late 14c.).

meanings.definitionCn: 有责任感的， 负责的； 需负责任的； 责任重大的， 重要的

etymology.story: 1590s, "answerable" (to another, for something), from obsolete French responsible (13c., Modern French responsable, as if from Latin *responsabilis), from Latin respons-, past participle stem of respondere "to respond" (see respond).
early 15c., from Anglo-French vagarant, waucrant, and sharing with it the history to be found under vagrant (n.).
"cloth from woven flax," early 14c.; earlier as an adjective, "made of flax" (c.

1540s, "follower or attendant of a superior person," from Middle French satellite (14c.), from Latin satellitem (nominative satelles) "attendant, companion, courtier, accomplice, assistant," perhaps from Etruscan satnal (Klein), or a compound of roots *satro- "full, enough" + *leit- (1) "to go" (Tucker); compare English follow, which is constructed of similar roots.

meanings.definitionCn: 照亮； 启发， 启迪； 阐明， 说明

etymology.story: "an animal resembling a serpent, with legs added to it" [Johnson], late 14c., lusarde, from Anglo-French lusard, Old French laisarde "lizard" (Modern French lézard), from Latin lacertus (fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 精神错乱， 神志失常

etymology.story: 1590s, from Latin delirium "madness," from deliriare "be crazy, rave," literally "go off the furrow," a plowing metaphor, from phrase de lire, from de "off, away" (see de-) + lira "furrow, earth thrown up between two furrows," from PIE *leis- (1) "track, furrow" (see learn)..

meanings.definitionCn: 主要考虑因素； 全神贯注

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + oc + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1550s, "state of occupying beforehand," from Latin praeoccupationem (nominative praeoccupatio) "a seizing beforehand, anticipation," noun of action from past participle stem of praeoccupare, from prae- "before" (see pre-) + occupare "seize" (see occupy).

1610s, "to thrust forward or onward, to drive along;" 1640s, "to cause to stick out," from Latin protrudere "thrust forward; push out," from pro- "forward" (see pro-) + trudere "to thrust, push," from PIE *treud- "to press, push, squeeze" (see threat).

mid-14c., from Old French residu (14c.), from Latin residuum "a remainder, that which is left behind," noun use of neuter of adjective residuus "remaining, left over," from residere "remain behind" (see reside)..

meanings.definitionCn: 狂暴， 狂热， 疯狂

etymology.story: mid-15c., "place of assembly in ancient Rome," from Latin forum "marketplace, open space, public place," apparently akin to foris, foras "out of doors, outside," from PIE root *dhwer- "door, doorway" (see door).

meanings.definitionCn: 轻视， 鄙视， 轻蔑

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin contemptus "scorn," from past participle of contemnere "to scorn, despise," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + *temnere "to slight, scorn," which is of uncertain origin.

late 14c., orisoun, from Old French orizon (14c., Modern French horizon), earlier orizonte (13c.), from Latin horizontem (nominative horizon), from Greek horizon (kyklos) "bounding (circle)," from horizein "bound, limit, divide, separate," from horos "boundary, landmark, marking stones." The h- was restored in English 17c.

meanings.definitionCn: 任务， 项目； 事业， 企业； 承诺， 保证

etymology.story: "enterprise," early 15c., verbal noun from undertake (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 比喻的， 借喻的， 象征的； 形象的

morphology.mnemonic: to shape into + 使…/具有…的 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: late 14c., "emblematical," from Old French figuratif "metaphorical," from Late Latin figurativus "figurative" (of speech), from figurat-, past participle stem of Latin figurare "to form, shape," from figura "a shape, form, figure" (see figure (n.)).

Old English swat "perspiration, moisture exuded from the skin," also "labor, that which causes sweat," from Proto-Germanic *swaitaz "sweat" (source also of Old Saxon, Old Frisian swet, Old Norse sveiti, Danish sved "sweat," Swedish svett, Middle Dutch sweet, Dutch zweet, Old High German sweiz, German Schweiß), from PIE *sweid- (2) "to sweat" (source also of Sanskrit svedah "sweat," Avestan xvaeda- "sweat," Greek hidros "sweat, perspiration," Latin sudor, Lettish swiedri, Welsh chwys "sweat").

meanings.definitionCn: 进步的； 逐步的， 渐进的； 【语】进行时的

morphology.mnemonic: 向前/支持 + 步/走 + 有…倾向的

meanings.definitionCn: 出卖， 泄露； 辜负， 对…不忠； 流露情感

etymology.story: late 13c., bitrayen "mislead, deceive, betray," from be- + obsolete Middle English tray, from Old French traine "betrayal, deception, deceit," from trair (Modern French trahir) "betray, deceive," from Latin tradere "hand over," from trans- "across" (see trans-) + dare "to give" (see date (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 贡献的； 捐助的； 促进的

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 给予/分配 + …者/…器 + 具有…特征/名词化

meanings.definitionCn: 故意的， 蓄意的； 审慎的， 深思熟虑的

etymology.story: 1540s, from Latin deliberatus, past participle of deliberare (see deliberation).

meanings.definitionCn: 诅咒； 宣誓， 发誓

etymology.story: Old English swerian "take an oath" (class VI strong verb; past tense swor, past participle sworen), from Proto-Germanic *swarjan-, (source also of Old Saxon swerian, Old Frisian swera, Old Norse sverja, Danish sverge, Middle Dutch swaren, Old High German swerien, German schwören, Gothic swaren "to swear"), from PIE root *swer- (1) "to speak, talk, say" (source also of Old Church Slavonic svara "quarrel," Oscan sverrunei "to the speaker").

meanings.definitionCn: 大气， 大气层； 空气； 气氛， 氛围

etymology.story: 1630s, atmosphaera (modern form from 1670s), from Modern Latin atmosphaera, from atmo-, comb.

early 15c., from Latin decoratus, past participle of decorare "to decorate, adorn, embellish, beautify," from decus (genitive decoris) "an ornament," from PIE root *dek- "to receive, be suitable" (see decent).

meanings.definitionCn: 生涯， 经历； 职业
松弛， 放松； 减缓， 放慢

root.meaning: be remiss, inactive or idle, fail to exert oneself

morphology.mnemonic: be remiss, inactive or idle, fail to exert oneself + 使变成/变得

etymology.story: early 15c., from slack (adj.) + -en (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 释放； 发布， 公开； 发泄

etymology.story: early 14c., "abatement of distress; means of deliverance," from Old French relais, reles (12c.), a back-formation from relesser, relaissier (see release (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 特许设立，给予特权；包租
纲领， 宣言； 宪章； 特许状

etymology.story: late 14c., "enduring," present participle adjective from abide (v.)..
1620s, from Latin liberatus, past participle of liberare "set free," from liber "free" (see liberal).

meanings.definitionCn: 道德的，道义上的；有道德的，品行端正的；精神的
有志于； 热望， 向往
爆炸； 激增； 冲动， 发怒

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 爆发/鼓掌

etymology.story: 1530s (transitive), "to reject with scorn," from Latin explodere "drive out or off by clapping, hiss off, hoot off," originally theatrical, "to drive an actor off the stage by making noise," hence "drive out, reject, destroy the repute of" (a sense surviving in an exploded theory), from ex- "out" (see ex-) + plaudere "to clap the hands, applaud," which is of uncertain origin.
"eruption" (of disease, hostilities, etc.), c.

meanings.definitionCn: 操作， 处理； 利用， 操纵

etymology.story: 1827, "to handle skillfully by hand," a back-formation from manipulation.

morphology.mnemonic: 相似/相同 + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story:
Old English scapan, past participle of scieppan "to create, form, destine" (past tense scop), from Proto-Germanic *skapjanan "create, ordain" (source also of Old Norse skapa, Danish skabe, Old Saxon scapan, Old Frisian skeppa, Middle Dutch schappen "do, treat," Old High German scaffan, German schaffen "shape, create, produce"), from PIE root *(s)kep-, forming words meaning "to cut, scrape, hack" (see scabies), which acquired broad technical senses and in Germanic a specific sense of "to create."

Old English scieppan survived into Middle English as shippen, but shape emerged as a regular verb (with past tense shaped) by 1500s.

General figurative sense of "recognition of claims to respect or duty" is attested from 1732..
representing the casual pronunciation of want to, by 1896..

1706, "register of deaths," from Medieval Latin obituarius "a record of the death of a person," literally "pertaining to death," from Latin obitus "departure, a going to meet, encounter" (a euphemism for "death"), from stem of obire "go toward, go to meet" (as in mortem obire "meet death"), from ob "to, toward" (see ob-) + ire "to go" (see ion).

etymology.story: 1690s, "desk with drawers, writing desk," from French bureau "office; desk, writing table," originally "cloth covering for a desk," from burel "coarse woolen cloth" (as a cover for writing desks), Old French diminutive of bure "dark brown cloth," which is perhaps either from Latin burrus "red," or from Late Latin burra "wool, shaggy garment." Offices being full of such desks, the meaning expanded 1720 to "division of a government." Meaning "chest of drawers" is from 1770, said to be American English but early in British use..

late 14c., "moist, wet; well-irrigated," from Old French moiste "damp, wet, soaked" (13c., Modern French moite), from Vulgar Latin *muscidus "moldy," also "wet," from Latin mucidus "slimy, moldy, musty," from mucus "slime" (see mucus).

late 14c., "respectable," from thrift + -y (2).
Old English gielp "boasting, pride, arrogance," from source of yelp (v.).

"newspapers, radio, TV, etc." 1927, perhaps abstracted from mass media (1923, a technical term in advertising), plural of medium, on notion of "intermediate agency," a sense found in that word in English from c.

1620s, short for plaudite "an actor's request for applause" (1560s), from Latin plaudite! "applaud!" second person plural imperative of plaudere "to clap, strike, beat; applaud, approve," of unknown origin (also in applaud, explode).

1898, coined by its discoverers, Sir William Ramsay and Morris W.
"ship's cargo," 1706; see manifest (adj.).

"silicon dioxide," 1756, from German Quarz, Zwarc "rock crystal," from Middle High German twarc, probably from a West Slavic source, compare Czech tvrdy, Polish twardy "quartz," noun uses of an adjective meaning "hard," from Old Church Slavonic tvrudu "hard," from Proto-Slavic *tvrd-, from PIE *(s)twer- "to grasp, hold; hard.".

1560s, from Middle French prélude "notes sung or played to test the voice or instrument" (1530s), from Medieval Latin preludium "prelude, preliminary," from Latin praeludere "to play beforehand for practice, preface," from prae- "before" (see pre-) + ludere "to play" (see ludicrous).

early 15c., from Latin adiacentem (nominative adiacens) "lying at," present participle of adiacere "lie at, border upon, lie near," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + iacere "to lie, rest," literally "to throw" (see jet (v.)), with notion of "to cast (oneself) down.".

"ill-humor," 1727, in humps and grumps "surly remarks," later the grumps "a fit of ill-humor" (1844), then "a person in ill humor" (1900); perhaps an extended sense of grum "morose, surly," which probably is related to Danish grum "cruel;" or perhaps suggested by grumble, grunt, etc..
