etymology.story: Old English *beorhtnian "to make bright" (see bright (adj.) + -en (1)).

12c., a merger of Old English ora "ore, unworked metal" (related to eorþe "earth," see earth; and cognate with Low German ur "iron-containing ore," Dutch oer, Old Norse aurr "gravel"); and Old English ar "brass, copper, bronze," from Proto-Germanic *ajiz- (source also of Old Norse eir "brass, copper," German ehern "brazen," Gothic aiz "bronze"), from PIE root *aus- (2) "gold" (see aureate).
1620s, from Latin praevius "going before," from prae "before" (see pre-) + via "road" (see via).

meanings.definitionCn: 争论， 辩论； 主张， 论证； 说服， 劝说
笨重的； 麻烦的； 累赘的， 冗长的

etymology.story: late 14c., from cumber (v.) + -some (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 给人以错觉； 掩饰； 证明…不正确

etymology.story: Old English beleogan "to deceive by lies," from be- + lie (v.1) "to lie, tell lies." Current sense of "to contradict as a lie" is first recorded 1640s.

meanings.definitionCn: 早熟的；过早的；不成熟的，仓促的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin praematurus "early ripe" (as fruit), "too early, untimely," from prae "before" (see pre-) + maturus "ripe, timely" (see mature (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 较高的；更好的；有优越感的，高傲的；超群的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin superior (see superior (adj.)), used in Medieval Latin with a noun sense of "one higher, a superior.".

meanings.definitionCn: 补偿， 赔偿； 赔偿金

morphology.mnemonic: to recompense, remunerate + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "action of compensating," from Latin compensationem (nominative compensatio) "a weighing one thing against another, a balancing," noun of action from past participle stem of compensare (see compensate).

meanings.definitionCn: 附件，零件；配饰；从犯，同谋

etymology.story: 1550s, "subordinate," from Late Latin accessorius, from accessor, agent noun from accedere "to approach" (see accede).
1887, "special group-study class for advanced students," from German Seminar "group of students working with a professor," from Latin seminarium "breeding ground, plant nursery" (see seminary).

meanings.definitionCn: 自然主义者； 博物学家

morphology.mnemonic: birth, + 与…有关的/形容词 + …主义者/…专家

etymology.story: "student of plants and animals," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 毒液； 恶意， 怨恨

etymology.story: mid-13c., venim, venym, "poison secreted by some animals and transferred by biting," from Anglo-French and Old French venim, venin "poison; malice," from Vulgar Latin *venimen (source also of Italian veleno, Spanish veneno), from Latin venenum "poison," earlier (pre-classical) "drug, medical potion," also "charm, seduction," probably originally "love potion," from PIE *wenes-no-, from root *wen- (1) "to strive after, wish, desire" (see Venus).

meanings.definitionCn: 尝试着做； 冒…风险， 使处于危险

etymology.story: "put something at stake in a game of chance," 1520s, from Middle French hasarder "to play at gambling, throw dice" (15c.), from hasard (see hazard (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 情节剧， 通俗剧； 戏剧性的事件

etymology.story: 1784 (1782 as melo drame), "a stage-play in which songs were interspersed and music accompanied the action," from French mélodrame (18c.), from Greek melos "song" (see melody) + French drame "drama" (see drama).

meanings.definitionCn: 植物的； 有生长力的

etymology.story: late 14c., "endowed with the power of growth," from Old French vegetatif "(naturally) growing," from Medieval Latin vegetativus, from vegetat-, past participle stem of vegetare (see vegetable (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 证据，证明；证词；检验，证实；校样
分阶段实行， 逐步做
冲淡， 稀释； 淡化， 削弱

etymology.story: 1550s, from Latin dilutus, past participle of diluere "dissolve, wash away, dilute," from dis- "apart" + -luere, comb.

meanings.definitionCn: 方法， 办法， 措施； 秩序

etymology.story: early 15c., "regular, systematic treatment of disease," from Latin methodus "way of teaching or going," from Greek methodos "scientific inquiry, method of inquiry, investigation," originally "pursuit, a following after," from meta- "after" (see meta-) + hodos "a traveling, way" (see cede).

meanings.definitionCn: 订合同； 收缩； 感染， 染上

etymology.story: early 14c., from Old French contract (Modern French contrat), from Latin contractus "a contract, agreement," from past participle of contrahere "to draw together," metaphorically, "to make a bargain," from com- "together" (see com-) + trahere "to draw" (see tract (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 卷轴； 纸卷， 画卷； 名册
行动迟缓的； 不活泼的； 无精打采的， 怠惰的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Middle English slugge "lazy person" (see sluggard) + -ish.
Old English geolca, geoloca "yolk," literally "the yellow part," from geolu "yellow" (see yellow (adj.)).

1670s, in anatomy, "pertaining to conveyance or circulation of fluids," from Modern Latin vascularis "of or pertaining to vessels or tubes," from Latin vasculum "a small vessel," diminutive of vas "vessel.".

meanings.definitionCn: 易受感染的； 易受影响的； 过敏的； 感情丰富的； 能经受…的， 容许…的
革新的， 创新的； 富有革新精神的

root.meaning: bring in new things, alter established practices

morphology.mnemonic: bring in new things, alter established practices + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1796 (with an isolated use from c.

meanings.definitionCn: 静止的， 寂静的； 静态的
抨击， 指责； 猛攻； 困扰
布料， 织品； 构造， 结构

etymology.story: late 15c., "building; thing made; a structure of any kind," from Middle French fabrique (14c.), verbal noun from fabriquer (13c.), from Latin fabricare "to make, construct, fashion, build," from fabrica "workshop," also "an art, trade; a skillful production, structure, fabric," from faber "artisan who works in hard materials," from Proto-Italic *fafro-, from PIE *dhabh- "to fit together" (source also of Armenian darbin "smith;" also see daft).

meanings.definitionCn: 精确的，准确的；严谨的，严格的；

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin exactus, past participle of exigere "require, enforce, demand, collect (money);" see exact (adj.).

meanings.definitionCn: 百分度的； 摄氏温度的

root.meaning: to reduce (a road, etc.) to a level or degree of inclination

morphology.mnemonic: centi + to reduce (a road, etc.) to a level or degree of inclination

etymology.story: 1799, from French; see centi- "hundred" + Latin gradus "degree" (see grade (n.))..
1816, "pertaining to the brain," from French cérébral (16c.), from Latin cerebrum "the brain" (also "the understanding"), from PIE *keres-, from root *ker- (1) "top of the head" (see horn (n.)).

root.meaning: dot marking end of a sentence

morphology.mnemonic: dot marking end of a sentence + ic

etymology.story: 1640s, from French périodique (14c.), from Latin periodicus, from periodus (see period).

morphology.mnemonic: 震动/地震 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1852, from seismo- + -logy.

meanings.definitionCn: 宝石， 珍品； 美丽绝伦的事物
久坐的， 固定不动的； 土生的， 不迁徙的； 沉积的

etymology.story: 1590s, "remaining in one place," from Middle French sédentaire (16c.) and directly from Latin sedentarius "sitting, remaining in one place," from sedentem (nominative sedens), present participle of sedere "to sit; occupy an official seat, preside; sit still, remain; be fixed or settled," from PIE root *sed- (1) "to sit" (source also of Sanskrit a-sadat "sat down," sidati "sits;" Old Persian hadis "abode;" Greek ezesthai "to sit," hedra "seat, chair, face of a geometric solid;" Old Irish suide "seat, sitting;" Welsh sedd "seat," eistedd "sitting;" Old Church Slavonic sežda, sedeti "to sit;" Lithuanian sedmi "to sit;" Russian sad "garden," Lithuanian soditi "to plant;" Gothic sitan, Old English sittan "to sit;" see sit).

meanings.definitionCn: 能力； 容量； 职责， 职位； 生产力

morphology.mnemonic: 取/抓 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French capacité "ability to hold" (15c.), from Latin capacitatem (nominative capacitas) "breadth, capacity, capability of holding much," noun of state from capax (genitive capacis) "able to hold much," from capere "to take" (see capable).

meanings.definitionCn: 大麻； 由大麻制成的麻醉药

etymology.story: Old English hænep "hemp, cannabis sativa," from Proto-Germanic *hanapiz (source also of Old Saxon hanap, Old Norse hampr, Old High German hanaf, German Hanf), probably a very early Germanic borrowing of the same Scythian word that became Greek kannabis (see cannabis).

late 13c., from Old French contrefait "imitated" (Modern French contrefait), past participle of contrefaire "imitate," from contre- "against" (see contra-) + faire "to make, to do" (from Latin facere; see factitious).

meanings.definitionCn: 课程， 教程； 过程， 进程； 路线； 一道菜
准确的， 精确的； 严谨的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Middle French précis "condensed, cut short" (14c.) and directly from Medieval Latin precisus, from Latin praecisus "abrupt, abridged, cut off," past participle of praecidere "to cut off, shorten," from prae "before" (see pre-) + caedere "to cut" (see -cide; for Latin vowel change, see acquisition).

meanings.definitionCn: 可察觉的， 可感知的； 明智的； 切合实际的； 意识到的

root.meaning: be conscious inwardly of (one's state or condition) is from 1680s. Meaning

morphology.mnemonic: be conscious inwardly of (one's state or condition) is from 1680s. Meaning  + able

etymology.story: late 14c., "capable of sensation or feeling;" also "capable of being sensed or felt, perceptible to the senses," hence "easily understood; logical, reasonable," from Late Latin sensibilis "having feeling: perceptible by the senses," from sensus, past participle of sentire "perceive, feel" (see sense (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 道歉， 认错； 辩解

etymology.story: 1590s, "to speak in defense of;" see apology + -ize.

mid-15c., "to bring together," frequentative of Middle English myngen "to mix," from Old English mengan (related to second element in among), from Proto-Germanic *mangjan "to knead together" (source also of Old Saxon mengian, Old Norse menga, Old Frisian mendza, German mengen), from PIE *mag- "to knead, fashion, fit" (see macerate).
in part from stem found in Old English gebrægd "craft, fraud," gebregd "commotion," Old Norse bragð "deed, trick," and in part from or influenced by related braid (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 澄清； 解体， 瓦解； 解开

morphology.mnemonic: 不/非/否定 + temporary popular enthusiasm

etymology.story:
1590s, from Late Latin edibilis "eatable," from Latin edere "to eat," from PIE root *ed- "to eat" (source also of Sanskrit admi "I eat;" Greek edo "I eat;" Lithuanian edu "I eat;" Hittite edmi "I eat," adanna "food;" Old Irish ithim "I eat;" Gothic itan, Old Swedish and Old English etan, Old High German essan "to eat;" 
 Avestan ad- "to eat;" Armenian utem "I eat;" Old Church Slavonic jasti "to eat," Russian jest "to eat")..

meanings.definitionCn: 合唱曲；合唱队；副歌，叠句；齐声，

etymology.story: 1560s, from Latin chorus "a dance in a circle, the persons singing and dancing, the chorus of a tragedy," from Greek khoros "band of dancers or singers, dance, dancing ground," perhaps from PIE *gher- "to grasp, enclose," if the original sense of the Greek word is "enclosed dancing floor." Extension from dance to voice is because Attic drama arose from tales inserted in the intervals of the dance.

meanings.definitionCn: 平卧的， 俯卧的； 易于做…的， 倾向于…的
资产， 财产； 有价值的人； 长处， 优点
展览会， 博览会； 阐释

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 放置 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "explanation, narration," from Old French esposicion "explanation, interpretation" (12c.), from Latin expositionem (nominative expositio) "a setting or showing forth; narration, explanation," noun of action from past participle stem of exponere "put forth; explain; expose" (see expound).

meanings.definitionCn: 日期，日子；时期，年代；约会；
暴露， 揭露； 展现

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French reveler "reveal" (14c.), from Latin revelare "reveal, uncover, disclose," literally "unveil," from re- "opposite of" (see re-) + velare "to cover, veil," from velum "a veil" (see veil (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 库房， 仓库； 公共汽车站， 火车站

etymology.story: 1795, "warehouse," from French dépôt "a deposit, place of deposit," from Old French depost "a deposit or pledge," from Latin depositum "a deposit," noun use of neuter past participle of deponere "lay aside" (see deposit (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 不切实际的； 理论的

morphology.mnemonic: 神 + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1610s, "contemplative," with -al (1) + Late Latin theoreticus "of or pertaining to theory," from Greek theoretikos "contemplative, speculative, pertaining to theory" (by Aristotle contrasted to praktikos), from theoretos "that may be seen or considered," from theorein "to consider, look at" (see theory).

Old English ule "owl," from Proto-Germanic *uwwalon- (source also of Middle Dutch, Dutch uil, Old High German uwila, German Eule, Old Norse ugla), a diminutive of PIE root *u(wa)l-, which is imitative of a wail or an owl's hoot (compare Latin ulula "owl;" also see ululation).

meanings.definitionCn: 可靠的；健康的，完好无损的；合理的

etymology.story: "free from special defect or injury," c.

Old English herebeorgian "take up quarters, lodge, shelter oneself" (cognate with Old Norse herbergja, Old High German heribergon, Middle Dutch herbergen), verbal formation from here-beorg "lodgings, quarters" (see harbor (n.)).

1610s (intransitive) "move to and fro;" 1660s, "swing to and fro;" from Latin vibratus, past participle of vibrare "set in tremulous motion, move quickly to and fro, quiver, tremble, shake," from PIE *wib-ro-, from root *weip- "to turn, vacillate, tremble ecstatically, move quickly to and fro" (source also of Lithuanian wyburiu "to wag" (the tail), Danish vippe, Dutch wippen "to swing," Old English wipan "to wipe").

meanings.definitionCn: 宜人的； 好客的， 殷勤的； 开通的

etymology.story: "kind and cordial to strangers or guests," 1560s, from Middle French hospitable, which is formed as if from a Medieval Latin hospitabilis, from the stem of Latin hospitari "be a guest," from hospes (genitive hospitis) "guest" (see host (n.1)).
1650s, from Medieval Latin multiplicativus, from multiplicat-, past participle stem of multiplicare (see multiply)..

meanings.definitionCn: 促进， 推动； 刺激； 推动力

etymology.story: early 15c., impetous "rapid movement, rush;" 1640s, with modern spelling, "force with which a body moves, driving force," from Latin impetus "an attack, assault; rapid motion; an impulse; violence, vigor, force;" figuratively "ardor, passion," from impetere "to attack," from assimilated form of in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + petere "aim for, rush at" (see petition (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 康复， 治疗； 复原

etymology.story: "restoration to health," Old English hæling, verbal noun from heal (v.).
"true to reality" (of art, literature, etc.), 1829; "involving a practical view of life" (opposed to idealistic), 1831; from realist + -ic.

meanings.definitionCn: 使恢复生机， 使再生； 使更强壮

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 生命 + ize

etymology.story: 1840, from re- "back, again" + vitalize.

meanings.definitionCn: 屏障， 障碍物； 检票口； 障碍， 阻力； 隔阂

etymology.story: early 14c., barere, from Anglo-French barrere, Old French barriere "obstacle, gatekeeper," from barre "bar" (see bar (n.1)).
also cariboo, 1660s, from Canadian French caribou, from Micmac (Algonquian) kaleboo or a related Algonquian name, literally "pawer, scratcher," from its kicking snow aside to feed on moss and grass..
1650s, alteration of simperen "to simmer" (late 15c.), possibly imitative; not thought to be connected to simper (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 取出内脏； 除去主要部分

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + viscer + 使…/具有…的

meanings.definitionCn: 绷紧肌肉；使顶住；使防备；加强，加固

etymology.story: mid-14c., "to seize, grasp," also "wrap, enshroud; tie up, fetter," from Old French bracier "to embrace," from brace "arms" (see brace (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 清新的； 敏捷的， 活泼的

etymology.story: 1550s, as Scottish bruisk, probably an alteration of French brusque (see brusque).

meanings.definitionCn: 噩梦； 无法摆脱的恐惧； 可怕的事

etymology.story: late 13c., "an evil female spirit afflicting sleepers with a feeling of suffocation," compounded from night + mare (3) "goblin that causes nightmares, incubus." Meaning shifted mid-16c.

morphology.mnemonic: 向前/支持 + to grow long, lengthen,

etymology.story: 1650s, "trick," from French prestige (16c.) "deceit, imposture, illusion" (in Modern French, "illusion, magic, glamour"), from Latin praestigium "delusion, illusion" (see prestigious).
1650s, from French spontanéité or a native formation from spontaneous + -ity..

meanings.definitionCn: 地核；果心，核心；要点，精髓
骗子， 冒名顶替者； 冒牌货； 欺骗

early 12c., from Anglo-French cuncile, from Old North French concilie (Old French concile, 12c.) "assembly; council meeting; body of counsellors," from Latin concilium "a meeting, a gathering of people," from PIE *kal-yo-, from root *kele- (2) "to shout" (see claim (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 火的， 似火的； 火成的， 火成岩的

etymology.story: 1660s, "pertaining to or resembling fire," from Latin igneus "of fire, fiery; on fire; burning hot," figuratively "ardent, vehement," from ignis "fire, a fire," extended to "brightness, splendor, glow;" figuratively "rage, fury, passion," from PIE root *egni- "fire" (source also of Sanskrit agnih "fire, sacrificial fire," Old Church Slavonic ogni, Lithuanian ugnis "fire").

meanings.definitionCn: 令人眼花缭乱的， 耀眼的

etymology.story: 1570s, from Middle French rectangle (16c.) and directly from Late Latin rectangulum, from rect-, comb.
1852, from rotation + -al (1)..

meanings.definitionCn: 撤销， 废止； 镇压； 平息

etymology.story: "to make void, annul," early 14c., from Old French quasser, casser "to annul, declare void," and directly from Medieval Latin quassare, alteration of Late Latin cassare, from cassus "null, void, empty" (see caste (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 扩展， 蔓延； 蔓延物
混凝土制的；具体的；确凿的

etymology.story: late 14c., "actual, solid," from Latin concretus "condensed, hardened, thick, hard, stiff, curdled, congealed, clotted," figuratively "thick; dim," literally "grown together;" past participle of concrescere "to grow together," from com- "together" (see com-) + crescere "to grow" (see crescent).

meanings.definitionCn: 缩短， 删节， 节略， 精简
处罚， 惩罚； 罚金； 犯规的处罚

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Middle French penalité and directly from Medieval Latin poenalitatem (nominative poenalitas), from Latin poenalis (see penal).

meanings.definitionCn: 鳞；规模；比例；刻度；【音】音阶；等级，级别

morphology.mnemonic: 生长/植物 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1560s, "act of vegetating," from Middle French végétation and directly from Medieval Latin vegetationem (nominative vegetatio) "a quickening, action of growing," from vegetare "grow, quicken" (see vegetable).
"interesting, winning, attractive," 1670s, present participle adjective from engage.

meanings.definitionCn: 投稿者； 贡献者， 捐助者； 促成物

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 给予/分配 + …者/…器

etymology.story: also contributer, mid-15c., from Anglo-French contributour, from Vulgar Latin *contributorem, agent noun from contribut-, stem of contribuere (see contribution).

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之下 + watershed, separation between river valleys

meanings.definitionCn: 爱管闲事的， 好打听的

morphology.mnemonic: 夜 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: also nosey, 1610s, "having a prominent nose," from nose (n.) + -y (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 猛然弓背跃起；猛然震荡；抵制，反抗

etymology.story: 1774, "glacier humped like a hill;" 1820 as "detached piece of a glacier or ice pack at sea," partial loan-translation of Dutch ijsberg, literally "ice mountain," from ijs "ice" (see ice (n.)) + berg "mountain" (see barrow (n.2)).

meanings.definitionCn: 安置； 派驻， 驻扎

morphology.mnemonic: 站立/设置 + 状态/行为/过程

meanings.definitionCn: 虚弱的， 衰弱的， 无力的

etymology.story: late 12c., "lacking strength or vigor" (physical, moral, or intellectual), from Old French feble "weak, feeble" (12c., Modern French faible), dissimilated from Latin flebilis "lamentable," literally "that is to be wept over," from flere "weep, cry, shed tears, lament," from PIE *bhle- "to howl" (see bleat (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 药草， 香草； 草本植物
放牧； 成群， 聚集
牧群，兽群；人群，芸芸众生

etymology.story: in early use also enverse, mid-15c., from Latin inversus, past participle of invertere "turn about, turn upside-down, upset, reverse, invert" (see invert).

meanings.definitionCn: 感知， 认知， 认识

etymology.story: mid-15c., "ability to comprehend," from Latin cognitionem (nominative cognitio) "a getting to know, acquaintance, knowledge," noun of action from past participle stem of cognoscere "to get to know, recognize" (see cognizance)..

morphology.mnemonic: to share a secret with + ance

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French confidence or directly from Latin confidentia, from confidentem (nominative confidens) "firmly trusting, bold," present participle of confidere "to have full trust or reliance," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + fidere "to trust" (see faith).

meanings.definitionCn: 烹饪； 烹调法， 烹调风格

etymology.story: 1786, from French cuisine "style of cooking," originally "kitchen, cooking, cooked food" (12c.), from Late Latin cocina, earlier coquina "kitchen," from Latin coquere "to cook" (see cook (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 任意的； 专制的， 专断的

etymology.story: early 15c., "deciding by one's own discretion," from Old French arbitraire (14c.) or directly from Latin arbitrarius "depending on the will, uncertain," from arbiter (see arbiter).

meanings.definitionCn: 专心的， 专注的； 急切的

etymology.story: late 14c., "very attentive, eager," from Latin intentus "attentive, eager, waiting, strained," past participle of intendere "to strain, stretch" (see intend).
1829, from readable + -ity..

1610s, from Latin astutus "crafty, wary, shrewd; sagacious, expert," from astus "cunning, cleverness, adroitness," which is of uncertain origin, perhaps from Greek asty "town," a word borrowed into Latin and with an overtone of "city sophistication" (compare asteism).

meanings.definitionCn: 挑战；艰巨任务，难题；质疑，质问
向…挑战； 公然反抗； 对…质疑
尊严， 庄严； 高贵， 尊贵； 自尊， 自重

etymology.story: early 13c., from Old French dignite "dignity, privilege, honor," from Latin dignitatem (nominative dignitas) "worthiness," from dignus "worth (n.), worthy, proper, fitting" from PIE *dek-no-, from root *dek- "to take, accept" (see decent)..

meanings.definitionCn: 掘出， 发掘； 揭露

etymology.story: "to dig up," mid-15c., from un- (2) "opposite of" + earth (v.) "bury (a corpse) in the ground" (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 发出； 放射； 排放

etymology.story: 1620s, from Latin emittere "send forth," from assimilated form of ex- "out" (see ex-) + mittere "to send" (see mission).

meanings.definitionCn: 猛咬；折断，断裂；打开，关上；呵斥，厉声说；拍照

etymology.story: "vehicle mounted on runners for use on ice and snow," 1703, American and Canadian English, from Dutch slee, shortened from slede (see sled (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 统治， 治理， 管理； 决定， 支配； 控制， 影响

etymology.story: late 13c., "to rule with authority," from Old French governer "steer, be at the helm of; govern, rule, command, direct" (11c., Modern French gouverner), from Latin gubernare "to direct, rule, guide, govern" (source also of Spanish gobernar, Italian governare), originally "to steer, to pilot," a nautical borrowing from Greek kybernan "to steer or pilot a ship, direct as a pilot," figuratively "to guide, govern" (the root of cybernetics).

morphology.mnemonic: 数/数量 + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin numerosus "numerous," from numerus "a number" (see number (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 地震的， 地震引起的； 影响深远的， 重大的

etymology.story: 1858, from seismo- + -ic..

meanings.definitionCn: 偏僻的， 偏远的； 久远的； 远程的； 关系远的； 微乎其微的； 冷漠的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Middle French remot or directly from Latin remotus "afar off, remote, distant in place," past participle of removere "move back or away" (see remove (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 自助餐厅， 自助食堂

etymology.story: 1839, American English, from Mexican Spanish cafeteria "coffee store," from café "coffee" (see coffee) + Spanish -tería "place where something is done" (usually business).

meanings.definitionCn: 破裂； 决裂， 断绝

morphology.mnemonic: 破裂 + 行为/过程/结果

etymology.story: late 14c., "to cast down," from Old French aflicter, from Latin afflictare "to damage, harass, torment," frequentative of affligere (past participle afflictus) "to dash down, overthrow," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + fligere (past participle flictus) "to strike," from PIE root *bhlig- "to strike" (source also of Greek phlibein "to press, crush," Czech blizna "scar," Welsh blif "catapult").
1630s, from Latin fluctuatus, past participle of fluctuare "to undulate" (see fluctuation).

meanings.definitionCn: 计算， 核算； 估计， 推测； 计划， 打算

etymology.story: 1560s, "to compute, to estimate by mathematical means," from Latin calculatus, past participle of calculare "to reckon, compute," from calculus (see calculus).

meanings.definitionCn: 怨恨的， 满怀恶意的

etymology.story: 1580s, from rancor + -ous.

meanings.definitionCn: 坚持的， 百折不挠的； 持续的

morphology.mnemonic: 通过/彻底 + 站立/设置 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1723, from persistence or from Latin persistentem (nominative persistens), present participle of persistere (see persist).

meanings.definitionCn: 授权； 批准， 许可

etymology.story: "give formal approval to," late 14c., autorisen, from Old French autoriser "authorize, give authority to" (12c.), from Medieval Latin auctorizare, from auctor (see author (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 精神创伤； 外伤； 痛苦经历

etymology.story: 1690s, "physical wound," medical Latin, from Greek trauma "a wound, a hurt; a defeat," from PIE *trau-, extended form of root *tere- (1) "to rub, turn," with derivatives referring to twisting, piercing, etc.

meanings.definitionCn: 听众； 观众； 读者

etymology.story: late 14c., "the action of hearing," from Old French audience, from Latin audentia "a hearing, listening," from audientum (nominative audiens), present participle of audire "to hear," from PIE compound *au-dh- "to perceive physically, grasp," from root *au- "to perceive" (source also of Greek aisthanesthai "to feel;" Sanskrit avih, Avestan avish "openly, evidently;" Old Church Slavonic javiti "to reveal").

late 14c., "to hold, occupy, reside in" (without regard to ownership), a back formation from possession and in part from Old French possesser "to have and hold, take, be in possession of" (mid-13c.), from Latin possess-, past participle stem of possidere "to have and hold, possess, be master of, own," from posse "to be able," from potis "able, powerful" (see potent) + esse "to be" (see be).

meanings.definitionCn: 献身， 奉献； 献词； 奉献仪式

etymology.story: late 14c., "action of dedicating," from Old French dedicacion (14c., Modern French dédication) "consecration of a church or chapel," or directly from Latin dedicationem, noun of action from dedicare (see dedicate).
mid-15c., "one who remains neutral," from Latin neutralis "of neuter gender," (see neutral (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 可以任选的， 非强制的

morphology.mnemonic: 最好/选择 + 状态/行为/过程 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1765, from option + -al (1)..

meanings.definitionCn: 分析； 分析报告； 分解

etymology.story: 1580s, "resolution of anything complex into simple elements" (opposite of synthesis), from Medieval Latin analysis (15c.), from Greek analysis "a breaking up, a loosening, releasing," noun of action from analyein "unloose, release, set free; to loose a ship from its moorings," in Aristotle, "to analyze," from ana "up, throughout" (see ana-) + lysis "a loosening," from lyein "to unfasten" (see lose).

meanings.definitionCn: 设计； 发明； 想出； 策划， 图谋

etymology.story: early 13c., "to form, fashion;" c.

meanings.definitionCn: 值得注意的，明显的；显著的；著名的；重要的

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French notable "well-known, notable, remarkable" (13c.) and directly from Latin notabilis "noteworthy, extraordinary," from notare "to note" (see note (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 日/天 + 与…有关的/形容词 + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: "business of writing, editing, or publishing a newspaper or public journal," 1821, regarded at first as a French word in English, from French journalisme (1781), from journal "daily publication" (see journal); compare journalist..

meanings.definitionCn: 慢性的， 延续很长的
暴露， 显露； 揭露； 曝光； 面临， 遭受

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 放置 + 行为/过程/结果

meanings.definitionCn: 疏散， 撤离； 排空

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + 空 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1520s (trans.), from Latin evacuatus, past participle of evacuare "to empty, make void, nullify," used by Pliny in reference to the bowels, used figuratively in Late Latin for "clear out;" from assimilated form of ex- "out" (see ex-) + vacuus "empty" (see vacuum).

meanings.definitionCn: 一次所孵的卵；离合器；掌握，控制；把握，抓紧

etymology.story: Old English clyccan "bring together, bend (the fingers), clench," from PIE *klukja- (source also of Swedish klyka "clamp, fork;" related to cling).

meanings.definitionCn: 破晓， 黎明； 开始， 发端
谦卑的；卑贱的；简陋的，低劣的

etymology.story: late 14c., "render oneself humble" (intrans.), also "to bend, kneel or bow;" late 15c.

early 15c., "benefit, profit, welfare;" later "a convenient or useful product," from Middle French commodité "benefit, profit," from Latin commoditatem (nominative commoditas) "fitness, adaptation, convenience, advantage," from commodus "suitable, convenient" (see commode).

meanings.definitionCn: 独立的， 自主的； 自立的； 不相干的， 不受影响的； 无偏见的， 中立的； 私立的； 有主见的； 无党派的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 向下/移除/取消 + 悬挂/称量/支付 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1610s, "not dependent on something else," from in- (1) "not, opposite of" + dependent.

meanings.definitionCn: 光滑的； 光彩夺目的； 浮华的

root.meaning: to add luster, make smooth and shining,

morphology.mnemonic: to add luster, make smooth and shining, + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: "smooth and shining," 1550s, from gloss (n.1) + -y (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 模糊形状； 模糊的记忆； 污点
似有道理的， 似乎正确的

etymology.story: 1540s, "acceptable, agreeable," from Latin plausibilis "deserving applause, acceptable," from plaus-, past participle stem of plaudere "to applaud" (see plaudit).

late 13c., from Old French cidre, cire "pear or apple cider" (12c., Modern French cidre), variant of cisdre, from Late Latin sicera, Vulgate rendition of Hebrew shekhar, a word used for any strong drink (translated in Old English as beor, taken untranslated in Septuagint Greek as sikera), related to Arabic sakar "strong drink," sakira "was drunk." Meaning gradually narrowed in English to mean exclusively "fermented drink made from apples," though this sense also was in Old French..

meanings.definitionCn: 谣言， 传闻， 道听途说

etymology.story: "information communicated by another, gossip," mid-15c., from phrase to hear say (Middle English heren seien, Old English herdon secgan).

mid-15c., "of this world," from Old French mondain "of this world, worldly, earthly, secular;" also "pure, clean; noble, generous" (12c.), from Late Latin mundanus "belonging to the world" (as distinct from the Church), in classical Latin "a citizen of the world, cosmopolite," from mundus "universe, world," literally "clean, elegant"; used as a translation of Greek kosmos (see cosmos) in its Pythagorean sense of "the physical universe" (the original sense of the Greek word was "orderly arrangement").

morphology.mnemonic: 殖民/定居 + ize + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1766, noun of action from colonize..
1650s, from Latin fortuitus "happening by chance, casual, accidental," from forte "by chance," ablative of fors "chance" (related to fortuna; see fortune).
late 14c., from Medieval Latin aequator (diei et noctis) "equalizer (of day and night)," agent noun from Latin aequare "make equal" (see equate).

root.meaning: quality of being male or female

morphology.mnemonic: quality of being male or female + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: 1968; see sexist + -ism..

meanings.definitionCn: 使无资格； 使失去能力； 使不胜任

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 取/抓 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from deceit + -ful.

root.meaning: acute or penetrating in intellect or perception

morphology.mnemonic: acute or penetrating in intellect or perception + 使变成/变得

etymology.story: 1520s, "bring to an edge or point," from sharp (adj.) + -en (1).
1764, from sediment + -ary.

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 发送 + ee

etymology.story: 1620s, from commit + -ee, or else a revival of Anglo-French commite, past participle of commettre "to commit," from Latin committere "to unite, connect" (see commit).

meanings.definitionCn: 仁； 粒； 核心， 要点

etymology.story: "edible substance in a nut or the stone of a fruit," Old English cyrnel "seed, kernel, pip," from Proto-Germanic *kurnilo- (source also of Middle High German kornel "a grain," Middle Dutch cornel "coarse meal"), from the root of corn "seed, grain" (see corn (n.1)) + -el, diminutive suffix.
"niggardly, penurious, extremely tight-fisted," 1650s, of uncertain origin, perhaps a dialectal alteration of earlier stingy "biting, sharp, stinging" (1610s), from sting (v.).
1590s, from Latin excavatus, past participle of excavare "to hollow out," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + cavare "to hollow, hollow out," from cavus "cave" (see cave (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 细微的， 微妙的； 精巧的， 巧妙的； 狡猾的； 敏锐的

etymology.story: 1839, "picture obtained by photography," coined by Sir John Herschel from photo- + -graph "instrument for recording; something written." It won out over other suggestions, such as photogene and heliograph.
1640s, "constitutional element" (earlier "one of a group of persons," 1560s), from Latin componentem (nominative componens), present participle of componere "to put together" (see composite).
1670s, "train of followers or attendants," from French suite, from Old French suite, sieute "act of following, attendance" (see suit (n.), which is an earlier borrowing of the same French word).

meanings.definitionCn: 淹没， 漫过； 压倒， 击败， 制服； 使难以忍受； 使应接不暇

etymology.story: early 14c., "to turn upside down, to overthrow," from over- + Middle English whelmen "to turn upside down" (see whelm).

"mortuary," 1821, from French Morgue, originally a specific building in Paris where bodies were exposed for identification:

Before that it was the place where new prisoners were displayed to keepers to establish their identification.

root.meaning: thing or person that goes before another

morphology.mnemonic: 不/非/否定 + thing or person that goes before another

etymology.story: 1620s, from un- (1) "not" + precedented.

meanings.definitionCn: 钱袋， 皮夹； 财政状况， 财力

etymology.story: also pocket-book, 1610s, originally a small book meant to be carried in one's pocket, from pocket (n.) + book (n.).
mid-14c., charcole, first element is either Old French charbon "charcoal," or, on the current theory, obsolete charren "to turn" (from Old English cerran) + cole "coal," thus, "to turn to coal.".

meanings.definitionCn: 掩饰； 伪装， 假装
帝国的， 帝王的； 至尊的； 专横的

morphology.mnemonic: the British Empire, + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., "having a commanding quality," from Old French imperial, emperial "imperial; princely, splendid; strong, powerful" (12c.), from Latin imperialis "of the empire or emperor," from imperium "empire" (see empire).

meanings.definitionCn: 披肩， 围巾； 包装材料

root.meaning: the recorded events of the past

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + the recorded events of the past + ic

etymology.story: 1832, modeled on French préhistorique; see pre- + historic..
1620s, "one side of a multi-sided body," from French facette (12c., Old French facete), diminutive of face "face, appearance" (see face (n.)).
"fictitious narrative," 1560s, from Italian novella "short story," originally "new story," from Latin novella "new things" (source of Middle French novelle, French nouvelle), neuter plural or fem.
"tediousness," 1660s, from Latin taedium "weariness, irksomeness, disgust," related to taedet "it is wearisome, it excites loathing," and to taedere "to weary," of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 自由的， 开明的； 慷慨的； 不严格的； 人文的

morphology.mnemonic: 自由 + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 和声； 和谐， 融洽， 协调； 相符， 一致

etymology.story: late 14c., "combination of tones pleasing to the ear," from Old French harmonie, armonie "harmony," also the name of a musical instrument (12c.), from Latin harmonia, from Greek harmonia "agreement, concord of sounds," also as a proper name, the personification of music, literally "means of joining," used of ship-planks, etc., also "settled government, order," related to harmos "fastenings of a door; shoulder," from PIE *ar-ti-, from *ar- "to fit together" (see arm (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 亲切的， 和蔼的； 温和的， 温暖的

etymology.story: 1560s, "pertaining to marriage," from Latin genialis "pleasant, festive," literally "pertaining to marriage rites," from genius "guardian spirit" (see genius), with here perhaps a special sense of "tutelary deity of a married couple." Originally used in English in the Latin literal sense; meaning "cheerful, friendly" first recorded 1746.

meanings.definitionCn: 摘要， 梗概； 典型， 典范

etymology.story: 1520s, "an abstract; brief statement of the chief points of some writing," from Middle French épitomé (16c.), from Latin epitome "an abridgment," from Greek epitome "an abridgment, a cutting on the surface; brief summary," from epitemnein "cut short, abridge," from epi "into" (see epi-) + temnein "to cut" (see tome).

meanings.definitionCn: 急剧地； 猛烈地； 尖刻地； 鲜明地

root.meaning: acute or penetrating in intellect or perception

morphology.mnemonic: acute or penetrating in intellect or perception + 以…方式/副词化

etymology.story: Old English scearplice "acutely, keenly; painfully, severely; attentively, quickly;" see sharp (adj.) + -ly (2).
1809, originally nautical, "in collision or entanglement," from a- (1) + foul (adj.).

meanings.definitionCn: 管理， 控制； 调整， 调节； 校准

etymology.story: early 15c., "adjust by rule, control," from Late Latin regulatus, past participle of regulare "to control by rule, direct," from Latin regula "rule" (see regular).

meanings.definitionCn: 模仿， 效仿； 仿制， 仿造

morphology.mnemonic: 模仿/复制 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1530s, a back-formation from imitation or imitator, or else from Latin imitatus, past participle of imitari "to copy, portray."
1630s, from French computer, from Latin computare "to count, sum up, reckon together," from com- "with" (see com-) + putare "to reckon," originally "to prune" (see pave).

1660s, "crayons, chalk-like pigment used in crayons," from French pastel "crayon," from Italian pastello "a pastel," literally "material reduced to a paste," from Late Latin pastellus "dye from the leaves of the woad plant," diminutive of pasta (see pasta).

meanings.definitionCn: 家宅， 农庄； 宅地
中间人， 调解人； 经纪人

etymology.story: in the trading sense, 1795, from middle + man.
Old English oferyrnan; see over- + run (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 避免， 规避； 转移

etymology.story: mid-14c., "living together, having dealings with others," also "manner of conducting oneself in the world;" from Old French conversation, from Latin conversationem (nominative conversatio) "act of living with," noun of action from past participle stem of conversari "to live with, keep company with," literally "turn about with," from Latin com- "with" (see com-) + vertare, frequentative of vertere (see versus).

morphology.mnemonic: 单一 + 统治/首领 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: "state ruled by monarchical government," mid-14c.; "rule by one person," late 14c.; from Old French monarchie "sovereignty, absolute power" (13c.), from Late Latin monarchia, from Greek monarkhia "absolute rule," literally "ruling of one," from monos "alone" (see mono-) + arkhein "to rule" (see archon)..

early 15c., "action of manifesting; exhibition, demonstration," from Late Latin manifestationem (nominative manifestatio), noun of action from past participle stem of Latin manifestare (see manifest (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 危险的， 罪恶的； 残酷的， 凶残的； 恶性的

etymology.story: late 14c., "unwholesome, impure, of the nature of vice, wicked, corrupting, pernicious, harmful;" of a text, "erroneous, corrupt," from Anglo-French vicious, Old French vicios "wicked, cunning, underhand; defective, illegal" (Modern French vicieux), from Latin vitiosus (Medieval Latin vicious) "faulty, full of faults, defective, corrupt; wicked, depraved," from vitium "fault" (see vice (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 邀请； 请柬； 吸引， 诱惑

etymology.story: mid-15c., "act of inviting, solicitation," from Latin invitationem (nominative invitatio) "an invitation, incitement, challenge," noun of action from past participle stem of invitare "invite, treat, entertain," originally "be pleasant toward," from in- "toward" (see in- (2)).
"solid part of the earth's surface," 1881; see litho- "stone" + sphere..
1550s, "ominous," from Middle French prodigieux and directly from Latin prodigiosus "strange, wonderful, marvelous, unnatural," from prodigium (see prodigy).

meanings.definitionCn: 复兴， 再流行； 复苏； 苏醒， 复活； 重演

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + long live ______; + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1650s, "act of reviving;" 1660s, "the bringing of an old play back to the stage," from revive + -al (2).

morphology.mnemonic: like new wine, + 行为/过程/结果

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French moistour "moisture, dampness, wetness" (13c., Modern French moiteur), from moiste (see moist)..

meanings.definitionCn: 树懒； 懒散， 怠惰

etymology.story: late 12c., "indolence, sluggishness," formed from Middle English slou, slowe (see slow (adj.)) + abstract formative -th (2).
"be wanting or deficient" (intransitive), late 12c., perhaps from Middle Dutch laken "to be wanting," from lak (n.) "deficiency, fault," or an unrecorded native cognate word (see lack (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 外部的， 外面的； 外来的； 对外的

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + tern + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French externe or directly from Latin externus "outside, outward" (from exterus; see exterior) + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 环境， 条件； 境况， 经济状况

etymology.story: early 13c., "conditions surrounding and accompanying an event," from Old French circonstance "circumstance, situation," also literally, "outskirts" (13c., Modern French circonstance), from Latin circumstantia "surrounding condition," neuter plural of circumstans (genitive circumstantis), present participle of circumstare "stand around, surround, encompass, occupy, take possession of" from circum "around" (see circum-) + stare "to stand" from PIE root *stā- "to stand" (see stet).

meanings.definitionCn: 修订， 修改； 复习

root.meaning: clamping tool with two jaws closed by a screw

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + clamping tool with two jaws closed by a screw

etymology.story: 1560s, "to look at again," from Middle French reviser (13c.), from Latin revisere "look at again, visit again, look back on," frequentative of revidere (past participle revisus), from re- "again" (see re-) + videre "to see" (see vision).

early 13c., autorite "book or quotation that settles an argument," from Old French auctorité "authority, prestige, right, permission, dignity, gravity; the Scriptures" (12c.; Modern French autorité), from Latin auctoritatem (nominative auctoritas) "invention, advice, opinion, influence, command," from auctor "master, leader, author" (see author (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 至为重要的； 最高权力的， 至尊的

etymology.story: 1530s, from Anglo-French paramont, Old French paramont "above" (in place, order, degree), mid-14c., from Old French par "by," from Latin per "through, for, by" (see per (prep.)) + amont "up," from a mont "upward" (see amount (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 祖母绿， 绿宝石； 翡翠绿

etymology.story: "bright green precious stone," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 立法机关， 立法团体

etymology.story: "a body of lawmakers," 1670s; see legislator + -ure..

meanings.definitionCn: 好交际的， 合群的； 友善的， 友好的

etymology.story: 1550s, "enjoying the company of others," from Middle French sociable (16c.) and directly from Latin sociabilis "close, intimate, easily united," from sociare "to join, unite," from socius "companion, ally" (see social (adj.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 令人高兴的， 使人兴奋的

etymology.story: Old English Sætern, a Roman god, also "most remote planet" (then known), from Latin Saturnus, originally a name of an Italic god of agriculture, possibly from Etruscan.

meanings.definitionCn: 复仇， 报仇； 向…报仇

etymology.story: late 14c., from Anglo-French avenger, Old French avengier, from a- "to" (see ad-) + vengier "take revenge" (Modern French venger), from Latin vindicare "to claim, avenge, punish" (see vindicate).

1570s, from Latin obsoletus "grown old, worn-out," past participle of obsolescere "fall into disuse, be forgotten about, become tarnished," which probably is from ob "away" (see ob-) + an expanded form of solere "to be used to, be accustomed" (see insolent)..

meanings.definitionCn: 仪器， 器械， 工具； 乐器

etymology.story: late 13c., "musical instrument, mechanical apparatus for producing musical sounds," from Old French instrument, enstrument "means, device; musical instrument" (14c., earlier estrument, 13c.) and directly from Latin instrumentum "a tool, an implement; means, furtherance; apparatus, furniture; ornament, dress, embellishment; a commission, authorization; a document," from instruere "arrange, furnish" (see instruct (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 〔大小、水平或数量〕中等的；中号的
传播媒介〔如报纸、电视等〕

etymology.story: "lightest volatile liquid obtained from distillation of petroleum," 1864 (alternative spelling gasolene is from 1865), from gas (n.) + -ol (probably here representing Latin oleum "oil") + chemical suffix -ine (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 界面， 分界面； 接口

root.meaning: to cover with something in front

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + to cover with something in front

etymology.story: 1874, "a plane surface regarded as the common boundary of two bodies," from inter- + face (n.).
1826 (implied in sculpted), from French sculpter, from Latin sculpt-, past participle stem of sculpere "to carve" (see sculpture).
"organic substance of the fat group," from French lipide, coined 1923 by G.

meanings.definitionCn: 致命的； 毁灭性的； 命中注定的

etymology.story: late 14c., "decreed by fate," also "fraught with fate," from Middle French fatal (14c.) and directly from Latin fatalis "ordained by fate, decreed, destined; destructive, deadly," from fatum (see fate (n.)); sense of "causing or attended with death" in English is from early 15c.

"organic compound produced in animal bodies to regulate activity and behavior," 1905, from Greek hormon "that which sets in motion," present participle of horman "impel, urge on," from horme "onset, impulse," from PIE *or-sma-, from root *er- (1) "to move, set in motion." Used by Hippocrates to denote a vital principle; modern scientific meaning coined by English physiologist Ernest Henry Starling (1866-1927).

meanings.definitionCn: 心理学； 心理， 心理特征

morphology.mnemonic: 心理/精神 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1650s, "study of the soul," from Modern Latin psychologia, probably coined mid-16c.

meanings.definitionCn: 脊； 山脊， 山脉； 高压脊； 隆起物

etymology.story: Old English hrycg "back of a man or beast," probably reinforced by Old Norse hryggr "back, ridge," from Proto-Germanic *khrugjaz (source also of Old Frisian hregg, Old Saxon hruggi, Dutch rug, Old High German hrukki, German Rücken "the back"), of uncertain origin.
1738, from hypothesis + -ize.

morphology.mnemonic: pure清，纯，净 + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 缠绕， 盘绕， 围绕
做， 履行， 完成； 表演， 演出； 执行
回顾的， 追想的； 有追溯效力的

morphology.mnemonic: retro + 看/观察 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1660s, from retrospect + -ive.

meanings.definitionCn: 发展； 演变， 进化， 进化论

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + volve + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1620s, "an opening of what was rolled up," from Latin evolutionem (nominative evolutio) "unrolling (of a book)," noun of action from past participle stem of evolvere "to unroll" (see evolve).

meanings.definitionCn: 赚钱的， 获利的， 有利可图的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French lucratif "profitable" and directly from Latin lucrativus "gainful, profitable," from lucratus, past participle of lucrari "to gain," from lucrum "gain, profit" (see lucre).

meanings.definitionCn: 肖像， 画像； 描写， 描绘

etymology.story: 1560s, "a figure, drawn or painted," a back formation from portraiture or directly from Middle French portrait, from Old French portret (13c.), noun use of past participle of portraire "to paint, depict" (see portray).

meanings.definitionCn: 空虚， 空洞； 无意义的事物

etymology.story: 1610s, from French acidité (26c.) or directly from Latin aciditatem (nominative aciditas), noun of quality from Latin acidus (see acid (adj.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 诉苦， 抱怨； 投诉； 控告

etymology.story: late 14c., "lamentation, grief," from Old French complainte (12c.) "complaint, lament," noun use of fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 山顶， 顶峰； 鼎盛时期； 小尖塔
注射； 注入， 灌输； 投入

etymology.story: 1796, from cannibal + -ism.
mid-15c., "quality of being neither good nor bad, neutral quality," from Latin indifferentia "want of difference, similarity," noun of quality from indifferentem (see indifferent).

mid-14c., from Latin copiosus "plentiful," from copia "an abundance, ample supply, profusion, plenty," from com- "with" (see com-) + ops (genitive opis) "power, wealth, resources," from PIE root *op- (1) "to work, produce in abundance," (see opus).

morphology.mnemonic: in + 取/抓 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "a beginning, a starting," from Old French inception and directly from Latin inceptionem (nominative inceptio) "a beginning; an undertaking," noun of action from past participle stem of incipere "begin, take in hand," from in- "in, on" (see in- (2)) + cipere comb.

meanings.definitionCn: 频道； 渠道， 途径； 通道； 海峡； 水道， 航道

morphology.mnemonic: to make up one's mind + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1610s, from Medieval Latin decisivus, from Latin decis-, past participle stem of decidere (see decide).
1580s, from Latin concisus "cut off, brief," past participle of concidere "to cut off, cut up, cut through, cut to pieces," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + caedere "to cut" (see -cide).

meanings.definitionCn: 论文； 论题， 论点

etymology.story: late 14c., "unaccented syllable or note," from Latin thesis "unaccented syllable in poetry," later (and more correctly) "stressed part of a metrical foot," from Greek thesis "a proposition," also "downbeat" (in music), originally "a setting down, a placing, an arranging; position, situation," from root of tithenai "to place, put, set," from PIE root *dhe- "to set, to put" (see factitious).

1560s, "to bulge out;" 1610s, "to strike heavily," perhaps from Scandinavian, probably echoic, original sense was "hitting" then of "swelling from being hit." Also has a long association with obsolete bum "to make a booming noise," which perhaps influenced surviving senses such as bumper crop, for something full to the brim (see bumper).

meanings.definitionCn: 先驱； 先兆， 前兆； 前身

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + to swear profanely + …者/…器

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French precurseur and directly from Latin praecursor "forerunner," agent noun from past participle stem of praecurrere, from prae "before" (see pre-) + currere "to run" (see current (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 恢复； 苏醒； 重新利用

etymology.story: early 15c., "return to consciousness; restore to health," from Middle French revivre (10c.), from Latin revivere "to live again," from re- "again" (see re-) + vivere "to live" (see vital).
1520s, "scent with perfumes," back-formation from fumigation.

meanings.definitionCn: 干旱的； 贫瘠的； 无趣的

etymology.story: 1650s, "dry, parched," from French aride (15c.) or directly from Latin aridus "dry, arid, parched," from arere "to be dry," from PIE root *as- "to burn, glow" (see ash (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 纤维素； 纤维制品； 纤维

etymology.story: late 14c., fibre "a lobe of the liver," also "entrails," from Medieval Latin fibre, from Latin fibra "a fiber, filament; entrails," which is of uncertain origin, perhaps related to Latin filum "a thread, string" (see file (n.1)) or from root of findere "to split" (see fissure).

meanings.definitionCn: 进行下去， 继续做； 发生； 继续下去； 行进， 前往

etymology.story: late 14c., "to go on," also "to emanate from, result from," from Old French proceder (13c., Modern French procéder) and directly from Latin procedere (past participle processus) "go before, go forward, advance, make progress; come forward," from pro- "forward" (see pro-) + cedere "to go" (see cede).

meanings.definitionCn: 音乐的；悦耳的；有音乐天赋的，爱好音乐的

etymology.story: from Italian aria, literally "air" (see air (n.1))..

meanings.definitionCn: 鳞茎； 鳞茎状物； 灯泡

etymology.story: 1560s, "an onion," from Middle French bulbe (15c.), from Latin bulbus "bulb, bulbous root, onion," from Greek bolbos "plant with round swelling on underground stem." Expanded by 1800 to "swelling in a glass tube" (thermometer bulb, light bulb, etc.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 不再考虑， 不接受； 消除， 摒除； 免职， 解雇； 解散， 遣散； 驳回， 不受理

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin dimissus, past participle of dimittere "send away, send different ways; break up, discharge; renounce, abandon," from dis- "apart, away" (see dis-) + mittere "send, let go" (see mission).

meanings.definitionCn: 深不可测的； 极坏的， 糟透的

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1650s, formed in English from abysm + -al (1).

etymology.story: "abundance of words," 1721, from French verbiage "wordiness" (17c.), from Middle French verbier "to chatter," from Old French verbe "word," from Latin verbum "word" (see verb)..
1610s, present participle adjective from underlie..

meanings.definitionCn: 智力， 智慧； 情报

etymology.story: late 14c., "the highest faculty of the mind, capacity for comprehending general truths;" c.
1781, coined by Jeremy Bentham (1748-1832) from utility + -arian on the model of + unitarian, etc.

meanings.definitionCn: 平滑的，平坦的；平稳的，均匀的；均衡的；平均的；偶数的

etymology.story: "end of the day," Old English æfen, Mercian efen, Northumbrian efern (see eve (n.))..
1797, from French ambiance (see ambient).

also instal, formerly also enstall, early 15c., "place in (ecclesiastical) office by seating in an official stall," from Old French installer (14c.) or directly from Medieval Latin installare, from Latin in- "in" (see in- (2)) + Medieval Latin stallum "stall," from a Germanic source (compare Old High German stal "standing place;" see stall (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 容积， 体积； 主体， 大部分
空房间； 空位， 空缺； 空虚

morphology.mnemonic: something desirable + 行为/结果/集合

etymology.story: late 14c., "feathers," from Old French plumage "plumage, appearance" (14c.), from plume (see plume (n.))..

海盗行为； 抢劫行为； 剽窃行为

etymology.story: early 15c., from Medieval Latin piratia, from Greek peirateia "piracy," from peirates (see pirate (n.))..

etymology.story: 1640s, formed in English from Latin loco "from a place" (ablative of locus "place") + motionem (nominative motio) "motion, a moving.".

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Old French magnificent, a back-formation from Latin magnificentior, comparative of magnificus "great, elevated, noble, distinguished," literally "doing great deeds" (see magnificence)..

再/重新/回 + 拉紧/约束

etymology.story: early 15c., "to reload" (a vessel), from re- "again, back" + charge "to load" (q.v.); modeled on Old French rechargier "to load, load back on" (13c.).

meanings.definitionCn: 制定法律； 通过立法

etymology.story: "to make laws," 1805, back-formation from legislation or legislator.

morphology.mnemonic: the British Empire, + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: mid-15c., in grammar; later "something imperative" (c.

morphology.mnemonic: to support by documentary evidence + …的场所/与…有关

meanings.definitionCn: 结果； 影响； 推理； 重要， 重大

etymology.story: late 14c., "inference, conclusion," from Old French consequence "result" (13c., Modern French conséquence), from Latin consequentia, from consequentem (nominative consequens), present participle of consequi "to follow after," from com- "with" (see com-) + sequi "to follow" (see sequel).

morphology.mnemonic: one's occupation, branch of business + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: 1640s, from French linéaire, from Latin linearis "belonging to a line," from linea "string, line" (see line (n.)).

1540s, from Middle French robuste (14c.) and directly from Latin robustus "strong and hardy," literally "as strong as oak," originally "oaken," from robur, robus "hard timber, strength," also "a special kind of oak," named for its reddish heartwood, from Latin ruber "red" (related to robigo "rust"), from PIE *reudh- (see red (adj.1)).

Old English hræfn (Mercian), hrefn; hræfn (Northumbrian, West Saxon), from Proto-Germanic *khrabanaz (source also of Old Norse hrafn, Danish ravn, Dutch raaf, Old High German hraban, German Rabe "raven," Old English hroc "rook"), from PIE root *ker- (2), imitative of harsh sounds (source also of Latin crepare "to creak, clatter," cornix "crow," corvus "raven;" Greek korax "raven," korone "crow;" Old Church Slavonic kruku "raven;" Lithuanian krauklys "crow").

meanings.definitionCn: 统治的， 支配的， 占优势的； 有统治权的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Middle French dominant (13c.), from Latin dominantem (nominative dominans), present participle of dominari (see domination).

etymology.story: early 14c., "announce," from Old French denoncier (12c., Modern French dénoncer), from Latin denuntiare "to announce, proclaim; denounce, menace; command, order," from de- "down" + nuntiare "proclaim, announce," from nuntius "messenger" (see nuncio).

meanings.definitionCn: 沼泽的， 湿地的； 松软的

"edge, rim," mid-15c., from Old French verge "twig, branch; measuring rod; penis; rod or wand of office" (12c.), hence, from the last sense, "scope, territory dominated" (as in estre suz la verge de "be under the authority of"), from Latin virga "shoot, rod, stick, slender green branch," of unknown origin.

etymology.story: "surroundings," 1877, from French milieu, "middle, medium, mean," literally "middle place" (12c.), from mi "middle" (from Latin medius; see medial (adj.)) + lieu "place" (see lieu)..

morphology.mnemonic: 微小 + musical instrument, + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: 1590s, from Late Latin entitatem (nominative entitas), from ens (genitive entis) "a thing," proposed by Caesar as present participle of esse "be" (see is), to render Greek philosophical term to on "that which is" (from neuter of present participle of einai "to be;" see essence).

meanings.definitionCn: 节约的， 节俭的； 少量的

etymology.story: "economical in use," 1590s, from Middle French frugal, from Latin frugalis, from undeclined adjective frugi "useful, proper, worthy, honest; temperate, economical," originally dative of frux (plural fruges) "fruit, produce," figuratively "value, result, success," related to fructus (see fruit), from PIE *bhrug- "agricultural produce," also "to enjoy." Sense evolved in Latin from "useful" to "profitable" to "economical."

meanings.definitionCn: 特权，特许；特许经销权；选举权
赋予特权； 赋予选举权

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 折叠/交织 + 统治

etymology.story: 1824, from Italian replica "copy, repetition, reply," from replicare "to duplicate," from Latin replicare "to repeat" (see reply (v.)).

1847, plural of Modern Latin bacterium, from Greek bakterion "small staff," diminutive of baktron "stick, rod," from PIE *bak- "staff used for support" (also source of Latin baculum "rod, walking stick").

"crowned with laurels" (as a mark of distinction), late 14c., earliest reference is to poetic distinction, from Latin laureatus "crowned with laurels," from laurea "laurel crown" (emblematic of victory or distinction in poetry), from fem.

"to hold or exercise sovereign power," late 13c., from Old French regner "rule, reign" (12c.), from Latin regnare "have royal power, be king, rule, reign," from regnum "kingship, dominion, rule, realm," related to regere "to rule, to direct, keep straight, guide" (see regal).

late 14c., "weak, unsound" (of things), from Latin infirmus "weak, frail, feeble, not strong or firm" (figuratively "superstitious, pusillanimous, inconstant"), from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + firmus "strong; stable," figuratively "constant, trusty" (see firm (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 速度， 节奏； 行进速度

etymology.story: "relative speed of a piece of music," 1724, from Italian tempo, literally "time" (plural tempi), from Latin tempus "time, season, portion of time" (see temporal).

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + socio + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: early 15c., "allied, connected, paired," from Latin associatus, past participle of associare (see associate (v.))..

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Anglo-French scrupulus, Middle French scrupuleux, or directly from Latin scrupulosus, from scrupulus (see scruple).

点滴搜集， 四处收集

大坑， 深坑； 矿井； 陷阱
主题， 主旨； 图形

1840, from Medieval Latin quantificare, from Latin quantus "as much," correlative pronominal adjective (see quantity) + facere "to make" (see factitious).
