meanings.definitionCn: 转折， 突然变化； 卷曲物
赦免； 免除； 宽恕

morphology.mnemonic: 远离/脱离 + 太阳/独自

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin absolvere "set free, loosen, acquit," from ab- "from" (see ab-) + solvere "loosen" (see solve).

meanings.definitionCn: 独处， 隐居； 隔离； 与世隔绝， 归隐

morphology.mnemonic: 跟随 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1610s, from Medieval Latin seclusionem (nominative seclusio), noun of action from past participle stem of Latin secludere (see seclude)..
1670s, variant of Middle English bulder (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 使坐落于； 找出， 定位

etymology.story: 1650s, "to establish oneself in a place, settle," from Latin locatus, past participle of locare "to place, put, set, dispose, arrange," from locus "a place" (see locus).
1866 in archaeology (somewhat earlier in geology); see meso- + lithic..
"many-stemmed woody plant," Old English bysc, from West Germanic *busk "bush, thicket" (source also of Old Saxon and Old High German busc, Dutch bosch, bos, German Busch).
late 14c., protectors of the arts, from Old French Muse and directly from Latin Musa, from Greek Mousa, "the Muse," also "music, song," from PIE root *men- "to think, remember" (see mind (n.)).
1560s, from Middle French combat (16c.), from combattre (see combat (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 令人忧伤的； 极严重的
矩形的，长方形的；椭圆形的
矩形， 长方形； 椭圆形

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin oblongus "more long than broad," originally "somewhat long," from ob "to, toward," here perhaps intensive (see ob-) + longus "long" (see long (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 团结一致， 统一， 联合； 协调； 统一体

etymology.story: 1872, "laws of origination;" see genetic + -ics.

meanings.definitionCn: 继续； 从事于； 追击， 追踪； 追求

etymology.story: late 13c., "to follow with hostile intent," from Anglo-French pursuer and directly from Old French poursuir (Modern  French poursuivre), variant of porsivre "to chase, pursue, follow; continue, carry on," from Vulgar Latin *prosequare, from Latin prosequi "follow, accompany, attend; follow after, escort; follow up, pursue," from pro- "forward" (see pro-) + sequi "follow" (see sequel).

meanings.definitionCn: 下流的， 粗俗的； 普通的， 通俗的

etymology.story: late 14c., "common, ordinary," from Latin vulgaris, volgaris "of or pertaining to the common people, common, vulgar, low, mean," from vulgus "the common people, multitude, crowd, throng," perhaps from a PIE root *wel- "to crowd, throng" (source also of Sanskrit vargah "division, group," Greek eilein "to press, throng," Middle Breton gwal'ch "abundance," Welsh gwala "sufficiency, enough") [not in Watkins].

meanings.definitionCn: 灾难， 灾祸； 困难

etymology.story: 1530s, "reversal of what is expected" (especially a fatal turning point in a drama), from Latin catastropha, from Greek katastrophe "an overturning; a sudden end," from katastrephein "to overturn, turn down, trample on; to come to an end," from kata "down" (see cata-) + strephein "turn" (see strophe).

meanings.definitionCn: 运送， 运输； 传达， 表达

etymology.story: 1570s, from Middle French convexe, from Latin convexus "vaulted, arched," past participle of convehere "to bring together," from com- "together," or "thoroughly" (see com-) + vehere "to bring" (see vehicle).

meanings.definitionCn: 赎回； 偿清， 付清； 兑换； 弥补， 补偿； 履行， 遵守

etymology.story: early 15c., "buy back, ransom," from Middle French redemer "buy back," from Latin redimere (see redemption).

meanings.definitionCn: 颅骨， 头骨； 脑袋； 头脑

etymology.story: "bony framework of the head," c.

1620s, "quality of having reason;" 1650s, "fact of being agreeable to reason," from French rationalité, from Late Latin rationalitas "reasonableness, rationality" (also source of Spanish racionalidad, Italian razionalita), from Latin rationalis (see rational)..

morphology.mnemonic: 单一 + 婚姻 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1610s, from French monogamie, from Late Latin monogamia, from Greek monogamia, from monogamos "marrying only once," from monos "single, alone" (see mono-) + gamos "marriage" (see gamete)..

early 15c., from Old French contaminer, from Latin contaminatus, past participle of contaminare "to defile," from contamen "contact, pollution," from com- "together" (see com-) + *tag-, base of tangere "to touch" (see tangent (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 暗指， 影射， 间接提到

etymology.story: 1530s, "mock," from Middle French alluder or directly from Latin alludere "to play, sport, joke, jest," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + ludere "to play" (see ludicrous).
"lethargy, listlessness," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 捕食性动物， 食肉动物； 掠夺者， 剥削者

etymology.story: 1862, from Latin praedator "plunderer," from praedari "to rob" (see predation).

meanings.definitionCn: 首领； 酋长， 族长

etymology.story: late 13c., accourd, from Old French acord "agreement," a back-formation from acorder (see accord (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 最先的； 最著名的， 最重要的

morphology.mnemonic: for the (reason) that. + the best, extremely good

etymology.story: Middle English formest, from Old English fyrmest, formest "earliest, first, most prominent," from Proto-Germanic *furmista-/*frumista- (related to Old English fruma "beginning"), from PIE *pre-mo-, suffixed (superlative) form of root *per- (1) "forward, through; before; first" (see per) + additional superlative suffix -est.

meanings.definitionCn: 自动的；无意识的；必然的，自然的
自动手枪； 自动换挡汽车

morphology.mnemonic: 不/分离 + untouched, + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1796, from dis- "do the opposite of" + integrate (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 连续不断的， 连贯的

etymology.story: 1610s, from French consécutif (16c.), from Medieval Latin consecutivus, from Latin consecutus "following closely," past participle of consequi (see consequence).

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 相似/相同 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "act of assimilating," from Old French assimilacion, from Latin assimilationem (nominative assimilatio) "likeness, similarity," noun of action from past participle stem of assimilare (see assimilate).
"number of times one quantity is contained in another," mid-15c., from Latin quotiens "how often? how many times?; as often as," pronominal adverb of time, from quot "how many?" (see quote (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 呻吟；叹息；发出呻吟般的声音
呻吟； 抱怨； 呻吟声， 叹息声

etymology.story: also merchandize, "to buy and sell; to market," late 14c.; see merchant + -ize.
small, smooth stone, late 13c., from Old English papolstan "pebblestone," of unknown origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 引导； 传导； 进行； 管理， 指挥； 表现
行为； 管理， 实施

etymology.story: early 15c., "to guide," from Latin conductus, past participle of conducere "to lead or bring together" (see conduce).

morphology.mnemonic: in-person + 以…方式/副词化 + hood

etymology.story: 1610s, alteration of livelode "means of keeping alive" (c.

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + un + 状态/行为/过程

meanings.definitionCn: 荒诞的， 奇异的； 极好的； 极大的

etymology.story: late 14c., "existing only in imagination," from Middle French fantastique (14c.), from Medieval Latin fantasticus, from Late Latin phantasticus "imaginary," from Greek phantastikos "able to imagine," from phantazein "make visible" (middle voice phantazesthai "picture to oneself"); see phantasm.
study of signs and symbols with special regard to function and origin, 1880, from semiotic; also see -ics.

meanings.definitionCn: 无法破译的； 难以领悟的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 向下/移除/取消 + to write in code + able

etymology.story: 1802, from in- (1) "not" + decipherable (see decipher (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: in + 移动/迁移 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: "to pass into a place as a new inhabitant or resident," especially "to move to a country where one is not a native, for the purpose of settling permanently there," 1620s, from Latin immigratum, past participle of imigrare "to remove, go into, move in," from assimilated form of in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + migrare "to move" (see migration).

meanings.definitionCn: 器械， 器具， 设备， 装置； 机构， 组织； 器官

etymology.story: 1620s, from Latin apparatus "tools, implements, equipment; preparation, a preparing," noun of state from past participle stem of apparare "prepare," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + parare "make ready" (see pare)..

meanings.definitionCn: 腐烂， 腐朽； 衰退， 衰落
偶然的； 附带发生的， 伴随而来的

morphology.mnemonic: 落于/发生 + …的人/处于…状态 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: "casual, occurring casually in connection with something else; of minor importance," 1640s, from Medieval Latin incidentalis, from incidens (see incident (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 使正式就任； 使了解， 传授； 使入伍

etymology.story: late 14c., "introduce, initiate, especially into office or employment," from Latin inductus, past participle of inducere "to lead into, introduce" (see induce).

meanings.definitionCn: 断裂， 折断； 分裂

morphology.mnemonic: 打破 + 行为/过程/结果

meanings.definitionCn: 变小， 变少， 减轻

etymology.story: 1812, "position of a champion," from champion (n.) + -ship.
1922, originally of industrial processes; see re- + cycle (v.).

Old English mersc, merisc "marsh, swamp," from Proto-Germanic *marisko (source also of Old Frisian and Old Saxon marsk "marsh," Middle Dutch mersch, Dutch mars, German Marsch, Danish marsk), probably from Proto-Germanic *mari- "sea" (see mere (n.))..

"precious stone next in hardness to a diamond," mid-13c., from Old French saphir (12c.) and directly from Latin sapphirus (source also of Spanish zafir, Italian zaffiro), from Greek sappheiros "blue stone" (the gem meant apparently was not the one that now has the name, but perhaps rather "lapis lazuli," the modern sapphire being perhaps signified by Greek hyakinthos), from a Semitic source (compare Hebrew sappir "sapphire"), but probably not ultimately from Semitic.

meanings.definitionCn: 漂流； 漂泊； 大意， 主旨； 倾向， 趋势

morphology.mnemonic: 人类 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: "science of the natural history of man," 1590s, originally especially of the relation between physiology and psychology, from Modern Latin anthropologia or coined independently in English from anthropo- + -logy.

late 14c., "gaping void," from Old French chaos (14c.) or directly from Latin chaos, from Greek khaos "abyss, that which gapes wide open, is vast and empty," from *khnwos, from PIE root *gheu- "to gape, yawn" (source also of Greek khaino "I yawn," Old English ginian, Old Norse ginnunga-gap; see yawn (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 大量的， 充足的， 丰富的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French abundant and directly from Latin abundantem (nominative abundans) "overflowing," present participle of abundare "to overflow" (see abound).

meanings.definitionCn: 吸收； 全神贯注， 专心致志

morphology.mnemonic: 远离/脱离 + sorb + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1590s, from Latin absorptionem (nominative absorptio), noun of action from past participle stem of absorbere "swallow up" (see absorb)..
late 14c., from Latin pigmentum "coloring matter, pigment, paint," figuratively "ornament," from stem of pingere "to color, paint" (see paint (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 预报； 预测， 预料

morphology.mnemonic: 在前/预先 + that which is cast

meanings.definitionCn: 变坏， 恶化； 变质

etymology.story: 1640s (as a past participle adjective, 1570s), from Late Latin deterioratus, past participle of deteriorare "get worse, make worse," from Latin deterior "worse, lower, inferior, meaner," contrastive of *deter "bad, lower," from PIE *de-tero-, from demonstrative stem *de- (see de).

meanings.definitionCn: 认出， 识别； 认可

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 知道/认识

etymology.story: early 15c., "resume possession of land," back-formation from recognizance, or else from Old French reconoiss-, stem of reconoistre "to know again, identify, recognize," from Latin recognoscere "acknowledge, recall to mind, know again; examine; certify," from re- "again" (see re-) + cognoscere "to get to know, recognize" (see cognizance).
"to cut in two," 1640s, from Modern Latin bisectus, from Latin bi- "two" (see bi-) + secare "to cut" (see section (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 引起， 导致； 诱使， 劝诱； 感应

etymology.story: formerly also enduce, late 14c., "to lead by persuasions or other influences," from Latin inducere "lead into, bring in, introduce, conduct; persuade; suppose, imagine," from in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + ducere "to lead" (see duke (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 远离/脱离 + 标准/规范 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1835, displaced older abnormous (1742) and rival anormal (1835) under influence of Latin abnormis "deviating from a rule," from ab- "off, away from" (see ab-) + norma "rule" (see norm).

Old English wanian "make or become smaller gradually, diminish, decline, fade," from Proto-Germanic *wanen (source also of Old Saxon wanon, Old Norse vana, Old Frisian wania, Middle Dutch waenen, Old High German wanon "to wane, to grow less"), from *wano- "lacking," from PIE *we-no-, from root *eue- "to leave, abandon, give out" (see vain).

meanings.definitionCn: 敏捷的， 机灵的； 精明的

etymology.story: 1794, "extremely minute particle," from French molécule (1670s), from Modern Latin molecula, diminutive of Latin moles "mass, barrier" (see mole (3)).

meanings.definitionCn: 排水系统； 污水处理； 污水

etymology.story: 1832, from sewer (n.1) + -age..

meanings.definitionCn: 辞去， 辞职； 放弃； 顺从， 听从

etymology.story: late 14c., "give up, surrender, abandon, submit; relinquish," from Old French resigner "renounce, relinquish" (13c.), from Latin resignare "to check off, annul, cancel, give back, give up," from re- "opposite" (see re-) + signare "to make an entry in an account book," literally "to mark" (see sign (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 勤奋的， 刻苦的， 勤勉的

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French diligent (14c.), from Latin diligentem "attentive, assiduous" (see diligence).
"act of adding flavor," 1510s; "something added to a dish to impart flavor," 1570s, verbal noun from season (v.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 老练的， 见多识广的； 精密的； 复杂的， 先进的； 高雅的， 有教养的

morphology.mnemonic: 智慧 + …主义者/…专家 + ic + 使…/具有…的

meanings.definitionCn: 反常， 异常； 异常的人

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1570s, from Latin anomalia, from Greek anomalia "inequality," noun of quality from anomalos "uneven, irregular," from an-, privative prefix, "not" (see an- (1)) + homalos "even," from homos "same" (see same)..

meanings.definitionCn: 有骑士精神的； 彬彬有礼的， 殷勤的

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French chevaleros "knightly, noble, chivalrous," from chevalier (see chevalier; also compare chivalry).

meanings.definitionCn: 日期栏； 国际日期变更线

etymology.story: 1880, imaginary line down the Pacific Ocean on which the calendar day begins and ends, from date (n.1) + line (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 居住于， 定居； 存在， 在于

etymology.story: late 15c., "to settle," from Middle French resider (15c.) and directly from Latin residere "sit down, settle; remain behind, rest, linger; be left," from re- "back, again" (see re-) + sedere "to sit" (see sedentary).

"manufactured goods, goods for sale," Old English waru "article of merchandise," also "protection, guard," hence probably originally "object of care, that which is kept in custody," from Proto-Germanic *waro (source also of Swedish vara, Danish vare, Old Frisian were, Middle Dutch were, Dutch waar, Middle High German, German ware "goods"), from PIE *wer- (4) "to perceive, watch out for" (see ward (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 应获奖的； 优秀的， 典范性的
奖品；奖金；难能可贵的东西

etymology.story: "to estimate," 1580s, alteration of Middle English prisen "to prize, value" (late 14c.), from stem of Old French preisier "to praise" (see praise (v.)).

late 14c., "an occurrence, incident, event," from Old French accident (12c.), from Latin accidentem (nominative accidens), present participle of accidere "happen, fall out, fall upon," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + cadere "fall" (see case (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 海的；海生的；海上贸易的

etymology.story: 14c., "seacoast;" see marine (adj.).

1760, "deep gorge," from French ravin "a gully" (1680s, from Old French raviner "to pillage, sweep down, cascade"), and from French ravine "violent rush of water, gully worn by a torrent," from Old French ravine "violent rush of water, waterfall; avalanche; robbery, rapine," both ultimately from Latin rapina "act of robbery, plundering" (see rapine); sense influenced by Latin rapidus "rapid." Middle English ravine meant "booty, plunder, robbery" from c.

meanings.definitionCn: 不兼容的； 不协调的， 合不来的

etymology.story: "that cannot coexist or be conjoined," mid-15c., from Medieval Latin incompatibilis, from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + compatibilis (see compatible).
1670s, from French routine "usual course of action, beaten path" (16c.), from route "way, path, course" (see route (n.)) + noun suffix -ine (see -ine (1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 令人反感的， 讨厌的； 味道不佳的

root.meaning: aesthetic judgment, faculty of discerning and appreciating what is excellent

morphology.mnemonic: 不/分离 + aesthetic judgment, faculty of discerning and appreciating what is excellent + 充满…的

meanings.definitionCn: 关税； 价目表， 收费表

etymology.story: 1590s, "arithmetical table," also "official list of customs duties on imports or exports; law regulating import duties," from Italian tariffa "tariff, price, assessment," Medieval Latin tarifa "list of prices, book of rates," from Arabic ta'rif "information, notification, a making known; inventory of fees to be paid," verbal noun from arafa "he made known, he taught." Sense of "classified list of charges made in a business" is recorded from 1757.
early 15c., from French canal, chanel "water channel, tube, pipe, gutter" (12c.), from Latin canalis "water pipe, groove, channel," noun use of adjective from canna "reed" (see cane (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 征募； 从军， 参军； 谋取

etymology.story: also inlist, 1690s (trans.), 1753 (intrans.), from en- (1) "make, put in" + list (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 使有效， 使生效； 证实

root.meaning: sufficiently supported by facts or authority, well-grounded

morphology.mnemonic: sufficiently supported by facts or authority, well-grounded + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1640s, from Medieval Latin validatus, past participle of validare "to make valid," from validus (see valid).

meanings.definitionCn: 动态的； 动力的； 充满活力的

morphology.mnemonic: 力量 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French sorplus "remainder, extra" (12c., Modern French surplus), from Medieval Latin superplus "excess, surplus," from Latin super "over" (see super-) + plus "more" (see plus).
"in a state of decline or decay (from a former condition of excellence)," 1837, from French décadent, back-formation from décadence (see decadence).

meanings.definitionCn: 坡度； 倾斜度， 斜率； 梯度

etymology.story: "steep slope of a road or railroad," 1835, principally in American English, probably from grade (n.) by analogy of quotient, etc.

early 15c., "to forbid, prohibit," back-formation from inhibition or else from Latin inhibitus, past participle of inhibere "to hold in, hold back, keep back." Psychological sense (1876) is from earlier, softened meaning of "restrain, check, hinder" (1530s).

meanings.definitionCn: 亲密的，密切的；详尽的，精通的；个人隐私的
泄露， 透露； 揭发

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French desclos "open, exposed, plain, explicit," past participle of desclore (Modern French déclore) "open, break open, unlock, reveal," from des- (see dis-) + clore "to close" (see close (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 宗教； 宗教信仰； 宗派， 教派

etymology.story: 1610s, from French diagramme, from Latin diagramma, from Greek diagramma "geometric figure, that which is marked out by lines," from diagraphein "mark out by lines, delineate," from dia- "across, out" (see dia-) + graphein "write, mark, draw" (see -graphy).

meanings.definitionCn: 反叛的， 挑衅的， 公然违抗的

etymology.story: 1837, from French défiant, present participle of défier (see defy).

meanings.definitionCn: 惨事， 灾难； 悲剧

etymology.story: late 14c., "play or other serious literary work with an unhappy ending," from Old French tragedie (14c.), from Latin tragedia "a tragedy," from Greek tragodia "a dramatic poem or play in formal language and having an unhappy resolution," apparently literally "goat song," from tragos "goat" + oide "song" (see ode).
"compound of oxygen with another element," 1790, from French oxide (1787), coined by G.

meanings.definitionCn: 惊人的， 令人吃惊的
用图说明；制图；记录，跟踪；计划
唤起， 引起， 激起

etymology.story: 1620s, from French évoquer or directly from Latin evocare "call out, rouse, summon" (see evocation).

meanings.definitionCn: 出席， 参加； 照料， 看管； 专心， 注意； 陪同
铲， 铁铲； 挖掘机

etymology.story: "fetus in utero at an early stage of development," mid-14c., from Medieval Latin embryo, properly embryon, from Greek embryon "a young one," in Homer, "young animal," later, "fruit of the womb," literally "that which grows," from assimilated form of en "in" (see en- (2)) + bryein "to swell, be full.".
"reckoning of numbers," late 14c., verbal noun from account (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 结盟； 排整齐； 校准； 使一致

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + one's occupation, branch of business

etymology.story: early 15c., "to copulate" (of wolves, dogs), literally "to range (things) in a line," from Middle French aligner, from Old French alignier "set, lay in line," from à "to" (see ad-) + lignier "to line," from Latin lineare, from linea (see line (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 安全的，有保障的；可靠的；确定的；
醉， 醉酒； 极度兴奋

morphology.mnemonic: in + 毒 + ic + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: Old English wæps, wæsp "wasp," altered (probably by influence of Latin vespa) from Proto-Germanic *wabis- (source also of Old Saxon waspa, Middle Dutch wespe, Dutch wesp, Old High German wafsa, German Wespe, Danish hveps), from PIE *wopsa-/*wospa- "wasp" (source also of Latin vespa, Lithuanian vapsa, Old Church Slavonic vosa "wasp," Old Irish foich "drone"), perhaps from *webh- "weave" (see weave (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 天篷； 罩盖， 遮篷； 天蓬似的树阴

etymology.story: "electrically neuter particle of the atom," 1921, coined by U.S.
1610s, "pertaining to movement," from French locomotif, from Latin loco "from a place" (ablative of locus "place;" see locus) + Late Latin motivus "moving" (see motive).

"great beyond measure," early 15c., from Old French immense (mid-14c.), from Latin immensus "immeasurable, boundless," also used figuratively, from assimilated form of in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + mensus "measured," past participle of metiri "to measure" (see measure (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 途径， 手段； 大街
腾出， 空出； 离， 退

etymology.story: 1640s, "to make void, to annul," from Latin vacatus, past participle of vacare "be empty, be void" (see vain).

1570s, from Latin inhaerentem (nominative inhaerens), present participle of inhaerere "be closely connected with, be inherent," literally "adhere to, cling to," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + haerere "to stick" (see hesitation).

meanings.definitionCn: 画笔； 刷子； 轻碰； 小冲突

morphology.mnemonic: a favoring circumstance + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: 1590s, formed in English from advantage, or else from French avantageux (15c.), from avantage (see advantage).
late 14c., "allotted, predestined," past participle adjective from give (v.).

"grass mown," Old English heg (Anglian), hieg, hig (West Saxon) "grass cut or mown for fodder," from Proto-Germanic *haujam (source also of Old Norse hey, Old Frisian ha, Middle Dutch hoy, German Heu, Gothic hawi "hay"), literally "that which is cut," or "that which can be mowed," from PIE *kau- "to hew, strike" (source also of Old English heawan "to cut;" see hew).

meanings.definitionCn: 侵占； 侵蚀， 蚕食

etymology.story: late 14c., "acquire, get," from Old French encrochier "seize, fasten on, hang on (to), cling (to); hang up, suspend," literally "to catch with a hook," from en- "in" (see en- (1)) + croc "hook," from Old Norse krokr "hook" (see crook (n.)).
"spoken," 1570s, past participle adjective from pronounce (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 灭亡； 湮灭， 毁灭； 老化

etymology.story: mid-13c., from periss- present participle stem of Old French perir "perish, be lost, be shipwrecked" (12c.), from Latin perire "to be lost, perish," literally "to go through," from per- "through, completely, to destruction" (see per) + ire "to go" (see ion).

meanings.definitionCn: 清楚的， 确切的； 肯定的

etymology.story: 1550s, from Latin definitus "defined, bounded, limited," past participle of definire (see define).

meanings.definitionCn: 规定， 明确要求； 约定

etymology.story: 1620s, "bargain, make a contract" (intransitive), back-formation from stipulation, or else from Latin stipulatus, past participle of stipulari "exact (a promise), bargain for." Transitive sense of "demand as a condition" is from 1640s.

meanings.definitionCn: 小心翼翼的， 留神的， 警惕的

etymology.story: late 15c., with -y (2) + ware, from Old English wær "prudent, aware, alert, wary," from Proto-Germanic *waraz (source also of Old Norse varr "attentive," Gothic wars "cautious," Old Saxon giwar, Middle Dutch gheware, Old High German giwar, German gewahr "aware"), from PIE root *wer- (4) "to perceive, watch out for" (see ward (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 适合； 适应； 调整； 改编， 改写
一批，一组，一群；一批生产的量

etymology.story: Old English *bæcce "something baked," from bacan "bake" (see bake (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 公共马车， 驿站马车

etymology.story: also stage-coach, 1650s, from stage (n.) in a sense of "division of a journey without stopping for rest" (c.

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 足够 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French insufficient (14c.) or directly from Late Latin insufficientem (nominative insufficiens) "not sufficient," from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + sufficientem (see sufficient).

meanings.definitionCn: 固有的， 本质的， 内在的

etymology.story: late 15c., "interior, inward, internal," from Middle French intrinsèque "inner" (14c.), from Medieval Latin intrinsecus "interior, internal," from Latin intrinsecus (adv.) "inwardly, on the inside," from intra "within" (see intra-) + secus "along, alongside," from PIE *sekw-os- "following," suffixed form of root *sekw- (1) "to follow" (see sequel).

meanings.definitionCn: 有罪的， 犯罪的； 内疚的

etymology.story: Old English gyltig "offending, delinquent, criminal," from gylt (see guilt (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 遗迹， 残余； 丝毫， 一点儿

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + 星 + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: 1620s, "situated between or among the stars," in reference to the night sky (modern astronomical sense is from 1670s), from inter- "between" + Latin stella "star" (see stellar).
early 15c., "full of light," from Latin luminosus "shining, full of light," from lumen (genitive luminis) "light," related to lucere "to shine" (see light (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 将系牢，捆绑；猛烈打击；鞭打；猛烈抨击，严厉斥责
海的， 海事的； 航海的

etymology.story: 1540s, "of or pertaining to the sea," from Middle French maritime (16c.) or directly from Latin maritimus "of the sea, near the sea," from mare (genitive maris) "sea" (see mere (n.)) + Latin ending -timus, originally a superlative suffix (compare intimus "inmost," ultimus "last"), here denoting "close association with." Maritimes "seacoast regions of a country" is from 1590s; specifically of the southeasternmost provinces of Canada by 1926..

meanings.definitionCn: 发出咔嗒声；使紧张，使恐惧

etymology.story: "threads running lengthwise in a fabric," Old English wearp, from Proto-Germanic *warpo- (source also of Middle Low German warp, Old High German warf "warp," Old Norse varp "cast of a net"), from PIE *werp- "to turn, bend" (see warp (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 地下室， 地窖； 建筑物的底部

etymology.story: "lowest story of a building except the cellar," 1730, from base (v.) + -ment..

meanings.definitionCn: 侮辱的， 诽谤的； 造成伤害的， 有害的

morphology.mnemonic: to injure, hurt, harm + 具有…特征/名词化 + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: early 15c., "abusive," from Old French injurios "unjust; harmful" (14c., Modern French injurieux) and directly from Latin iniuriosus "unlawful, acting unjustly, wrongful, harmful," from iniuria "injustice, unlawful violence, insult" (see injury).

meanings.definitionCn: 圣歌；单调的吟唱；反复呼喊的话语

etymology.story: 1792, from French commune "small territorial divisions set up after the Revolution," from Middle French commune "free city, group of citizens" (12c.), from Medieval Latin communia, noun use of neuter plural of Latin adjective communis, literally "that which is common," from communis (see common (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 胡言乱语； 含糊不清地说； 喋喋不休
调节焦距； 使聚焦； 使集中

etymology.story: "servile parasite," 1826, apparently shortened from toad-eater "fawning flatterer" (1742), originally (1620s) "the assistant of a charlatan," who ate a toad (believed to be poisonous) to enable his master to display his skill in expelling the poison.

meanings.definitionCn: 乡村的； 淳朴的； 用粗糙木材制作的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin rusticus "of the country, rural; country-like, plain, simple, rough, coarse, awkward," from rus (genitive ruris) "open land, country" (see rural).
"molten rock issuing from a volcano," 1750, from Italian (Neapolitan or Calabrian dialect) lava "torrent, stream," traditionally said to be from Latin lavare "to wash" (see lave).

meanings.definitionCn: 最小的； 最低限度的

morphology.mnemonic: 小/突出 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: "smallest, least," 1660s, from Latin minimus (see minim) + -al (1)..

meanings.definitionCn: 生态； 生态学； 生态环境

etymology.story: 1873, oecology, "branch of science dealing with the relationship of living things to their environments," coined in German by German zoologist Ernst Haeckel (1834-1919) as Ökologie, from Greek oikos "house, dwelling place, habitation" (see villa) + -logia "study of" (see -logy).

meanings.definitionCn: 有毒的， 分泌毒液的； 恶意的， 狠毒的

morphology.mnemonic: bitter, virulent feeling or language + 具有…性质的
跑/进程 + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: late 13c., from Anglo-French corageous, Old French corageus (12c., Modern French courageux), from corage (see courage).

meanings.definitionCn: 谨慎的； 深谋远虑的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French prudent "with knowledge, deliberate" (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 分泌； 藏匿， 躲藏

etymology.story: 1707, back-formation from secretion.

early 14c., from en- (1) + close, and partially from Old French enclos, past participle of enclore "surround; confine; contain." Specific sense of "to fence in waste or common ground" for the purpose of cultivation or to give it to private owners is from c.
"survey, summary," 1934, American English, from over- + view (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 疾病； 弊端， 恶疾

etymology.story: early 14c., "discomfort, inconvenience," from Old French desaise "lack, want; discomfort, distress; trouble, misfortune; disease, sickness," from des- "without, away" (see dis-) + aise "ease" (see ease).
late 14c., notwiþstondynge, from not + present participle of the verb withstand.

meanings.definitionCn: 挥发性的， 易挥发的； 不稳定的； 反复无常的

etymology.story: 1590s "fine or light," also "evaporating rapidly" (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 种类的， 属的； 一般的， 普通的

etymology.story: 1670s, "belonging to a large group of objects," formed in English from Latin gener-, stem of genus "race, kind" (see genus) + -ic.

meanings.definitionCn: 签名； 宣传， 代言； 签署； 赞同， 支持
一样地，相似地；同样程度地

etymology.story: late 14c., "tutelary or moral spirit" who guides and governs an individual through life, from Latin genius "guardian deity or spirit which watches over each person from birth; spirit, incarnation; wit, talent;" also "prophetic skill," originally "generative power" (or "inborn nature"), from PIE *gen(e)-yo-, from root *gene- "to produce, give birth, beget" (see genus).

meanings.definitionCn: 基础设施， 基础结构

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之下/基础 + 建造 + 行为/过程/结果

etymology.story: 1887, from French infrastructure (1875); see infra- + structure (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 典型的， 有代表性的

etymology.story: "order forbidding ships from certain other nations from entering or leaving a nation's ports," 1590s, from Spanish embargo "seizure, arrest; embargo," noun of action from embargar "restrain, impede, arrest, embargo," from Vulgar Latin *imbarricare, from assimilated form of in- "into, upon" (see in- (2)) + *barra (see bar (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 枯萎， 凋谢； 衰退； 萎缩； 破灭

etymology.story: 1530s, alteration of Middle English wydderen "dry up, shrivel" (late 14c.), intransitive, apparently a differentiated and special use of wederen "to expose to weather" (see weather (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 引文， 引语， 语录； 报价

etymology.story: mid-15c., "numbering," later (1530s) "marginal notation," noun of action from quote (v.) or else from Medieval Latin quotationem (nominative quotatio), noun of action from past participle stem of quotare "to number." Meaning "an act of quoting" is from 1640s; that of "passage quoted" is from 1680s.

meanings.definitionCn: 夜行性的； 夜间发生的

morphology.mnemonic: 夜 + urn + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 15c., from Old French nocturnal "nightly, nocturnal," or directly from Late Latin nocturnalis, from Latin nocturnus "belonging to the night," from nox (genitive noctis) "night," cognate with Old English neaht (see night) + -urnus, suffix forming adjectives of time.

meanings.definitionCn: 臭名昭著的， 声名狼藉的

etymology.story: 1540s, "publicly known," from Medieval Latin notorius "well-known, commonly known," from Latin notus "known," past participle of noscere "come to know" (see know).

meanings.definitionCn: 作为例证的； 用做说明的； 解释性的

morphology.mnemonic: not well, poorly + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1640s, from illustrat-, past participle stem of Latin illustrare (see illustration) + -ive..

meanings.definitionCn: 使重叠， 使叠加； 添加

morphology.mnemonic: 超/在…之上 + in + 放置

etymology.story: 1787, back-formation from superimposition (1680s), or from super- + impose.

late 14c., infant, infaunt, "a child," also especially "child during earliest period of life, a newborn" (sometimes meaning a fetus), from Latin infantem (nominative infans) "young child, babe in arms," noun use of adjective meaning "not able to speak," from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + fans, present participle of fari "to speak," from PIE root *bha- (2) "to speak, tell, say" (see fame (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 人工制品， 手工艺品

etymology.story: 1821, artefact, "anything made by human art," from Italian artefatto, from Latin arte "by skill" (ablative of ars "art;" see art (n.)) + factum "thing made," from facere "to make, do" (see factitious).
1841, coined in Modern Latin by Sir Richard Owen, from comb.

early 15c., "spiked road barrier used for defense," from turn + pike (n.2) "shaft." Sense transferred to "horizontal cross of timber, turning on a vertical pin" (1540s), which were used to bar horses from foot roads.
mid-14c., from Old French depriver, from Medieval Latin deprivare, from Latin de- "entirely" (see de-) + privare "release from" (see private).
mid-17c., present participle adjective from tantalize.

meanings.definitionCn: 花的， 像花的； 饰以花的， 绘有花的

morphology.mnemonic: flower花（flor也作flour） + 统治 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1640s, "pertaining to Flora," from French floral (16c.), from Latin floralis "pertaining to Flora; of flowers" (see flora).

meanings.definitionCn: 害虫； 令人讨厌的人

etymology.story: 1550s (in imprecations, "a pest upon ____," etc.), "plague, pestilence," from Middle French peste (1530s), from Latin pestis "deadly contagious disease; a curse, bane," of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 昏厥， 昏倒； 痴迷

etymology.story: late 14c., "condition of babyhood," also "childhood, youth," from Anglo-French enfaunce and directly from Latin infantia "early childhood," from infantem "young child," literally "one unable to speak" (see infant).
1520s, from Middle French suprême (15c.) and directly from Latin supremus "highest," superlative of superus "situated above," from super "above" (see super-).

meanings.definitionCn: 哲学； 哲学体系， 思想体系； 人生观， 人生哲学

morphology.mnemonic: 爱 + 具有…特征/名词化

meanings.definitionCn: 令人不能容忍地； 肆无忌惮地

morphology.mnemonic: 超过/外部 + rage + 具有…性质的 + 以…方式/副词化

meanings.definitionCn: 期满终止， 失效； 衰退； 背弃
复兴， 振兴； 再生

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + gener + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1550s, back-formation from regeneration or else from Latin regeneratus, past participle of regenerare "bring forth again" (see regeneration).

meanings.definitionCn: 潜在的， 隐伏的； 潜伏的； 休眠的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "concealed, secret," from Latin latentem (nominative latens) "lying hid, concealed, secret, unknown," present participle of latere "lie hidden, lurk, be concealed," from PIE *late-, suffixed form of root *lādh- "to be hidden" (source also of Greek lethe "forgetfulness, oblivion," Old Church Slavonic lajati "to lie in wait for").

meanings.definitionCn: 预防， 预先阻止； 先发制人
极好的； 非常的， 极度的； 可怕的

etymology.story: 1660s, "frightening," from Latin terrificus "causing terror or fear, frightful," from terrere "fill with fear" (see terrible) + root of facere "to make" (see factitious).

1610s, "shallow recess in a wall," from French niche "recess (for a dog), kennel" (14c.), perhaps from Italian nicchia "niche, nook," from nicchio "seashell," said by Klein and Barnhart to be probably from Latin mitulus "mussel," but the change of -m- to -n- is not explained.

meanings.definitionCn: 祭拜； 崇拜， 崇敬
草地， 草坪； 操场
传染， 感染； 传染病

morphology.mnemonic: in + 做/制造 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "infectious disease; contaminated condition;" from Old French infeccion "contamination, poisoning" (13c.) and directly from Late Latin infectionem (nominative infectio) "infection, contagion," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin inficere "to spoil, to stain" (see infect).

meanings.definitionCn: 笨重的； 笨拙的； 沉闷的

morphology.mnemonic: to weigh a matter mentally + 具有…性质的

meanings.definitionCn: 顽固的， 倔强的； 难对付的

etymology.story: late 14c., of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 不精确的； 不严密的

etymology.story: 1804, from assimilated form of in- (1) "not, opposite of" + precise.

meanings.definitionCn: 阐明， 确切表达； 规划， 构想； 制定

morphology.mnemonic: 形状/形态 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: "to express in a formula," 1837, from formula + -ate (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 吹捧， 美化； 赞美， 赞扬

etymology.story: mid-14c., "praise, honor, extol" (God or a person), also "vaunt, be proud of, boast of; glorify oneself, be proud, boast;" from Old French glorefiier "glorify, extol, exalt; glory in, boast" (Modern French glorifier), from Late Latin glorificare "to glorify," from Latin gloria "fame, renown, praise, honor" (see glory (n.)) + -ficare, from facere "to make, do" (see factitious).

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + 移动/迁移 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1778, a back-formation from emigration, or else from Latin emigratus, past participle of emigrare "move away." In 19c.
"full of turns and curves," 1570s, from Latin sinuosus "full of curves, folds, or bendings," from sinus "curve, fold, bend" (see sinus).

meanings.definitionCn: 三角形； 三角形物体

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French triangle (13c.), from Latin triangulum "triangle," noun use of neuter of adjective triangulus "three-cornered, having three angles," from tri- "three" (see tri-) + angulus "corner, angle" (see angle (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 外星人；组织之外的人；外国人，

etymology.story: 1856, from French linguistique (1833); see linguist + -ic.

meanings.definitionCn: 卫生； 卫生设施， 卫生设备

etymology.story: 1848, irregularly formed from sanitary.
"a choice or select body, the best part," 1823, from French élite "selection, choice," from Old French eslite (12c.), fem.
1610s, literally "resembling a chub," from chub, the short, thick type of fish + -y (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 象牙； 象牙制品； 象牙色， 乳白色

etymology.story: 1640s, from Late Latin anomalus, from Greek anomalos "uneven, irregular" (see anomaly).

meanings.definitionCn: 不足的， 缺乏的； 有缺陷的， 不完善的

etymology.story: 1580s, from Latin deficientem (nominative deficiens), present participle of deficere "to desert, revolt, fail," from de- "down, away" (see de-) + facere "to do, perform" (see factitious)..
late 14c., earlier henne forth (late Old English); see hence + forth..
1680s, "greater weight," from Latin praeponderans, present participle of praeponderare "make heavier" (see preponderate).

meanings.definitionCn: 用链条拴住； 联号， 连锁店
有效的， 有效率的； 有能力的， 能胜任的

etymology.story: late 14c., "making, producing immediate effect, active, effective," from Old French efficient and directly from Latin efficientem (nominative efficiens) "effective, efficient, producing, active," present participle of efficere "work out, accomplish" (see effect).

meanings.definitionCn: 一致的； 稳定的； 调和的； 始终如一的

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 站立/设置 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1570s, "standing firm, standing still," from Latin consistentem (nominative consistens), present participle of consistere (see consist).

meanings.definitionCn: 反转；彻底转变，颠倒；撤销，废除；交换；
毁坏； 抢劫， 掠夺

etymology.story: 1610s, from French ravage "destruction" (see ravage (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 贫瘠的， 荒芜的； 不结果实的， 不育的
持久的， 永久的； 固定不变的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French permanent (14c.) or directly from Latin permanentem (nominative permanens) "remaining," present participle of permanere "endure, hold out, continue, stay to the end," from per- "through"  (see per) + manere "stay" (see mansion).

meanings.definitionCn: 鼓励，促进，助长；培养，抚育；领养

etymology.story: Old English *fostrian "to supply with food, nourish, support," from fostor "food, nourishment, bringing up," from Proto-Germanic *fostra-, from extended form of PIE root *pa- "to protect; feed" (see food).

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 取/抓 + acle

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French receptacle (14c.) and directly from Latin receptaculum "place to receive and store things," from receptare, frequentative of recipere "to hold, contain" (see receive).
1837, American English, from summary + -ize.

early 15c., "gentleness, lightness," from Middle French facilité "easiness, ease," from Latin facilitatem (nominative facilitas) "easiness, ease, fluency, willingness," from facilis "easy" (see facile).

meanings.definitionCn: 群居的； 合群的， 爱社交的

etymology.story: 1660s, "disposed to live in flocks" (of animals), from Latin gregarius "pertaining to a flock; of the herd, of the common sort, common," from grex (genitive gregis) "flock, herd," from PIE *gre-g-, reduplicated form of root *ger- (1) "to gather together, assemble" (source also of Sanskrit gramah "heap, troop;" Greek ageirein "to assemble," agora "assembly;" Latin gremium "bosom, lap;" Old Church Slavonic grusti "handful," gramota "heap;" Lithuanian gurgulys "chaos, confusion," gurguole "crowd, mass").

meanings.definitionCn: 面露喜色；发射，播送；照射
束， 波束， 光线； 梁； 笑容， 喜色

etymology.story: mid-13c., "bird's bill," from Old French bec "beak," figuratively "mouth," also "tip or point of a nose, a lance, a ship, a shoe," from Latin beccus (source also of Italian becco, Spanish pico), said by Suetonius ("De vita Caesarum" 18) to be of Gaulish origin, perhaps from Gaulish beccus, possibly related to Celtic stem bacc- "hook." Or there may be a link in Old English becca "pickax, sharp end." Jocular sense of "human nose" is from 1854 (but also was used mid-15c.

meanings.definitionCn: 小说； 虚构， 编造

morphology.mnemonic: 塑造，虚构 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., ficcioun, "that which is invented or imagined in the mind," from Old French ficcion "dissimulation, ruse; invention, fabrication" (13c.) and directly from Latin fictionem (nominative fictio) "a fashioning or feigning," noun of action from past participle stem of fingere "to shape, form, devise, feign," originally "to knead, form out of clay," from PIE *dheigh- "to build, form, knead" (source also of Old English dag "dough;" see dough).

meanings.definitionCn: 替换， 取代； 归还， 把…放回原处

etymology.story: 1590s, "to restore to a previous place or position," from re- "back, again" + place (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 航空， 航空学； 飞行

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1866, from French aviation, noun of action from stem of Latin avis "bird" (see aviary).

meanings.definitionCn: 约会， 约定； 任命， 委派； 职位

morphology.mnemonic: put (someone) in charge + 结果/过程/状态

etymology.story: early 15c., "an agreement," also "a fixing of a date for official business," from Middle French apointement, from apointer (see appoint).
"combine or bring together" (two or more things), 1825, from synthesis + -ize.

1849 (n.) "a stage of a journey by ox wagon;" 1850 (v.), "to travel or migrate by ox wagon," from Afrikaans trek, from Dutch trekken "to march, journey," originally "to draw, pull," from Middle Dutch trecken (cognate with Middle Low German trecken, Old High German trechan "to draw").

meanings.definitionCn: 压碎，挤压；挤进，塞入；制止
软式墙网球， 壁球； 果汁汽水

etymology.story: "to crush, squeeze," early 14c., squachen, from Old French esquasser, escasser "to crush, shatter, destroy, break," from Vulgar Latin *exquassare, from Latin ex- "out" (see ex-) + quassare "to shatter" (see quash "to crush").
1680s (Ben Jonson used it 1610s as a mock-name), from play (n.) + wright (n.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 打扰； 中断； 阻碍
打拍子，指挥；敲打；打败，胜过；跳动
跳动；节拍，节奏；有规律的敲击

etymology.story: "defeated, overcome by effort," c.

"capable of being done, accomplished or carried out," mid-15c., from Anglo-French faisible, from Old French faisable "possible, that may be done; easy, convenient," from fais-, stem of faire "do, make," from Latin facere "to make, do, perform" (see factitious).

meanings.definitionCn: 开处方； 规定； 指示

etymology.story: "to write down as a direction," mid-15c., from Latin praescribere "write beforehand" (see prescription).
also proof-read, 1878, back-formation from proofreader.

morphology.mnemonic: 力量/能力 + …的人/处于…状态 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., "possible" (as opposed to actual), from Old French potenciel and directly from Late Latin potentialis "potential," from Latin potentia "power, might, force;" figuratively "political power, authority, influence," from potens "powerful" (see potent).

meanings.definitionCn: 总的，毛的；粗俗的，粗野的；令人不快的

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 站立/设置

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French resister "hold out against" (14c.), from Latin resistere "to make a stand against, oppose; to stand back; withstand," from re- "against" (see re-) + sistere "take a stand, stand firm" (see assist).
1819 (implied in archived), from archives.

meanings.definitionCn: 解除武装； 裁军； 消除， 消解

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French desarmer (11c.), from des- (see dis-) + armer "to arm" (see arm (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 外推； 推断， 推知

etymology.story: 1862 (in a Harvard observatory account of the comet of 1858), from extra- + ending from interpolate.

meanings.definitionCn: 刺激； 激励， 激发

etymology.story: 1610s, "rouse to action," from Latin stimulatus, past participle of stimulare (see stimulation).

meanings.definitionCn: 互惠， 互助； 互惠主义

etymology.story: 1766, from French réciprocité (18c.), from reciproque, from Latin reciprocus, past participle of reciprocare (see reciprocal)..
late 13c., "to cause an eclipse of," from Old French eclipser, from eclipse (see eclipse (n.)).Figurative use from 1570s.

meanings.definitionCn: 正方形的；成直角的，方的；平方的
使成正方形； 求平方； 使打成平局
粗心的， 疏忽的； 无忧无虑的； 不关心的， 冷漠的

etymology.story: Old English carleas "free from anxiety; unconcerned," from care (n.) + -less; a compound probably from Proto-Germanic.

meanings.definitionCn: 浸泡， 浸湿， 浸透

etymology.story: Old English socian (intransitive) "to soak, to lie in liquid," from Proto-Germanic *sukon (source also of West Flemish soken), possibly from PIE *sug-, from root *seue- (2) "to take liquid" (see sup (v.2)).
1670s, from counter- + act (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 荒废的， 空无一人的； 被离弃的， 被遗弃的
上下班往返， 经常往返； 代偿； 减刑

etymology.story: mid-15c., "to change, transform," from Latin commutare "to often change, to change altogether," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + mutare "to change" (see mutable).

mid-14c., "to prop, support with a prop;" of obscure etymology though widespread in Germanic (Middle Dutch schooren "to prop up, support," Old Norse skorða (n.) "a piece of timber set up as a support").

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 统治/首领 + …主义者/…专家

etymology.story: 1670s; see anarchy + -ist.
1782, from French classifier, from classe (see class (n.)) + -fier (see -fy).
late 14c., from Latin altitudinem (nominative altitudo) "height, altitude," from altus "high" (see old)..

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 寻找/询问

meanings.definitionCn: 群， 群体； 殖民地； 聚居地

morphology.mnemonic: 殖民/定居 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: late 14c., "ancient Roman settlement outside Italy," from Latin colonia "settled land, farm, landed estate," from colonus "husbandman, tenant farmer, settler in new land," from colere "to inhabit, cultivate, frequent, practice, tend, guard, respect," from PIE root *kwel- (1) "move around" (source of Latin -cola "inhabitant;" see cycle (n.)).

"four-wheeled vehicle to carry heavy loads," late 15c., from Middle Dutch wagen, waghen, from Proto-Germanic *wagnaz (source also of Old English wægn, Modern English wain, Old Saxon and Old High German wagan, Old Norse vagn, Old Frisian wein, German Wagen), from PIE *wogh-no-, suffixed form of root *wegh- "to carry, to move" (source also of Sanskrit vahanam "vessel, ship," Greek okhos, Latin vehiculum, Old Church Slavonic vozŭ "carriage, chariot," Russian povozka, Lithuanian va žis "a small sledge," Old Irish fen, Welsh gwain "carriage, cart;" see weigh).

meanings.definitionCn: 首次演出； 初次亮相

etymology.story: 1751, from French début "first appearance," a figurative use from débuter "make the first stroke at billiards," also "to lead off at bowls" (a game akin to bowling), 16c., from but "mark, goal," from Old French but "end" (see butt (n.3)).

meanings.definitionCn: 支持， 维护； 维持

etymology.story: late 14c., "measurement, size," from Latin dimensionem (nominative dimensio) "a measuring," noun of action from past participle stem of dimetri "to measure out," from dis- (see dis-) + metiri "to measure" (see measure (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 思乡病， 乡愁； 怀旧之情， 恋旧

etymology.story: 1770, "severe homesickness considered as a disease," Modern Latin, coined 1668 in a dissertation on the topic at the University of Basel by scholar Johannes Hofer (1669-1752) as a rendering of German heimweh "homesickness" (for which see home + woe).

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + 保持/服务

etymology.story: early 13c., from Old French deservir (Modern French desservir) "deserve, be worthy of, earn, merit," from Latin deservire "serve well," from de- "completely" (see de-) + servire "to serve" (see serve).

meanings.definitionCn: 过程； 程序， 手续； 工序， 制作法

etymology.story: early 14c., "fact of being carried on" (as in in process), from Old French proces "a journey; continuation, development; legal trial" (13c.) and directly from Latin processus "a going forward, advance, progress," from past participle stem of procedere "go forward" (see proceed).

meanings.definitionCn: 发芽，抽条，生长；迅速出现，涌现出

etymology.story: "shoot of a plant, sprout; a twig," Old English sprota, from the verb (see sprout (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 恢复； 修复； 归还， 交还

etymology.story: late 14c., from Anglo-French viscous and directly from Late Latin viscosus "sticky," from Latin viscum "anything sticky, birdlime made from mistletoe, mistletoe," probably from PIE root *weis- "to melt away, flow" (used of foul or malodorous fluids); see virus..
1806, from nostalgia + -ic.

meanings.definitionCn: 可供替代的； 非传统的
可供选择的事物；选择的自由，选择的余地

morphology.mnemonic: altern + 使…/具有…的 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1620s, in rhetoric, from Medieval Latin alternativus (see alternative (adj.)).

late 15c., "sodium carbonate," an alkaline substance extracted from certain ashes (now made artificially), from Italian sida (or Medieval Latin soda) "a kind of saltwort," from which soda was obtained, of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 山崩， 塌方； 压倒性胜利

etymology.story: also land-slide, 1841, "fall or down-slide of a mass of rock, earth, etc.

tract of level or undulating grassland in North America, by 1773, from French prairie "meadow, grassland," from Old French praerie "meadow, pastureland" (12c.), from Vulgar Latin *prataria, from Latin pratum "meadow," originally "a hollow." The word existed in Middle English as prayere, but was lost and reborrowed to describe the American plains.

meanings.definitionCn: 擦破， 擦痛； 恼怒， 焦躁
咀嚼； 思量， 熟思

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Middle French domicile (14c.), from Latin domicilium, perhaps from domus "house" (see domestic) + colere "to dwell" (see colony).

meanings.definitionCn: 徒步的； 缺乏想象力的
急忙， 匆忙， 仓促
解释， 描述， 叙述； 账目； 账户
放射性的， 有辐射的

morphology.mnemonic: 射线/放射 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1898, from French radio-actif, coined by Pierre and Marie Curie from radio-, comb.

meanings.definitionCn: 天真的； 清白的； 无恶意的

morphology.mnemonic: public house with lodging + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: mid-14c., "doing no evil; free from sin, guilt, or moral wrong," from Old French inocent "harmless; not guilty; pure" (12c.), from Latin innocentem (nominative innocens) "not guilty, blameless; harmless; disinterested," from in- "not" (see in- (1)) + nocentem (nominative nocens), present participle of nocere "to harm," from *nok-s-, suffixed form of PIE root *nek- (1) "death" (see necro-).
1570s, from Late Latin alphabetum (Tertullian), from Greek alphabetos, from alpha + beta.
"a step in walking," especially a long one, from Old English stride "a stride, a step," from the root of stride (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 前进，向前移动；发展，进步；促进；预付
赏识； 鉴赏， 欣赏； 感激

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 价格/价值 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1650s, "to esteem or value highly," from Late Latin appretiatus, past participle of appretiare "to set a price to" (see appraise).

meanings.definitionCn: 集合，召集；恢复，重新振作
柔软的； 柔和的； 不强烈的； 心肠软的； 软性的

etymology.story:
Old English softe, earlier sefte, "gentle, mild-natured; easeful, comfortable, calm, undisturbed; luxurious," from West Germanic *samfti, from Proto-Germanic *samftijaz "level, even, smooth, gentle, soft" (source also of Old Saxon safti, Old High German semfti, German sanft; and from a variant form with -ch- for -f-, Middle Dutch sachte, Dutch zacht, German sacht), from root *som- "fitting, agreeable."

From c.

meanings.definitionCn: 虚构的； 假的； 假装的， 虚伪的

morphology.mnemonic: 塑造，虚构 + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: 1610s, "artificial, counterfeit;" 1620s, "existing only in imagination," from Medieval Latin fictitius, a misspelling of Latin ficticius "artificial, counterfeit," from fictus "feigned, fictitious, false," past participle of fingere "to shape, form, devise, feign" (see fiction).

meanings.definitionCn: 主要的；最初的；最好的；首先的
使准备好； 事先指点
智力， 理解力； 知识分子

etymology.story: "the sum of the cognitive facilities (except sense or sense and imagination), the capacity for reasoning truth," late 14c.

early 14c., from Old French ambler "walk as a horse does," from Latin ambulare "to walk, to go about, take a walk," perhaps a compound of ambi- "around" (see ambi-) and -ulare, from PIE root *el- "to go" (source also of Greek ale "wandering," alaomai "wander about;" Latvian aluot "go around or astray").

1530s, in reference to subjects of sovereigns or governments, from Middle French loyal, from Old French loial, leal "of good quality; faithful; honorable; law-abiding; legitimate, born in wedlock," from Latin legalem, from lex "law." In most cases it has displaced Middle English leal, which is from the same French source.

morphology.mnemonic: to esteem (something) more than others + ance

etymology.story: mid-15c., "advancement in position or status;" 1650s as "act of prefering," from Middle French preference (14c., Modern French préférence), from Medieval Latin preferentia, from past participle stem of Latin praeferrere (see prefer).

meanings.definitionCn: 主流， 主要倾向； 主流思想

etymology.story: also main-stream, main stream, "principal current of a river," 1660s, from main (adj.) + stream (n.); hence, "prevailing direction in opinion, popular taste, etc.," a figurative use first attested in Carlyle (1831).
late 14c., from Old French opportunite (13c.) and directly from Latin opportunitatem (nominative opportunitas) "fitness, convenience, suitableness, favorable time," from opportunus (see opportune).

meanings.definitionCn: 放荡的， 放纵的， 道德沦丧的

etymology.story: late 14c., "loose, negligent, morally or religiously lax," from Latin dissolutus "loose, disconnected," past participle of dissolvere "loosen up" (see dissolve).

meanings.definitionCn: 法院，法庭；宫廷，朝廷；球场
献殷勤， 讨好； 招致； 求爱

etymology.story: "woo, offer homage," as one does at court, 1570s; see court (n.).

early 13c., from Old French chapele (12c., Modern French chapelle), from Medieval Latin cappella "chapel, sanctuary for relics," literally "little cape," diminutive of Late Latin cappa "cape" (see cap (n.)); by tradition, originally in reference to the sanctuary in France in which the miraculous cape of St.

meanings.definitionCn: 弄歪； 扭曲， 歪曲

etymology.story: 1580s, from Latin distortus, past participle of distorquere "to twist different ways, distort," from dis- "completely" + torquere "to twist" (see torque (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 次要的； 辅助的， 附设的； 附属的

morphology.mnemonic: 沉降/补助 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1540s, from Latin subsidiarius "belonging to a reserve, of a reserve, reserved; serving to assist or supplement," from subsidium "a help, aid, relief, troops in reserve" (see subsidy).

meanings.definitionCn: 冒险，投机；投机活动，风险项目
相互关联， 紧密联系

root.meaning: stand in some relation; have reference or respect

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + stand in some relation; have reference or respect

etymology.story: also inter-relate, 1831 (implied in interrelated), transitive, "bring into reciprocal relation," from inter- "between" + relate (v.).
late 14c., from Old French corrosif (13c.), from corroder (see corrode)..

meanings.definitionCn: 热情； 积极性； 热衷的事物

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French discerner (13c.) "distinguish (between), separate" (by sifting), and directly from Latin discernere "to separate, set apart, divide, distribute; distinguish, perceive," from dis- "off, away" (see dis-) + cernere "distinguish, separate, sift" (see crisis).

1560s, "disposition to favor," with -ty + obsolete adjective propense "inclined, prone" (1520s), from Latin propensus, past participle of propendere "incline to, hang forward, hang down, weigh over," from pro- "forward" (see pro-) + pendere "hang" (see pendant)..

meanings.definitionCn: 乞求， 恳求， 请求
易弯曲的； 柔韧的； 灵活的， 可变通的

etymology.story: early 15c., "capable of being bent; mentally or spiritually pliant," from Middle French flexible or directly from Latin flexibilis "that may be bent, pliant, flexible, yielding;" figuratively "tractable, inconstant," from flex-, past participle stem of flectere "to bend," which is of uncertain origin.
1580s, from Middle French agile (14c.) and directly from Latin agilis "nimble, quick," from agere "to move, drive" (see act (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + full满，全 + 有点…/像…

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French repleniss-, extended present participle stem of replenir "to fill up," from re-, here probably an intensive prefix, + -plenir, from Latin plenus "full" (see plenary).

meanings.definitionCn: 顺从的， 服从劝导的； 有服从义务的

etymology.story: 1590s, "liable," from Anglo-French amenable, Middle French amener "answerable" (to the law), from à "to" (see ad-) + mener "to lead," from Latin minare "to drive (cattle) with shouts," variant of minari "threaten" (see menace (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 描写， 描述， 描绘

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + paint画，描绘

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin depictus, past participle of depingere "to portray, paint, sketch; describe, imagine," from de- "down" (see de-) + pingere "to paint" (see paint (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 不破坏生态平衡的， 可持续的； 足可支撑的； 可忍受的

etymology.story: 1610s, "bearable," from sustain + -able.

meanings.definitionCn: 单足跳行；齐足跳行；跳上

etymology.story: 1702, "to leave one's companions," from Latin secedere "go away, withdraw, separate; rebel, revolt" (see secession).

root.meaning: sum of qualities that define a person

morphology.mnemonic: sum of qualities that define a person + ic

etymology.story: adjective and noun both first attested 1660s, from character + -istic on model of Greek kharakteristikos.
"part of a country distant from the coast," 1796, from interior (adj.); meaning "internal part, inside" is from 1828.

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + method or pattern of weaving

etymology.story: 1570s (trans.), hybrid from inter- + weave (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 唤起， 唤醒； 激起， 鼓舞； 激怒

etymology.story: mid-15c., intransitive probably from Anglo-French or Old French reuser, ruser, originally used in English of hawks shaking the feathers of the body, but like many hawking terms it is of obscure origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 能力； 性能； 容量； 潜质

etymology.story: 1580s, from capable + -ity.

meanings.definitionCn: 构成整体所必需的， 不可或缺的； 完整的

etymology.story: late 15c., "of or pertaining to a whole; intrinsic, belonging as a part to a whole," from Middle French intégral (14c.), from Medieval Latin integralis "forming a whole," from Latin integer "whole" (see integer).

meanings.definitionCn: 集会； 集会者； 装配， 安装； 立法机构， 议会

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 相似/聚集 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: early 15c., "to chip" (intransitive, of stone); from Old English forcippian "to pare away by cutting, cut off," verbal form of cipp "small piece of wood" (see chip (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 复制； 再制， 再生

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 折叠/交织 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: early 15c., "repeat," from Latin replicatus, past participle of replicare (see reply).

meanings.definitionCn: 感情的， 情绪的； 引起情感的； 易动感情的

morphology.mnemonic: em + 状态/行为/过程 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1821, "pertaining to emotion," from emotion + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 明显的， 值得注意的

etymology.story: 1796, "worthy of notice," from notice (n.) + -able.

early 14c., transitive and intransitive, from Old French assembler "come together, join, unite; gather" (11c.), from Latin assimulare "to make like, liken, compare; copy, imitate; feign, pretend," later "to gather together," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + simulare "to make like" (see simulation).

meanings.definitionCn: 冬眠； 休眠， 蛰伏

etymology.story: 1660s, "action of passing the winter" (of plants, insect eggs, etc.), from Latin hibernationem (nominative hibernatio) "the action of passing the winter," noun of action from past participle stem of hibernare "to winter, pass the winter, occupy winter quarters;" related to hiems "winter," from PIE *gheim- "snow, winter" (source also of Sanskrit heman "in winter," Hittite gimmanza, Greek kheima, Old Church Slavonic zima, Lithuanian žiema "winter").

meanings.definitionCn: 过渡， 过渡时期； 转变

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin transitionem (nominative transitio) "a going across or over," noun of action from past participle stem of transire "go or cross over" (see transient)..

meanings.definitionCn: 前进， 进发； 行进， 齐步走； 游行示威
行进，行军；示威游行；进行曲；进行，进展

etymology.story: "to walk with regular tread," early 15c., from Middle French marcher "to march, walk," from Old French marchier "to stride, march," originally "to trample, tread underfoot," perhaps from Frankish *markon or some other Germanic source related to obsolete Middle English march (n.) "borderland" (see march (n.2)).

meanings.definitionCn: 爆炸；发出尖响；猛烈抨击
一阵； 爆炸； 冲击波； 响声

etymology.story: Old English blæstan "to blow, belch forth," from the root of blast (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 击退， 驱逐； 排斥； 抵制， 拒绝； 使反感， 使厌恶

etymology.story: early 15c., "to drive away, remove," from Old French repeller or directly from Latin repellere "to drive back," from re- "back" (see re-) + pellere "to drive, strike" (see pulse (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 宣传， 推广， 宣扬

etymology.story: 1902; see public (adj.) + -ize.

root.meaning: to be scorekeeper, to keep the score in a game or contest

morphology.mnemonic: 不足/在…之下 + to be scorekeeper, to keep the score in a game or contest

etymology.story: 1771, "to draw a line under," from under + score (v.).
also sea-shore, 1520s, from sea + shore (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 系列， 连续； 连续剧

etymology.story: 1610s, "a number or set of things of one kind arranged in a line," from Latin series "row, chain, series, sequence, succession," from serere "to join, link, bind together, arrange, attach, put; join in speech, discuss," from PIE root *ser- (3) "to line up, join" (source also of Sanskrit sarat- "thread," Greek eirein "to fasten together in rows," Gothic sarwa (plural) "armor, arms," Old Norse sörve "necklace of stringed pearls," Old Irish sernaid "he joins together," Welsh ystret "row").
early 17c., from Medieval Latin cultivatus, past participle of cultivare "to cultivate," from Late Latin cultivus "tilled," from Latin cultus (see cult).

meanings.definitionCn: 茂盛； 茁壮成长； 兴旺， 繁荣
富有想象力的； 创新的

etymology.story: late 14c., ymaginatyf, "pertaining to imagination; forming images, given to imagining," from Old French imaginatif and directly from Medieval Latin imaginativus, from imaginat-, stem of Latin imaginari "picture to oneself" (see imagine).

meanings.definitionCn: 踏车； 枯燥乏味的工作

etymology.story: invented (and named) 1822; originally an instrument of prison discipline; from tread (v.) + mill (n.1).

meanings.definitionCn: 代表； 表现； 描绘； 象征

etymology.story: late 14c., "to bring to mind by description," also "to symbolize, serve as a sign or symbol of; serve as the type or embodiment of;" from Old French representer "present, show, portray" (12c.), from Latin repraesentare "make present, set in view, show, exhibit, display," from re-, intensive prefix (see re-), + praesentare "to present," literally "to place before" (see present (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 分派， 派遣； 发送

etymology.story: 1510s, "to send off in a hurry," from a word in Spanish (despachar "expedite, hasten") or Italian (dispacciare "to dispatch").

meanings.definitionCn: 文集， 文献， 汇编； 语料库

etymology.story: (plural corpora), late 14c., from Latin corpus, literally "body" (see corporeal).

meanings.definitionCn: 不便的； 让人不舒服的

etymology.story: late 14c., "injurious, dangerous," also "absurd, illogical" (senses now obsolete), from Latin inconvenientem (nominative inconveniens) "unsuitable, not accordant, dissimilar," from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + convenientem (see convenient).

meanings.definitionCn: 改编成戏剧； 戏剧化， 戏剧性地表现

etymology.story: 1780s, "to adopt for the stage," see drama (Greek stem dramat-) + -ize.
1550s, from Middle French absurde (16c.), from Latin absurdus "out of tune; foolish" (see absurdity).
"superior officer of a religious house or order," late Old English, from Medieval Latin prior "superior officer," noun use of Latin adjective meaning "former, superior" (see prior (adj.)).

late 15c., "shot, missiles;" later "a stroke in drawing, a short line" (1580s), from Middle French trait "line, stroke, feature, tract," from Latin tractus "drawing, drawing out, dragging, pulling," later "line drawn, feature," from past participle stem of trahere "to pull, draw" (see tract (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 路标， 地标； 〈喻〉里程碑

etymology.story: Old English landmearc "object set up to mark the boundaries of a kingdom, estate, etc.," from land (n.) + mearc (see mark (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 拱顶；金库，保险库；撑杆跳

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French concave (14c.) or directly from Latin concavus "hollow, arched, vaulted, curved," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + cavus "hollow" (see cave (n.))..

mid-14c., from Old French daulphin, from Medieval Latin dolfinus, from Latin delphinus "dolphin," from Greek delphis (genitive delphinos) "dolphin," related to delphys "womb," perhaps via notion of the animal bearing live young, or from its shape, from PIE *gwelbh-.

meanings.definitionCn: 制订， 颁布； 法律， 法规

morphology.mnemonic: 使成为/放入 + 做/驱动 + 结果/过程/状态

etymology.story: 1766, "passing of a bill into law," from enact + -ment.

late Old English planete, from Old French planete (Modern French planète), from Late Latin planeta, from Greek planetes, from (asteres) planetai "wandering (stars)," from planasthai "to wander," of unknown origin, possibly from PIE *pele- (2) "flat, to spread" on notion of "spread out." So called because they have apparent motion, unlike the "fixed" stars.
1670s, from Modern Latin ornithologia (1590s); see ornitho- + -logy..

meanings.definitionCn: 必备的， 作为先决条件的

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + 再/重新/回 + quire

etymology.story: 1630s (n.) "something required beforehand," 1650s (adj.), "required beforehand," both from pre- + requisite..

meanings.definitionCn: 数，计算；算入；看做，认为；值得考虑，有重要意义
计数， 计算； 总数； 罪状
缓慢行进； 爬行； 悄悄地移动
落叶的； 非永久的， 短暂的

etymology.story: 1680s, from Latin deciduus "that which falls off," from decidere "to fall off," from de- "down" (see de-) + cadere "to fall" (see case (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 学者； 奖学金获得者

etymology.story: Old English scolere "student," from Medieval Latin scholaris, noun use of Late Latin scholaris "of a school," from Latin schola (see school (n.1)).
1530s, "mutually opposed, at variance," from Late Latin contradictorius "containing a contradiction or objection," from contradictus, past participle of contradicere (see contradiction).

meanings.definitionCn: 为…伴奏； 陪伴， 陪同； 伴随， 和…一起发生

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + com + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: early 15c., "to be in company with," from Middle French accompagner, from Old French acompaignier (12c.) "take as a companion," from à "to" (see ad-) + compaignier, from compaign (see companion).

meanings.definitionCn: 即刻的； 当前的； 最接近的； 紧接的， 紧靠的

etymology.story: late 14c., "intervening, interposed;" early 15c., "with nothing interposed; direct," also with reference to time, "without delay, instant," from Old French immediat (14c.), from Late Latin immediatus "without anything between," from assimilated form of in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + mediatus "in the middle" (see mediate)..

meanings.definitionCn: 明白易懂的； 明了的

etymology.story: late 15c., from Latin perspicuus "transparent, clear, evident," from perspicere "look through, look closely at" (see perspective).

morphology.mnemonic: 好/善 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: mid-15c., "helpful, advantageous," from Middle French bénéficial and directly from Latin beneficialis "pertaining to a favor," from beneficium (see benefice).

meanings.definitionCn: 过失， 责备； 责任

etymology.story: early 13c., from Old French blasme "blame, reproach; condemnation," a back-formation from blasmer (see blame (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 困惑； 混乱， 混淆

etymology.story: late 13c., "overthrow, ruin," from Old French confusion (11c.) "disorder, confusion, shame," from Latin confusionem (nominative confusio) "a mingling, mixing, blending; confusion, disorder," noun of action from confundere "to pour together," also "to confuse" (see confound).

meanings.definitionCn: 陈词滥调， 老生常谈

etymology.story: 1812, "dullness," from French platitude "flatness, vapidness" (late 17c.), from Old French plat "flat" (see plateau (n.)); formed on analogy of latitude, etc.

1727, from Latin auditorium "lecture room," literally "place where something is heard," noun use of neuter of auditorius (adj.) "of or for hearing," from auditus, past participle of audire "to hear" (see audience); also see -ory.

meanings.definitionCn: 会议； 开庭； 开庭期； 一节

etymology.story: late 14c., "periodical sitting of a court," from Old French session "act or state of sitting; assembly," from Latin sessionem (nominative sessio) "act of sitting; a seat; loitering; a session," noun of action from past participle stem of sedere "to sit" (see sedentary).

meanings.definitionCn: 触觉的； 有触觉的； 可感触的

etymology.story: 1610s, "perceptible to touch," from French tactile (16c.) and directly from Latin tactilis "tangible, that may be touched," from tactus, past participle of tangere "to touch" (see tangent (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 外来的， 外在的， 非本质的

etymology.story: "not of the essence or inner nature of a thing," 1540s, from French extrinsèque, from Late Latin extrinsecus (adj.) "outer," from Latin extrinsecus (adv.) "outwardly, on the outside; from without, from abroad," from exter "outside" + in, suffix of locality, + secus "beside, alongside," originally "following," from PIE *sekw-os "following," from root *sekw- (1) "to follow" (see sequel)..

meanings.definitionCn: 平淡的， 乏味的； 清淡的； 沉稳的

etymology.story: 1660s, from Italian blando "delicate," or Old French bland "flattering, complimentary," both from Latin blandus "smooth-talking, flattering, alluring," perhaps from PIE *mlad-, nasalized variant of *meld-, extended form of root *mel- (see melt).
popular name for a bacterium, 1878, from French microbe, "badly coined ...

meanings.definitionCn: 招待会； 反响； 接纳， 接待； 接收

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 取/抓 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., in astrology, "effect of two planets on each other;" sense of "act of receiving" is recorded from late 15c., from Latin receptionem (nominative receptio) "a receiving," noun of action from past participle stem of recipere (see receive).

meanings.definitionCn: 木材， 木料； 杂物
独唱， 独奏， 独舞

etymology.story: 1690s, "piece of music for one voice or instrument," from Italian solo, literally "alone," from Latin solus "alone" (see sole (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 显示器； 监视器； 班长
学费； 教学， 讲授

etymology.story: early 15c., "protection, care, custody," from Anglo-French tuycioun (13c.), Old French tuicion "guardianship," from Latin tuitionem (nominative tuitio) "a looking after, a caring for, watching over, protection, guardianship," from tuitus, past participle of tueri "to look after" (see tutor (n.)).
temperature scale, 1753, named for Gabriel Daniel Fahrenheit (1686-1736), Prussian physicist who proposed the scale in 1714.

meanings.definitionCn: 可触摸的， 可感知的； 明确的， 确凿的； 有形的， 实际的

etymology.story: 1580s, "capable of being touched," from Middle French tangible and directly from Late Latin tangibilis "that may be touched," from Latin tangere "to touch" (see tangent (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 充满， 拥塞； 充血

etymology.story: early 15c., "to bring together" (transitive), from Latin congestus, past participle of congerere "to bring together, pile up," from com- "together" (see com-) + gerere "to carry, perform" (see gest).
1580s, "sending of mail by post;" 1650s as "cost of sending something by mail," from post (n.3) + -age.
late 14c., from Old French caverne (12c.) "cave, vault, cellar," from Late Latin caverna "cave," from Latin cavus "hollow" (see cave (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 物质的， 身体上的； 重要的； 实质性的， 客观存在的

etymology.story: late 14c., "substance, matter from which a thing is made," from material (adj.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 贴近， 紧挨， 毗连

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + unite, become associated, form an alliance

meanings.definitionCn: 壮观的场面； 壮观的演出

morphology.mnemonic: 看/观察 + acul + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: 1680s, from Latin spectaculum "a sight, show" (see spectacle) + -ar.

meanings.definitionCn: 独特的； 罕见的； 奇怪的， 古怪的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "belonging exclusively to one person," from Latin peculiaris "of one's own (property)," from peculium "private property," literally "property in cattle" (in ancient times the most important form of property), from pecu "cattle, flock," related to pecus "cattle" (see pecuniary).

meanings.definitionCn: 颜色； 色度， 色调； 信仰， 观点； 形式， 样子
习惯用语； 术语； 风格， 特色

etymology.story:
1580s, "form of speech peculiar to a people or place;" meaning "phrase or expression peculiar to a language" is from 1620s; from Middle French idiome (16c.) and directly from Late Latin idioma "a peculiarity in language," from Greek idioma "peculiarity, peculiar phraseology" (Fowler writes that "A manifestation of the peculiar" is "the closest possible translation of the Greek word"), from idioumai "to appropriate to oneself," from idios "personal, private," properly "particular to oneself."

This is from PIE *swed-yo-, suffixed form of root *s(w)e-, pronoun of the third person and reflexive (referring back to the subject of a sentence), also used in forms denoting the speaker's social group, "(we our-)selves" (source also of Sanskrit svah, Avestan hva-, Old Persian huva "one's own," khva-data "lord," literally "created from oneself;" Greek hos "he, she, it;" Latin suescere "to accustom, get accustomed," sodalis "companion;" Old Church Slavonic svoji "his, her, its," svojaku "relative, kinsman;" Gothic swes "one's own;" Old Norse sik "oneself;" German Sein; Old Irish fein "self, himself")..

meanings.definitionCn: 外交官； 有手腕的人

etymology.story: 1813, from French diplomate, a back-formation from diplomatique (see diplomatic) on model of aristocrate from aristocratique..

meanings.definitionCn: 总统， 校长， 主席

morphology.mnemonic: sit in front of, + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: late 14c., "appointed governor of a province; chosen leader of a body of persons," from Old French president and directly from Latin praesidentum (nominative praesidens) "president, governor," noun use of present participle of praesidere "to act as head or chief" (see preside).

meanings.definitionCn: 肌肉的； 肌肉发达的； 强健的， 强壮的

etymology.story: 1680s, "pertaining to muscles," from Latin musculus (see muscle (n.)) + -ar.

meanings.definitionCn: 主要产品；原材料；主食；订书钉

etymology.story: 1889, shortening of memorandum (q.v.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 诡计， 奸计； 策略

meanings.definitionCn: 协作， 合作； 勾结， 通敌

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 劳动/工作 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1802, from French collaborateur, from Latin collaboratus, past participle of collaborare "work with," from com- "with" (see com-) + labore "to work" (see labor (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 肤浅的， 浅薄的； 表面的， 外表的， 外层的

morphology.mnemonic: 超/在…之上 + 做/制造 + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 执行的， 实施的； 行政的
执行者；管理人员；行政负责人

morphology.mnemonic: to inflict capital punishment + 有…倾向的

meanings.definitionCn: 融化， 熔化； 溶化； 消散
连接到无线电设备；勾住
钩， 吊钩； 钩状物

late 14c., "a foot race; an ancient measure of length," from Latin stadium "a measure of length; a course for foot-racers" (commonly one-eighth of a Roman mile or a little over 600 English feet; translated in early English Bibles by furlong), from Greek stadion "a measure of length; a race-course, a running track," especially the track at Olympia, which was one stadion in length.

meanings.definitionCn: 冷冻， 冷藏； 使冷却， 使变冷

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 冷 + er + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1530s, back-formation from refrigeration, or else from Latin refrigeratus, past participle of refrigerare "make cool or cold."
early 15c., "chance, accident; incidental charge," from casual (adj.) on model of royalty, penalty, etc.

root.meaning: having to do with outer space

morphology.mnemonic: having to do with outer space + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 标准，规格；测量仪表，计量器

meanings.definitionCn: 地带， 地区； 腰带， 皮带； 带状物

etymology.story: 1670s, "absurd thing;" 1680s, "words or actions meant to invoke ridicule," from French ridicule, noun use of adjective (15c.), or from Latin ridiculum "laughing matter, joke," from noun use of neuter of ridiculus (see ridiculous)..
mid-15c., "to declare or state formally or solemnly," from Old French protester, from Latin protestari "declare publicly, testify, protest" (see protest (n.)).

root.meaning: be conscious inwardly of (one's state or condition) is from 1680s. Meaning

morphology.mnemonic: be conscious inwardly of (one's state or condition) is from 1680s. Meaning  + 有…倾向的

"pertaining to the faculty of the soul that receives and analyzes sensory information;" from Old French sensitif "capable of feeling" (13c.) and directly from Medieval Latin sensitivus "capable of sensation," from Latin sensus, past participle of sentire "feel perceive" (see sense (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 空的； 闲置的； 茫然的， 空虚的
买得起， 担负得起； 冒险做； 提供， 给予

meanings.definitionCn: 在地下， 往地下； 秘密地， 不公开地
地下的； 秘密的， 不公开的 记 组合词： under + ground → 地下的

morphology.mnemonic: 不足/在…之下 + instruct thoroughly in the basics

保护， 掩蔽； 躲避
解剖； 解剖构造； 剖析

mid-13c., "convent headed by an abbot or abbess," from Anglo-French abbeie, Old French abaïe, from Late Latin abbatia, from abbas (genitive abbatis); see abbot..
"that can be surrendered or given up," 1610s; see alien (adj.) + -able.

"unmoved by danger, undaunted," 1690s, from French intrépide (16c.) and directly from Latin intrepidus "unshaken, undaunted, not alarmed," from in- "not" (see in- (1)) + trepidus "alarmed," from PIE *trep-(1) "to tremble" (see trepidation).

morphology.mnemonic: gener + 与…有关的/形容词 + ize
