late 13c., "to bring up, nurture" (a child, a feeling, etc.), from Old French norriss-, stem of norrir "raise, bring up, nurture, foster; maintain, provide for" (12c., Modern French nourrir), from Latin nutrire "to feed, nurse, foster, support, preserve," from *nutri (older form of nutrix "nurse"), literally "she who gives suck," from PIE *nu- (from root *(s)nau- "to swim, flow, let flow," hence "to suckle;" see nutriment) + fem.
late 14c., "to make longer," also "to grow longer," from length + -en (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 碰，撞，撞击；侵袭；突然想到；给…以深刻印象；罢工；敲响

etymology.story: Old English strican (past tense strac, past participle stricen) "pass lightly over, stroke, smooth, rub," also "go, move, proceed," from Proto-Germanic *strikan- (source also of Old Norse strykva "to stroke," Old Frisian strika, Middle Dutch streken, Dutch strijken "to smooth, stroke, rub," Old High German strihhan, German streichen), from PIE root *streig- "to stroke, rub, press" (see strigil).

meanings.definitionCn: 制服， 征服； 抑制， 克制

etymology.story: late 14c., "to conquer and reduce to subjection," from Old French souduire, but this meant "deceive, seduce," from Latin subducere "draw away, lead away, carry off; withdraw" (see subduce).

meanings.definitionCn: 肯定的； 积极的； 确凿的； 自信的， 乐观的； 正电的

morphology.mnemonic: 分开/离开 + 群体 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1540s, from Latin segregatus, past participle of segregare "set apart, lay aside; isolate; divide," literally "separate from the flock," from *se gregare, from se "apart from" (see secret (n.)) + grege, ablative of grex "herd, flock" (see gregarious).
1550s, from Cuban Spanish maiz, from Arawakan (Haiti) mahiz..

meanings.definitionCn: 细心的， 小心翼翼的

etymology.story: 1530s, "fearful, timid," from Latin meticulosus "fearful, timid," literally "full of fear," from metus "fear, dread, apprehension, anxiety," of unknown origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 租金额； 租赁， 出租

morphology.mnemonic: 肾 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: mid-14c., "rent roll;" late 14c., "income from rents," from Anglo-French rental, Medieval Latin rentale; see rent (n.1) + -al (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 前景，前途；可能性；景象；期望

etymology.story: "electronic system for locating objects by means of radio waves," 1941, acronym (more or less) from radio detecting and ranging.

meanings.definitionCn: 拒绝给， 不给； 抑制； 忍住
运动的， 运动引起的； 动力学的

etymology.story: "relating to muscular motion," 1841, from Greek kinetikos "moving, putting in motion," from kinetos "moved," verbal adjective of kinein "to move" (see cite).
1610s, "to indicate specially," from special (adj.) + -ize, perhaps on model of French spécialiser.

meanings.definitionCn: 转动， 旋转； 轮流

etymology.story: 1794, intransitive, back-formation from rotation.

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 声音 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French consonant (13c.), from Latin consonantem (nominative consonans), present participle of consonare (see consonant (n.))..
1550s, from en- (1) "make, put in" + gulf (n.) or else from Old French engolfer.

meanings.definitionCn: 遗产， 继承物； 继承； 遗传

etymology.story: late 14c., enheritaunce "fact of receiving by hereditary succession;" early 15c.

meanings.definitionCn: 爬行动物， 爬虫类； 卑鄙的人

etymology.story: late 14c., "creeping or crawling animal," from Old French reptile (early 14c.) and directly from Late Latin reptile, noun use of neuter of reptilis (adj.) "creping, crawling," from rept-, past participle stem of repere "to crawl, creep," from PIE root *rep- "to creep, crawl" (source also of Lithuanian replioju "to creep").
1793, "little box," from French cassette, from Middle French casset, diminutive of Old North French casse "box" (see case (n.2)).

meanings.definitionCn: 接受得快的； 善于接受的， 能接纳的

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 取/抓 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1540s, from Medieval Latin receptivus, from Latin recipere (see receive).

meanings.definitionCn: 刀刃， 刀片； 叶片； 草叶； 桨叶

etymology.story: Old English blæd "a leaf," but also "a leaf-like part" (of spade, oar, etc.), from Proto-Germanic *bladaz (source also of Old Frisian bled "leaf," German Blatt, Old Saxon, Danish, Dutch blad, Old Norse blað), from PIE *bhle-to-, suffixed form (past participle) of *bhel- (3) "to thrive, bloom," possibly identical with *bhel- (2) "to blow, swell" (see bole).

meanings.definitionCn: 溶液； 解答， 解决

morphology.mnemonic: 松开/解决 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "a solving or being solved," from Old French solucion "division, dissolving; explanation; payment" or directly from Latin solutionem (nominative solutio) "a loosening or unfastening," noun of action from past participle stem of solvere "to loosen, untie, solve, dissolve" (see solve).

meanings.definitionCn: 用透视法缩小； 缩短， 节略

morphology.mnemonic: 在前/预先 + make short + 使变成/变得

meanings.definitionCn: 分开， 叉开； 分歧； 偏离

etymology.story: 1660s, from Modern Latin divergere "go in different directions," from dis- "apart" (see dis-) + vergere "to bend, turn" (see verge (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 对立的， 反对的， 对抗的

etymology.story: 1580s, from Latin opponentem (nominative opponens), present participle of opponere "oppose, object to," literally "set against, set opposite," from ob "against" (see ob-) + ponere "to put, set, place" (see position (n.))..

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + celer + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1520s, from Latin acceleratus, past participle of accelerare "to hasten,  quicken," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + celerare "hasten," from celer "swift" (see celerity).

meanings.definitionCn: 徽章； 标记， 象征

etymology.story: 1580s, "relief, raised ornament on vessels, etc.," from Latin emblema "inlaid ornamental work," from Greek emblema (genitive emblematos) "an insertion," from emballein "to insert," literally "to throw in," from assimilated form of en "in" (see en- (2)) + ballein "to throw" (see ballistics).

morphology.mnemonic: look upon, observe + 没有…的/无…的

etymology.story: "indifferent," 1590s, from regard (n.) + -less.

meanings.definitionCn: 没收， 充公， 征用

etymology.story: 1550s, originally, "to appropriate for the treasury," from Latin confiscatus, past participle of confiscare, from com- "together" (see com-) + fiscus "public treasury," literally "money basket" (see fiscal).

meanings.definitionCn: 子女的； 子孙后代的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Late Latin filialis "of a son or daughter," from Latin filius "son," filia "daughter," possibly from a suffixed form of PIE root *bheue- "to be, exist, grow" (see be), though *dhe(i)- "to suck, suckle" (see fecund) "is more likely" [Watkins]..

morphology.mnemonic: 大气/气象 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: "science of the atmosphere, weather forecasting," 1610s, from French météorologie and directly from Greek meteorologia "treatise on celestial phenomena, discussion of high things," from meteoron, literally "thing high up" (see meteor), + -logia "treatment of" (see -logy)..

meanings.definitionCn: 发生， 出现； 存在于

etymology.story: 1520s, "meet, meet in argument," from Middle French occurrer "happen unexpectedly" or directly from Latin occurrere "run to meet, run against, befall, present itself," from ob "against, toward" (see ob-) + currere "to run" (see current (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 说明， 解释； 加插图于； 显示…存在

etymology.story: 1520s, "light up, shed light on;" 1610s, "educate by means of examples," back-formation from illustration, and in some cases from Latin illustratus, past participle of illustrare "light up, make light, illuminate." Sense of "provide pictures to explain or decorate" is 1630s.
1952, acronym for self-contained underwater breathing apparatus..

meanings.definitionCn: 确定的，最后的，决定性的；

suffix.meaning: thing or animal spoken about before.

morphology.mnemonic: de + thing or animal spoken about before. + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French definitif (12c.), from Latin definitivus "explanatory, definitive," from past participle stem of definire (see define).

meanings.definitionCn: 无价值之物，废物；拙劣的文学作品；没用的人
剧团； 马戏团； 芭蕾舞团

etymology.story: 1825, "company, band," especially of performers, actors, dancers, etc., from French troupe "company" (see troop (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 运输， 运送； 中转； 转变； 交通运输系统

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin transitus, past participle of transire "go or cross over" (see transient).
Old English sar "bodily pain or injury, wound; sickness, disease; state of pain or suffering," from root of sore (adj.).

meanings.definitionCn: 位置； 职位； 立场； 姿势， 姿态； 见解

morphology.mnemonic: 放置 + 状态/行为/过程

meanings.definitionCn: 缩短， 缩减， 削减

etymology.story: late 15c., from Middle French courtault "made short," from court "short" (Old French cort, from Latin curtus; see curt) + -ault pejorative suffix of Germanic origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 暗淡的光； 容光焕发； 喜悦
有专利的； 显而易见的

etymology.story: late 14c., in letters patent, literally "open letter," from Old French patente, from Latin patentum (nominative patens) "open, lying open," present participle of patere "lie open, be open," from PIE *pete- "to spread" (see pace (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 鞘， 外壳； 套； 护皮； 紧身连衣裙

etymology.story: Old English sceað, scæð, from Proto-Germanic *skaithiz (source also of Old Saxon scethia, Old Norse skeiðir (plural), Old Frisian skethe, Middle Dutch schede, Dutch schede, Old High German skaida, German scheide "a sheath, scabbard"), according to OED, possibly from root *skei- "divide, split" (see shed (v.)) on notion of a split stick with the sword blade inserted.

1760 as a botanical term for the fertilizing element of flowers (from Linnæus, 1751), earlier "fine flour" (1520s), from Latin pollen "mill dust; fine flour," related to polenta "peeled barley," and pulvis (genitive pulveris) "dust," from PIE root *pel- (1) "dust; flour" (source also of Greek poltos "pap, porridge," Sanskrit pálalam "ground seeds," Lithuanian pelenai, Old Church Slavonic popelu, Russian pépelŭ "ashes")..

meanings.definitionCn: 重新塑造， 改造； 改编； 改变

etymology.story: 1789, from re- "back, again" + model (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 沟； 槽； 唱片上的纹路
证书， 证明书； 文凭， 结业证书

morphology.mnemonic: 确定 + 使…化/造成 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: early 15c., "action of certifying," from French certificat, from Medieval Latin certificatum "thing certified," noun use of neuter past participle of certificare (see certify).

meanings.definitionCn: 疏散； 散布， 散开

morphology.mnemonic: 分开/二 + 散，撒 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1821; see disperse + -al (2)..

1610s, "one's share or portion," from Latin quantum (plural quanta) "as much as, so much as; how much? how far? how great an extent?" neuter singular of correlative pronominal adjective quantus "as much" (see quantity).

meanings.definitionCn: 球拍； 喧嚷， 吵闹； 勒索， 诈骗
弄脏， 弄乱； 搞糟
酸的； 馊的； 乖戾的， 尖酸刻薄的； 酸痛的
使变形； 改造， 改革； 转换

morphology.mnemonic: 穿越/转变 + 形状/形态

etymology.story: mid-14c., "change the form of" (transitive), from Old French transformer (14c.), from Latin transformare "change in shape, metamorphose," from trans- "across" (see trans-) + formare "to form" (see form (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 巨大； 重要性； 星等， 星球的亮度
真的， 非人造的； 真诚的， 诚实的

etymology.story: 1590s, "natural, not acquired," from Latin genuinus "native, natural, innate," from root of gignere "beget" (see genus), perhaps influenced in form by contrasting adulterinus "spurious." [Alternative etymology is from Latin genu "knee," from a supposed ancient custom of a father acknowledging paternity of a newborn by placing it on his knee.] Meaning "really proceeding from its reputed source" is from 1660s.

meanings.definitionCn: 鼓动， 煽动； 激怒， 使激动， 使不安； 搅动

etymology.story: 1580s, "to disturb," from Latin agitatus, past participle of agitare "to put in constant motion, drive onward, impel," frequentative of agere "to move, drive" (see act (n.)).
type of electromagnetic wave, 1931, coined in English from micro- + wave (n.).
1847, applied to a species of large apes (Troglodytes gorilla) by U.S.

meanings.definitionCn: 免疫的， 有免疫力的； 不受影响的； 免除的， 豁免的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "free, exempt" (from taxes, tithes, sin, etc.), from Latin immunis "exempt from public service, untaxed; unburdened, not tributary," from assimilated form of in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + munis "performing services" (compare municipal), from PIE *moi-n-es-, suffixed form of root *mei- (1) "to change" (see mutable).

1748, "pamphlet; short written work stitched together," from French brochure "a stitched work," from brocher "to stitch" (sheets together), from Old French brochier "to prick, jab, pierce," from broche "pointed tool, awl" (see broach (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 缺点， 负面； 底侧； 下降趋势

etymology.story: 1680s, "underside," from down (adv.) + side.

meanings.definitionCn: 等式， 方程式； 等同， 相等； 均衡

morphology.mnemonic: equi + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., a term in astrology (from French équation, 14c.); general sense of "action of making equal" is from 1650s, from Latin aequationem (nominative aequatio) "an equal distribution, a sharing in common," noun of state from past participle stem of aequare (see equal (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 给人深刻印象的； 感人的

morphology.mnemonic: in + device for pressing cloth + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1590s, "capable of being easily impressed" (a sense now rare or obsolete), from impress (v.1) + -ive.

morphology.mnemonic: 向前/支持 + 看/观察 + …者/…器

etymology.story: also prospecter, 1846 in the mining sense; agent noun from prospect (v.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 使恶化， 使加重； 混合

etymology.story: late 14c., originally compouned, past participle of compounen (see compound (v.)).
1550s, from dis- "opposite of" + credit (v.).

morphology.mnemonic: extra + 顺序/等级 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin extraordinarius "out of the common order," from extra ordinem "out of order," especially the usual order, from extra "out" (see extra-) + ordinem, accusative of ordo "order" (see order (n.)).

root.meaning: be conscious inwardly of (one's state or condition) is from 1680s. Meaning

morphology.mnemonic: be conscious inwardly of (one's state or condition) is from 1680s. Meaning  + …者/…器 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1749, "pertaining to sense or sensation," from Latin sensorius, from sensus, past participle of sentire "to perceive, feel" (see sense (n.))..

Old English mere "sea, ocean; lake, pool, pond, cistern," from Proto-Germanic *mari (source also of Old Norse marr, Old Saxon meri "sea," Middle Dutch maer, Dutch meer "lake, sea, pool," Old High German mari, German Meer "sea," Gothic marei "sea," mari-saiws "lake"), from PIE *mori- "sea" (source also of Latin mare, Old Church Slavonic morje, Russian more, Lithuanian mares, Old Irish muir, Welsh mor "sea," Gaulish Are-morici "people living near the sea")..

early 14c., "area outside a town or city," whether agricultural or residential but most frequently residential, from Old French suburbe "suburb of a town," from Latin suburbium "an outlying part of a city" (especially Rome), from sub "below, near" (see sub-) + urbs (genitive urbis) "city" (see urban).

meanings.definitionCn: 绷紧的； 肌肉结实的； 结构严谨的， 紧凑的

etymology.story: mid-13c., tohte "stretched or pulled tight," possibly from tog-, past participle stem of Old English teon "to pull, drag," from Proto-Germanic *tugn, from PIE *deuk- "to lead" (see duke (n.)), which would connect it to tow (v.) and tie.

meanings.definitionCn: 简化， 使简单， 使单纯

etymology.story: 1650s, from French simplifier "to make simpler" (15c.), from Medieval Latin simplificare "to simplify," from Latin simplex "simple" (see simplex) + root of facere "to make" (see factitious).

"reducing friction," 1809, from Latin lubricantem (nominative lubricans), present participle of lubricare "to make slippery or smooth," from lubricus "slippery; easily moved, sliding, gliding;" figuratively "uncertain, hazardous, dangerous; seductive," from PIE *sleubh- "to slip, slide" (see sleeve)..
"join or fasten with glue," late 14c., from Old French gluer, gluier "smear with glue; join together," from glu "glue, birdlime" (see glue (n.)).
1540s, with reference to eye sockets; 1839 with reference to heavenly bodies; from orbit (n.) + -al (1)..

early 14c., soudur, from Old French soldure, soudeure, from souder, originally solder, "to consolidate, close, fasten together, join with solder" (13c.), from Latin solidare "to make solid," from solidus "solid" (see solid (adj.)).

late 14c., envolupen, "be involved" (in sin, crime, etc.), from Old French envoleper "envelop, cover; fold up, wrap up" (10c., Modern French envelopper), from en- "in" (see en- (1)) + voloper "wrap up," which is of uncertain origin, perhaps Celtic (see Gamillscheg, Diez) or Germanic ("Century Dictionary").

meanings.definitionCn: 结实的； 坚定的， 坚决的
反射， 映现； 显示， 表明； 仔细考虑， 深思

etymology.story: late 14c., "turn or bend back;" early 15c., "to divert, to turn aside, deflect," from Old French reflecter (14c.), from Latin reflectere "bend back, turn back" (see reflection).
"of the nature of an addition from without," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 正在进行的；不间断的；继续存在的

etymology.story: also on-going, 1877, from on + going (see go)..

meanings.definitionCn: 全套装备； 全套服装
无知的， 愚昧的； 无知觉的

etymology.story: late 14c., "lacking wisdom or knowledge; unaware," from Old French ignorant (14c.), from Latin ignorantem (nominative ignorans) "not knowing, ignorant," present participle of ignorare "not to know, to be unacquainted; mistake, misunderstand; take no notice of, pay no attention to," from assimilated form of in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + Old Latin gnarus "aware, acquainted with" (source also of Classical Latin noscere "to know," notus "known"), from Proto-Latin suffixed form *gno-ro-, suffixed form of PIE root *gno- "to know" (see know).

meanings.definitionCn: 勒索， 敲诈， 强夺

etymology.story: 1520s (as a past participle adj.

meanings.definitionCn: 踩碎； 践踏， 摧残

etymology.story: late 14c., "to walk heavily," frequentative form of tramp (v.) + -el (3).

meanings.definitionCn: 可以忽略的， 微不足道的； 无关紧要的

etymology.story: "capable of being neglected," 1819, from negligence + -ible.

meanings.definitionCn: 挫折， 阻碍； 倒退

etymology.story: also set-back, 1670s, "reversal, check to progress," from set (v.) + back (adv.).

"sprinkle liquid in drops," 1520s, from Middle Dutch sprayen, from Proto-Germanic *sprewjan (source also of German sprühen "to sparkle, drizzle," Spreu "chaff," literally "that which flies about"), from extended form of PIE root *sper- (4) "to sow, scatter" (see sprout (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 区别， 辨别； 使有别于， 成为…的特征； 看清， 听出； 使杰出， 使著名

etymology.story: 1560s, from Middle French distinguiss-, stem of distinguer, or directly from Latin distinguere "to separate between, keep separate, mark off, distinguish," perhaps literally "separate by pricking," from dis- "apart" (see dis-) + -stinguere "to prick" (compare extinguish and Latin instinguere "to incite, impel").

meanings.definitionCn: 原始的， 最初的； 基本的

morphology.mnemonic: 第一/首要 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., from Late Latin primordialis "first of all, original," from Latin primordium "a beginning, the beginning, origin, commencement," from primus "first" (see prime (adj.)) + stem of ordiri "to begin" (see order (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 耕种， 务农， 农牧业

etymology.story: also hair-cut, 1887, "act of cutting the hair," from hair (n.) + cut (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 装饰性的， 做装饰用的

morphology.mnemonic: decor + 使…/具有…的 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French decoratif, from decorat-, past participle stem of Latin decorare (see decorate)..

meanings.definitionCn: 尖的， 尖角的； 尖锐的， 尖刻的

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + to grow long, lengthen, + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: "to make long or longer," 1530s, from Late Latin elongatus, past participle of elongare "to prolong, protract" (see elongation).

1540s, re-Latinized from sinthoma (late 14c.), from Medieval Latin sinthoma "symptom of a disease," altered from Late Latin symptoma, from Greek symptoma "a happening, accident, disease," from stem of sympiptein "to befall, happen; coincide, fall together," from assimilated form of syn- "together" (see syn-) + piptein "to fall," from PIE *pi-pt-, reduplicated form of root *pet- "to rush; to fly" (see petition (n.)).
"wear off by rubbing," c.
Old English þærbig "thus, by means of or because of that;" see there + by.

meanings.definitionCn: 支配， 掌权， 行使

etymology.story: Old English weldan (Mercian), wieldan, wealdan (West Saxon) "have power over, compel, tame, subdue" (class VII strong verb; past tense weold, past participle gewealden), merged with weak verb wyldan, both from Proto-Germanic *waldan "to rule" (source also of Old Saxon and Gothic waldan, Old Frisian walda "to govern, rule," Old Norse valda "to rule, wield, to cause," Old High German waltan, German walten "to rule, govern").
1580s, "ill-starred," from French désastreux (16c.), from désastre (see disaster) or from Italian desastroso.

meanings.definitionCn: 冲动， 一时的念头； 刺激， 推动力； 脉冲

etymology.story: early 15c., "an act of impelling, a thrust, push," from Latin impulsus "a push against, pressure, shock," figuratively "incitement, instigation," past participle of impellere "to strike against, push against" (see impel).
1590s, from crystal + -ize.
1690s, from sub- + species..

meanings.definitionCn: 淋浴； 下阵雨； 洒落， 纷纷降落； 抛撒
阵雨，暴雨；淋浴；一阵，一大批
传染性的， 传染病的； 有感染力的

morphology.mnemonic: in + 做/制造 + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: "catching, having the quality of spreading from person to person, communicable by infection," 1540s of diseases, 1610s of emotions, actions, etc.; see infection + -ous.

meanings.definitionCn: 同时发生； 意见一致

etymology.story: early 15c., "collide, clash in hostility," from Latin concurrere "to run together, assemble hurriedly; clash, fight," in transferred use, "to happen at the same time," from com- "together" (see com-) + currere "to run" (see current (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 卓著的， 杰出的； 突起的， 凸出的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "projecting, jutting out," from Latin prominentem (nominative prominens) "prominent," present participle of prominere "jut or stand out, be prominent, overhang," from pro- "before, forward" (see pro-) + -minere "project, jut out," which is related to mons "hill" (see mount (n.1)).
1922 (earlier insuline, 1914), coined in English from Latin insula "island" (see isle and compare insula); so called because the hormone is secreted by the islets of Langerhans in the pancreas.

meanings.definitionCn: 说反话的， 讽刺的； 出乎意料的

etymology.story: 1620s, "pertaining to irony," from Late Latin ironicus, from Greek eironikos "dissembling, putting on a feigned ignorance," from eironeia (see irony).

morphology.mnemonic: 基础 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: mid-15c., "primary, original, pertaining to a foundation," modeled on Late Latin fundamentalis "of the foundation," from Latin fundamentum "foundation" (see fundament).

"pertaining to sacred things," 1660s, from Latin hieraticus, from Greek hieratikos "pertaining to a priest or his office, priestly, devoted to sacred purposes," from hierateia "priesthood," from hiereus "priest," from hieros "sacred, holy, hallowed; superhuman, mighty; divine" (see ire).

also intitle, late 14c., "to give a title to a chapter, book, etc.," from Anglo-French entitler, Old French entiteler "entitle, call" (Modern French intituler), from Late Latin intitulare "give a title or name to," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + titulus "title" (see title (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 单一 + 音 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1706, originally in transferred sense of "wearisome, tiresome," from French monotonie (1670s), from Greek monotonia "sameness of tone, monotony," from monotonos "monotonous, of one tone," from monos "single, alone" (see mono-) + tonos "tone" (see tenet).

meanings.definitionCn: 轮廓； 概要， 大纲

morphology.mnemonic: 超过/外部 + one's occupation, branch of business

meanings.definitionCn: 褪色； 凋谢； 逐渐消失
过期未付的， 逾期的； 过度的， 过火的； 迟到的， 延误的

etymology.story: "past the due date," 1845 of bills, 1890 of library books, 1970 of menstrual periods, from over- + due (adj.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 名义上的； 微不足道的

morphology.mnemonic: name名 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., "pertaining to nouns," from Latin nominalis "pertaining to a name or names," from nomen (genitive nominis) "name," cognate with Old English nama (see name (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 走/让步

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French exces (14c.) "excess, extravagance, outrage," from Latin excessus "departure, a going beyond the bounds of reason or beyond the subject," from stem of excedere "to depart, go beyond" (see exceed).

meanings.definitionCn: 展望； 观点， 看法； 远景； 透视图， 透视法； 前途

morphology.mnemonic: 通过/彻底 + 看/观察 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: late 14c., "science of optics," from Old French perspective and directly from Medieval Latin perspectiva ars "science of optics," from fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 星系； [G-]银河系， 银河； 群英

etymology.story: late 14c., from French galaxie or directly from Late Latin galaxias "the Milky Way" as a feature in the night sky (in classical Latin via lactea or circulus lacteus)from Greek galaxias (adj.), in galaxias kyklos, literally "milky circle," from gala (genitive galaktos) "milk" (see lacto-).

meanings.definitionCn: 侵入， 侵略； 侵扰

etymology.story: "enter in a hostile manner," late 15c., from Latin invadere "to go, come, or get into; enter violently, penetrate into as an enemy, assail, assault, make an attack on," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + vadere "go, walk" (see vamoose).

meanings.definitionCn: 介于中间的， 发生于期间的
聚集， 成群； 以沉重的脚步行走

etymology.story: 1580s, "lump; cluster of trees," from Middle English clompe "a lump" (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 彻底的； 详尽的； 一丝不苟的
民主政体， 民主制度； 民主国家； 民主精神

etymology.story: 1570s, from Middle French démocratie (14c.), from Medieval Latin democratia (13c.), from Greek demokratia "popular government," from demos "common people," originally "district" (see demotic), + kratos "rule, strength" (see -cracy)..
1530s, from Latin pæninsula "a peninsula," literally "almost an island," from pæne "almost" + insula "island" (see isle).

1640s, "to unfold, open out, expand," from Latin evolvere "to unroll, roll out, roll forth, unfold," especially of books; figuratively "to make clear, disclose; to produce, develop," from assimilated form of ex- "out" (see ex-) + volvere "to roll" (see volvox).

mid-15c., from Latin congratulationem (nominative congratulatio), noun of action from past participle stem of congratulari "wish joy," from com- "together, with" (see com-) + gratulari "give thanks, show joy," from gratus "agreeable" (see grace (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 停止； 放弃； 离开； 辞
家庭的，家用的；家喻户晓的

etymology.story: late 14c., "members of a family collectively (including servants)," also "furniture and articles belonging to a house;" see house (n.) + hold (n.1).

meanings.definitionCn: 负面的； 不利的； 逆的

etymology.story: late 14c., "contrary, opposing," from Old French avers (13c., Modern French adverse) "antagonistic, unfriendly, contrary, foreign" (as in gent avers "infidel race"), from Latin adversus "turned against, turned toward, fronting, facing," figuratively "hostile, adverse, unfavorable," past participle of advertere, from ad- "to" (see ad-) + vertere "to turn" (see versus).

meanings.definitionCn: 新闻简报； 公告， 布告； 简报

etymology.story: 1765, from French bulletin (16c.), modeled on Italian bulletino, diminutive of bulletta "document, voting slip," itself a diminutive of Latin bulla (see bull (n.2)) with equivalent of Old French -elet (see -let).

1630s, "reason, rationale," from Latin ratio "reckoning, numbering, calculation; business affair, procedure," also "reason, reasoning, judgment, understanding," from rat-, past participle stem of reri "to reckon, calculate," also "think" (see reason (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 交叉点， 汇合处； 连接， 接合

morphology.mnemonic: 连接 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1711, "act of joining," from Latin iunctionem (nominative iunctio) "a joining, uniting," noun of action from past participle stem of iungere "to join together" (see jugular).
1834; the name was introduced by Faraday on the suggestion of the Rev.

meanings.definitionCn: 本能， 直觉； 生性， 天性

etymology.story: early 15c., "a prompting" (a sense now obsolete), from Old French instinct (14c.) or directly from Latin instinctus "instigation, impulse, inspiration," noun use of past participle of instinguere "to incite, impel," from in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + stinguere "prick, goad," from PIE *steig- "to prick, stick, pierce" (see stick (v.)).

"connoisseur in eating and drinking," 1820, from French gourmet, altered (by influence of Middle French gourmant "glutton") from Old French groume, originally "wine-taster, wine merchant's servant" (in 13c.

meanings.definitionCn: 名声， 声望， 名誉

morphology.mnemonic: 认为/名声 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-14c., "credit, good reputation," from Latin reputationem (nominative reputatio) "consideration, a thinking over," noun of action from past participle stem of reputare "reflect upon, reckon, count over," from re- "repeatedly" (see re-) + putare "to reckon, consider" (see putative)..

meanings.definitionCn: 长钉， 大钉； 猛增， 急升

etymology.story: Old English scrybb "brushwood, shrubbery," a rare and late word (but preserved also, perhaps, in Shrewsbury), possibly from a Scandinavian source (compare dialectal Danish skrub "brushwood," Norwegian skrubba "dwarf tree").

meanings.definitionCn: 提供， 显示； 介绍， 陈述； 赠送； 表演

etymology.story: late 14c., "act of presenting," from Old French presentacion (13c.), from Latin praesentationem (nominative praesentatio) "a placing before," noun of action from  past participle stem of praesentare (see present (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 起源/种类 + 过程/状态

etymology.story: Old English Genesis, first book of the Pentateuch, which tells among other things of the creation of the world, from Latin genesis "generation, nativity," in Late Latin taken as the title of first book of the Old Testament, from Greek genesis "origin, creation, generation," from gignesthai "to be born," related to genos "race, birth, descent" (see genus).

meanings.definitionCn: 下意识的， 潜意识的

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之下 + limin + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1873, "below the threshold" (of consciousness or sensation), formed from Latin stem of sublime (Latin limen, genitive liminis) + -al (1)).

morphology.mnemonic: 使成为/放入 + 重/严重

meanings.definitionCn: 小旅馆， 客栈； 小酒店

etymology.story: late 13c., "wine shop," later "public house" (mid-15c.), from Old French taverne (mid-13c.) "shed made of boards, booth, stall," also "tavern, inn," from Latin taberna "shop, inn, tavern," originally "hut, shed, rude dwelling," possibly [Klein] by dissimilation from *traberna, from trabs (genitive trabis) "beam, timber," from PIE *treb- "dwelling" (source also of Lithuanian troba "a building," Old Welsh treb "house, dwelling," Welsh tref "a dwelling," Irish treb "residence," Old English ðorp "village, hamlet, farm, estate").

meanings.definitionCn: 闪烁的微光；隐约的迹象；一丝，一线
别扭的； 笨拙的； 难处理的， 棘手的； 难操纵的

etymology.story: mid-14c., "in the wrong direction," from awk "back-handed" + adverbial suffix -weard (see -ward).

morphology.mnemonic: something that reminds, + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1845, originally "a throw or fall from a horse," from spill (v.).

mid-15c., originally of the Mass, from Latin celebratus "much-frequented; kept solemn; famous," past participle of celebrare "assemble to honor," also "to publish; sing praises of; practice often," originally "to frequent in great numbers," from celeber "frequented, populous, crowded;" with transferred senses of "well-attended; famous; often-repeated."
mid-13c., "to end at, to border on," from Old French aboter "join end to end, touch upon" (13c.), from à "to" (see ad-) + bout "end" (see butt (n.3)).

root.meaning: to set or place in position

morphology.mnemonic: to set or place in position + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French montagneux, from Vulgar Latin *montaneosus "mountainous," from *montanea (see mountain)..

morphology.mnemonic: 劳动/工作 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1620s, "pertaining to geometry," shortened form of geometrical (q.v.).
1620s, from Latin inspectus, past participle of inspicere "look at, observe, view; look into, inspect, examine," from in- "into" (see in- (2)) + specere "to look" (see scope (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 竞选活动； 活动； 战役
生死攸关的； 至关重要的； 精力充沛的， 有活力的

etymology.story: late 14c., "of or manifesting life," from Latin vitalis "of or belonging to life," from vita "life," related to vivere "to live," from PIE root *gweie- (1) "to live" (see bio-).

meanings.definitionCn: 冲突； 不一致； 争论

etymology.story: early 15c., "armed encounter, battle," from Old French conflit and directly from Latin conflictus (see conflict (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 雕刻术， 刻版术； 版画

morphology.mnemonic: 使成为/放入 + 重/严重

meanings.definitionCn: 粗糙不平的；粗暴的；艰难的

etymology.story: 1620s, from law (n.) + suit (n.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 序列的； 连续的； 随之而来的

morphology.mnemonic: 跟随 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1816, from Late Latin sequentia (see sequence) + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 发现， 察觉； 探测； 查明

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin detectus, past participle of detegere "uncover, expose," figuratively "discover, reveal, disclose," from de- "un-, off" (see de-) + tegere "to cover" (see stegosaurus).

meanings.definitionCn: 派生的，引出的；无创意的
派生词， 衍生词； 派生物

morphology.mnemonic: der + 使…/具有…的 + 有…倾向的

meanings.definitionCn: 业余爱好的； 业余的

etymology.story: 1784, "one who has a taste for (something)," from French amateur "lover of," from Latin amatorem (nominative amator) "lover," agent noun from amatus, past participle of amare "to love" (see Amy).

1914 as public entertainment show of horse-riding skill, from earlier meaning "cattle round-up" (1834), from Spanish rodeo, "pen for cattle at a fair or market," literally "a going round," from rodear "go round, surround," related to rodare "revolve, roll," from Latin rotare "go around" (see rotary)..
early 15c., "act of helping or aiding," from Middle French assistance, from assister (see assist (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 贸易， 商业； 交往， 交流

etymology.story: 1530s, from Middle French commerce (14c.), from Latin commercium "trade, trafficking," from com- "together" (see com-) + merx (genitive mercis) "merchandise" (see market (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 游说， 拉选票； 细查
海湾； 分歧， 隔阂， 鸿沟

etymology.story: late 14c., "profound depth," from Old French golf "a gulf, whirlpool," from Italian golfo "a gulf, a bay," from Late Latin colfos, from Greek kolpos "bay, gulf of the sea," earlier "trough between waves, fold of a loose garment," originally "bosom," the common notion being "curved shape." This is from PIE *kwelp- "to arch, to vault" (compare Old English hwealf, a-hwielfan "to overwhelm").

meanings.definitionCn: 原因的， 因果关系的

etymology.story: 1530s, from Latin causalis "relating to a cause," from causa (see cause (n.))..
1899 in the geological sense, from tectonic (also see -ics); earlier it meant "building or constructive arts in general" (1850)..

meanings.definitionCn: 惊奇， 惊异； 奇迹， 奇事

etymology.story: Old English wundrian "be astonished," also "admire; make wonderful, magnify," from the source of wonder (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 公寓， 府邸； 大厦

etymology.story: mid-14c., "chief residence of a lord," from Old French mansion "stay, permanent abode, house, habitation, home; mansion; state, situation" (13c.), from Latin mansionem (nominative mansio) "a staying, a remaining, night quarters, station," noun of action from past participle stem of manere "to stay, abide," from PIE *men- "to remain, wait for" (source also of Greek menein "to remain," Persian mandan "to remain").

meanings.definitionCn: 概括的， 概要的； 即刻的， 立即的

morphology.mnemonic: 拿/消耗 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: "a summary statement or account," c.

morphology.mnemonic: not well, poorly + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1590s, from French illusorie, from Late Latin illusorius "ironical, of a mocking character," from illus-, past participle stem of Latin illudere "mock, jeer at, make fun of," literally "play with," from assimilated form of in- "at, upon" (see in- (2)) + ludere "to play" (see ludicrous)..

whitish metal element, 1755, coined in 1754 by Swedish mineralogist Axel von Cronstedt (1722-1765) from shortening of Swedish kopparnickel "copper-colored ore" (from which it was first obtained), a half-translation of German Kupfernickel, literally "copper demon," from Kupfer (see copper) + Nickel "demon, goblin, rascal" (a pet form of masc.

meanings.definitionCn: 牵涉； 使必要， 需要

etymology.story: mid-14c., "convert (an estate) into 'fee tail' (feudum talliatum)," from en- (1) "make" + taile "legal limitation," especially of inheritance, ruling who succeeds in ownership and preventing the property from being sold off, from Anglo-French taile, Old French taillie, past participle of taillier "allot, cut to shape," from Late Latin taliare "to split" (see tailor (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 劣等的，较差的；次要的；级别低的
下级， 下属， 晚辈
戏剧的； 引人注目的； 突然的， 巨大的； 戏剧性的

etymology.story: 1580s, from Late Latin dramaticus, from Greek dramatikos "pertaining to plays," from drama (genitive dramatos; see drama).

1660s, spinette, "small harpsichord," from Middle French espinette (16c., Modern French épinette), from Italian spinetta, said by Scaliger to be a diminutive of spina "thorn, spine," from Latin spina "thorn" (see spine), so called because the strings were plucked with thorn-like quills [Barnhart].

1580s, from Spanish mosquito "little gnat," diminutive of mosca "fly," from Latin musca "fly," from PIE root *mu- "gnat, fly," imitative of insect buzzing (compare Sanskrit maksa-, Greek myia, Old English mycg, Modern English midge, Old Church Slavonic mucha), perhaps imitative of the sound of humming insects..

1530s, "a hanger-on, a toady, person who lives on others," from Middle French parasite (16c.) or directly from Latin parasitus "toady, sponger," and directly from Greek parasitos "one who lives at another's expense, person who eats at the table of another," from noun use of an adjective meaning "feeding beside," from para- "beside" (see para- (1)) + sitos "food," of unknown origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 人工的， 人造的， 假的； 矫揉造作的， 假装

morphology.mnemonic: device, trick + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., in the phrase artificial day "part of the day from sunrise to sunset," from Old French artificial, from Latin artificialis "of or belonging to art," from artificium (see artifice).
1630s, from deficience (mid-15c.) + -cy; or from Late Latin deficientia, from deficientem (see deficient)..

meanings.definitionCn: 想象中的， 虚构的， 假想的

etymology.story: "not real, existing only in fancy," late 14c., ymaginaire, from imagine + -ary; or else from Late Latin imaginarius "seeming, fancied," also literal, "pertaining to an image," from Latin imaginari "picture to oneself." Imaginary friend (one who does not exist) attested by 1789..

meanings.definitionCn: 除去； 移动， 搬迁

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + act of moving + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1590s; see remove (v.) + -al (2)..
early 15c., from Middle French commenter (15c.), from Latin commentari, from commentum (see comment (n.)).
1630s, "to smoke" (tobacco), from vapor + -ize.

mid-14c., "escape inclusion in; lie beyond the scope of," from Old French transcendre "transcend, surpass," and directly from Latin transcendere "climb over or beyond, surmount, overstep," from trans- "beyond" (see trans-) + scandere "to climb" (see scan (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 长满刺的， 多刺的， 带刺的； 棘手的

morphology.mnemonic: the back of a book + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1580s, from spine + -y (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 按年代顺序排列的， 年代学的

morphology.mnemonic: 时间 + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: "arranged in order by time," 1610s, from chronology + -ical.

meanings.definitionCn: 人道主义者， 博爱者， 慈善家

etymology.story: 1794 in the theological sense "one who affirms the humanity of Christ but denies his pre-existence and divinity," from human (adj.) + suffix from unitarian, etc.
1660s, "action of employing," from French emploi, from Middle French verb employer (see employ (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 额外费用，增收费，附加费

etymology.story: early 15c., "overcharge, charge too much expense," from Old French surcharger "to overload, overburden, overcharge" (12c.), from sur- "over" (see sur- (1)) + chargier "to load" (see charge (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 健壮的， 结实的； 稳固的； 顽强的
阳台； 楼厅， 楼座

etymology.story: 1610s, from Italian balcone, from balco "scaffold," from a Germanic source (perhaps Langobardic *balko- "beam," cognate with Old English balca "beam, ridge;" see balk (n.)) + Italian augmentative suffix -one.

meanings.definitionCn: 慢慢灌输； 逐渐培养

etymology.story: also instil, early 15c., "to introduce (liquid, feelings, etc.) little by little," from Latin instillare "put in by drops; to drop, trickle," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + stilla "a drop" (see distill).

meanings.definitionCn: 在于， 存在于； 由…组成， 由…构成

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 站立/设置

etymology.story: 1520s, from Middle French consister (14c.) or directly from Latin consistere "to stand firm, take a standing position, stop, halt," from com- "together" (see com-) + sistere "to place," causative of stare "to stand, be standing" (see stay (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 美味的， 可口的； 合意的， 认可的

etymology.story: 1660s, "good-tasting," from palate + -able.

meanings.definitionCn: 协调的， 一致的； 兼容的； 能和睦相处的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Middle French compatible (15c.), from Medieval Latin compatibilis, from Late Latin compati (see compassion).

meanings.definitionCn: 评论家， 批评家； 吹毛求疵者

etymology.story: 1580s, "one who passes judgment," from Middle French critique (14c.), from Latin criticus "a judge, literary critic," from Greek kritikos "able to make judgments," from krinein "to separate, decide" (see crisis).
1898, loan-translation of German Photosynthese, from photo- "light" (see photo-) + synthese "synthesis" (see synthesis).
"secondary article accompanying a larger one in a newspaper," 1948, from side (adj.) + bar (n.1)..
late 14c., "provide with a crest," from Old French crester, from creste (see crest (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 职业； 同行； 专业； 宣称

morphology.mnemonic: declare openly + 状态/行为/过程

meanings.definitionCn: 悲叹， 哀叹， 公开谴责

etymology.story: 1550s, "to give up as hopeless," from French déplorer (13c.), from Latin deplorare "deplore, bewail, lament, give up for lost," from de- "entirely" (see de-) + plorare "weep, cry out," which is of unknown origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 压缩； 缩短； 浓缩

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + device for pressing cloth

meanings.definitionCn: 冷酷地； 严肃地； 坚定地

etymology.story: Old English grimlice; see grim (adj.) + -ly (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 爱护， 珍爱； 怀有， 抱有

etymology.story: early 14c., cherischen, from Old French cheriss-, present participle stem of chierir "to hold dear" (12c., Modern French chérir), from chier "dear," from Latin carus "dear, costly, beloved" (see whore).

meanings.definitionCn: 以高人一等的态度对待； 光顾； 赞助， 资助

morphology.mnemonic: one who advances the cause + ize

etymology.story: 1580s, "to act as a patron towards," from patron + -ize, or from Old French patroniser.

early 15c., from Old French éminent "prominent" (13c.) or directly from Latin eminentem (nominative eminens) "standing out, projecting, prominent, high," figuratively "distinguished, distinctive," present participle of eminere "stand out, project; be prominent, be conspicuous," from assimilated form of ex- "out" (see ex-) + -minere, which is related to mons "hill" (see mount (n.1)).
